title: xt update: verify and refresh global xt Pi packages
description:
PROBLEM: xt update currently refreshes registry-managed project files but does not check whether xt-shipped Pi packages are present and updated in global/user Pi scope. User policy: running xt update must also assure Pi packages.
SUCCESS: xt update dry-run reports missing/outdated global xt Pi packages; xt update --apply installs or refreshes them using the same canonical Pi package registry as init/reload.
SCOPE: cli/src/commands/update.ts, shared Pi package inventory/assurance helpers in cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts, update tests.
NON_GOALS: No registry-file drift redesign beyond invoking/checking Pi package assurance; no project-scoped package migration in this bead.
CONSTRAINTS: Preserve multi-repo --root behavior; avoid reinstalling packages already current; inject command runners for tests.
VALIDATION: Tests cover dry-run missing/outdated package reporting and --apply invoking package install/update for exactly the stale packages.

title: Registry: stop copying packages/pi-extensions into project .xtrm
description:
PROBLEM: `.xtrm/registry.json` currently includes an `extensions` asset sourced from `packages/pi-extensions`, causing xt install/init/update to copy shipped Pi extension package contents into project `.xtrm/packages/pi-extensions`. Under the global-only policy, this package payload is not repo-specific and should not be scaffolded per repo.
SUCCESS: Generated registry no longer contains the `packages/pi-extensions` asset for project scaffold. Existing project copies are either ignored as legacy or flagged by a separate doctor/check bead.
SCOPE: `scripts/gen-registry.mjs`, `.xtrm/registry.json`, focused registry/pack parity tests.
NON_GOALS: Do not remove `packages/pi-extensions` from npm package files; do not change global Pi package publication/install behavior.
CONSTRAINTS: Keep hooks/skills/config project assets intact.
VALIDATION: `npm run gen-registry` omits the extensions asset; install/update tests do not expect `.xtrm/packages/pi-extensions` in target projects.

title: Pi runtime: assure every xt-shipped Pi package in global scope
description:
PROBLEM: Xt-shipped Pi packages/extensions are not consistently enforced as global-only. Some package entries can still be project-scoped, and only a subset is force-ensured globally. User policy: every Pi package xt ships must be installed in user/global Pi scope, not per project.
SUCCESS: Fresh `xt init` and `xt pi reload` ensure every package from the canonical xt Pi package registry is present in global/user Pi packages, including `npm:@jaggerxtrm/pi-extensions`, `npm:pi-serena-tools`, `npm:pi-gitnexus`, `npm:@zenobius/pi-worktrees`, `npm:@robhowley/pi-structured-return`, `npm:@aliou/pi-guardrails`, and `npm:@aliou/pi-processes`.
SCOPE: `cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts` global package assurance logic and tests.
NON_GOALS: Do not implement outdated-version checks here; do not change package membership decisions; do not alter MCP/skills/hooks.
CONSTRAINTS: Do not delete unrelated user packages. Existing project-scoped xt package leftovers may be migrated by a separate bead.
VALIDATION: Test proves all canonical xt Pi packages are checked/installed against global/user Pi package state, not project `.pi/settings.json`.
notes: Implementation complete: ensureAlwaysGlobalPiPackages now checks every package returned by getXtManagedPiPackages against the global Pi agent npm tree. runPiRuntimeSync global mode no longer returns before global package assurance; it syncs extensions if needed and then always ensures global packages. Tests updated in cli/test/pi-runtime.test.ts and cli/src/tests/pi-runtime-safeguards.test.ts to prove all canonical packages are installed or skipped from global agent dirs and project Pi settings are not treated as proof. Validation: focused update/pi-runtime tests passed 25/25. Typecheck still fails on known xtrm-d2mr baseline. Code-sanity 73a461 found stale two-package test coverage; fixed by updating cli/test/pi-runtime.test.ts.
close_reason: Done: global Pi package assurance now covers every canonical xt-managed Pi package, including @jaggerxtrm/pi-extensions; focused tests pass; typecheck remains blocked by known xtrm-d2mr baseline.

title: xt pi setup --check: stop reading missing config/pi/extensions
description:
PROBLEM: `cli/src/commands/install-pi.ts` `--check` and setup sync paths still build a source path at `config/pi/extensions`, but that directory no longer exists; managed Pi extension sources are resolved by `pi-runtime.ts`.
SUCCESS: `xt pi setup --check` uses `resolveManagedPiExtensionsSourceDir()` / current Pi runtime inventory or delegates to `runPiRuntimeSync` dry-run logic, and no code path requires `config/pi/extensions`.
SCOPE: `cli/src/commands/install-pi.ts`, stale assertions in `cli/test/install-pi.test.ts`.
NON_GOALS: No API key prompt rewrite, no global-only package policy change.
CONSTRAINTS: Keep `xt pi setup` first-time auth/config writing behavior unchanged.
VALIDATION: Test proves `--check` works in a repo without `config/pi/extensions` and reports via the current runtime source resolver.

title: Pi packages: add parity test for managed package lists
description:
PROBLEM: Pi package names are declared in several places and have drifted. Before refactoring behavior, add a narrow parity test that exposes exact divergence between runtime-managed packages, `package.json` `pi.packages`, and Pi setup templates.
SUCCESS: A failing/passing test enumerates package-list drift and documents allowed exceptions explicitly.
SCOPE: test-only unless a tiny exported helper is required: `cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts`, `package.json`, `.xtrm/config/pi/install-schema.json`, `.xtrm/config/pi/settings.json.template`, related test file.
NON_GOALS: Do not change install behavior in this bead; do not decide global-vs-project package scope here.
CONSTRAINTS: If exceptions remain, encode them as named constants with comments, not implicit omissions.
VALIDATION: Test fails if a managed Pi package is added to one canonical surface but omitted from the others without an explicit exception.

title: xt update: compare installed files against current package registry
description:
PROBLEM: `xt update` decides whether to run `runInstall()` by checking files against the target repo's installed `.xtrm/registry.json`. A repo that is clean relative to an old registry can report `already-current` even when the current npm package has newer registry hashes/files.
SUCCESS: `xt update` dry-run reports pending changes when current package registry differs from the target install; `xt update --apply` refreshes those files via `runInstall()`.
SCOPE: `cli/src/commands/update.ts`, a small helper if needed to resolve current package `.xtrm/registry.json`, `cli/src/tests/update.test.ts`.
NON_GOALS: No new doctor UI, no Pi scope policy changes, no registry format migration.
CONSTRAINTS: Preserve `--repo`, `--root`, `--json`; preserve local drift rather than silently classifying it as package-outdated.
VALIDATION: Add a fixture where target files match an old target registry but differ from the current package registry; dry-run must not say `already-current`, and `--apply` must call `runInstall()` once.

title: Audit xt init/update install and drift scope
description: PROBLEM: Need a current repository audit of xt init / xt update behavior, including which packages/dependencies are installed globally, which assets/packages are installed per repo, what initialization/update phases run, and what drift detection/repair exists or is missing. SUCCESS: Produce an evidence-backed audit map of command flow, install scopes, dependency inventory, drift detection mechanisms, known gaps/risks, and recommended follow-up beads. SCOPE: CLI command surfaces for xt init/update/install, machine/bootstrap dependency installers, Pi runtime sync, registry/scaffold/update paths, docs/memories relevant to install/update drift, tests that assert install/update behavior. NON_GOALS: No source edits, no package installs, no destructive cleanup, no executing init/update against real projects. CONSTRAINTS: READ_ONLY; cite files/symbols/commands; distinguish global machine-level installs from per-repo managed files and per-project runtime settings; include drift detection vs drift repair separately. VALIDATION: Findings cite concrete files/symbols and, where useful, non-mutating commands such as package metadata inspection or test names. OUTPUT: Audit report with global installs, per-repo installs, drift detection matrix, risk/gap list, and proposed follow-up issues.
close_reason: Job 782cd2 stopped (cancelled)

title: service-registry resolver doesn't probe canonical xtrm pack layout
description:
PROBLEM: skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py:get_registry_path probes only two locations: <root>/service-registry.json (preferred) and <root>/.claude/skills/service-registry.json (legacy). xtrm-tools itself ships service-registry.json under .xtrm/skills/user/packs/<pack>/service-registry.json (e.g. mercury pack on darth-feedor). Result: drift_detector.py scan returns 'No drift detected' WITHOUT erroring on any project that uses an xtrm pack — silent false-clean state. Confirmed live on darth-feedor 2026-05-07; required a manual symlink (ln -s .xtrm/skills/user/packs/mercury/service-registry.json service-registry.json) to make the detector functional. Same trap will hit any project that consumes an xtrm pack of service skills.

SUCCESS:
- get_registry_path additionally probes <root>/.xtrm/skills/user/packs/*/service-registry.json (sorted glob; first hit wins; if multiple exist, prefer one matching the project's active pack manifest if discoverable, else lexicographically first with a warning).
- Resolution order documented in module docstring updated.
- When NO registry is found, scan/sync subcommands print a one-line stderr hint pointing at expected paths (instead of silently returning empty).

SCOPE:
- skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py — get_registry_path + module docstring.
- skills/updating-service-skills/scripts/drift_detector.py — scan/sync error path, only if it currently swallows the no-registry case.
- Mirror updates under .xtrm/skills/default/{creating,updating}-service-skills/ if those exist.

NON_GOALS:
- No new env var beyond existing SERVICE_REGISTRY_PATH override.
- No automatic registry creation.
- No symlink-management on consumer projects.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Backward-compatible: if root/legacy registry already exists, behavior unchanged.
- Pure stdlib (Path.glob).
- No I/O during import; resolution stays lazy.

VALIDATION:
- New unit test fixtures: project with xtrm pack-only registry; project with both root and xtrm pack (root wins); project with no registry (clear stderr).
- Live: drop into a fresh clone of darth-feedor (after removing the workaround symlink), run drift_detector.py scan — should find drift across the territories without manual setup.

REFERENCES:
- darth-feedor commit c7bdfb5 (workaround symlink) and bead py_backend-p5r.
- darth-feedor memory 'service-skills-drift-detector-silent-fail-mode-drift'.

title: Release: deprecate bad xtrm-tools 2.x stream after scoped migration
description:
AUDIT FINDING: npm latest points to xtrm-tools@0.7.17 but historical 2.x versions exist through at least 2.4.6; current package self-dependency ^2.0.2 can pull bad stream. Need safe deprecation/migration.

SUCCESS: Users are guided away from bad 2.x and toward canonical scoped package or fixed shim.

ACCEPTANCE:

- Publish fixed scoped package and/or xtrm-tools shim first.
- Run fresh-machine smoke.
- npm deprecate xtrm-tools@2.x with clear message.
- Ensure latest dist-tag remains safe.
- Docs mention uninstall old global package if both scoped/unscoped bins conflict.

title: Package: remove self-dependency and audit runtime dependencies
description:
AUDIT FINDING: xtrm-tools@0.7.17 package.json contains dependency "xtrm-tools": "^2.0.2" inside xtrm-tools itself. This can pull nested package versions, bloat install, confuse resolution, or mask missing dependencies.

SUCCESS: package dependencies reflect actual runtime needs with no self-dependency.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Remove or justify self-dependency.
- Verify cli/dist/index.cjs external requires are satisfied by root dependencies or bundled.
- npm pack/install smoke runs with npm ls clean enough to catch missing deps.
- Add CI assertion that package does not depend on itself.

title: Runtime: strict registry/source mismatch mode for xt install/update
description:
AUDIT FINDING: installFromRegistry logs missing package source files and continues. That is user-friendly but lets CI/fresh-machine smoke pass with incomplete Category B payload.

SUCCESS: CI and release smoke can force xt install/update to fail hard on registry/source mismatch.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Add xt install/update strict mode or env var (e.g. XTRM_STRICT_REGISTRY=1).
- Strict mode exits nonzero when missingSourceSkipped > 0 or registry/source mismatch occurs.
- Error lists missing sources.
- Fresh-machine smoke uses strict mode.
- Fixture deleting one registry file proves strict failure.

title: CI: run release gates explicitly in npm publish workflow
description:
AUDIT FINDING: publish workflow runs npm ci/build/publish but does not explicitly run vendor, registry, pack parity, payload hygiene, or fresh-machine smoke gates. It may rely on lifecycle behavior without specialists source configured.

SUCCESS: npm publish is impossible unless the same release-contract gates pass in the publish workflow.

ACCEPTANCE:
- .github/workflows/publish.yml runs mirror/registry drift gate, registry↔pack parity, npm pack payload assertions, payload denylist, and fresh-machine smoke before npm publish.
- Workflow provides specialists source via tarball/SHA/checkout or fails clearly.
- Logs/artifacts include specialists source ref and gate outputs.
- Publish blocked on any gate failure.

title: CI: enforce registry-to-npm-pack parity for managed assets
description:
AUDIT FINDING: xtrm audits found .xtrm/registry.json has 408 files, with 7 registry files missing from npm pack and 205 packed managed files absent from registry. Required-path checks alone are insufficient.

SUCCESS: npm package and registry are mutually consistent for managed assets.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Run npm pack --dry-run --json.
- For every file in .xtrm/registry.json, assert corresponding path is in pack unless explicitly allowlisted with reason.
- For every packed file under managed roots (.xtrm/config, .xtrm/hooks, .xtrm/skills/default, .xtrm/skills/optional, packages/pi-extensions), assert registry contains it or allowlist explains why.
- Failure prints missing/extra path lists.
- Current known gaps (documenting tests, using-service-skills test, pi-extensions .serena .gitignore) are resolved or allowlisted intentionally.

title: Audit package: xtrm-tools npm payload contents and exclusions
description:
ROLE: READ_ONLY overthinker focused ONLY on xtrm-tools npm package contents. Do not edit files.

MISSION: Audit exactly what xtrm-tools packages/releases to npm, and decide whether package contents are sufficient and minimal for runtime/install/update.

SCOPE:
- package.json: name/version/bin/files/scripts/dependencies/devDependencies.
- npm pack --dry-run --json output.
- .xtrm/registry.json and .xtrm/skills/default/.xtrm/hooks/default payload.
- dist/src/bin assets required by xt install/update/doctor.
- docs/README/CHANGELOG/license that should or should not be included.
- Exclude/flag dev-specific or local-machine files: .wolf, .beads, reports/scratch, local logs/dbs, absolute /home/dawid paths, repo-only worktrees, symlinks to specialists.
- Confirm runtime essentials are included: registry, default skills including using-specialists-v3 and update-specialists, hooks, templates, package metadata, executables.

OUTPUT REQUIRED:
1. Current npm package payload summary with suspicious inclusions/exclusions.
2. Missing runtime asset risks.
3. Unnecessary/dev asset risks.
4. Proposed package.json files changes, if any.
5. Proposed CI npm-pack assertion list.
6. Whether npm install alone is sufficient without repo clone/symlink.

## Summary

xtrm-tools npm payload sufficient for core runtime install/update. Not minimal. Several dev/eval/local artifacts ship.

## Status

Read-only audit complete. No code edits. `npm pack --dry-run --json` checked.

## Phase 1 - Initial Analysis

Current package:

- name/version: `xtrm-tools@0.7.17`
- bin:
  - `xtrm -> cli/dist/index.cjs`
  - `xt -> cli/dist/index.cjs`
  - `ghgrep -> scripts/ghgrep.mjs`
- pack:
  - 616 files
  - 2.0 MB tarball
  - 9.2 MB unpacked
- included essentials:
  - `cli/dist/index.cjs`
  - `cli/dist/index.cjs.map`
  - `.xtrm/registry.json`
  - `.xtrm/config/**`
  - `.xtrm/hooks/**`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/**`
  - `.xtrm/skills/optional/**`
  - `packages/pi-extensions/**`
  - `README.md`
  - `CHANGELOG.md`
- missing doc:
  - no root `LICENSE` file included; repo root appears to lack one. `license: MIT` exists in `package.json`.

Runtime essentials present:

- `using-specialists-v3`: included.
- `update-specialists`: included.
- hooks: included.
- templates/config: included.
- executable dist: included.
- package metadata: included.

Registry mismatch:

- `.xtrm/registry.json` has 408 registry files.
- Registry versions all `0.7.16`, while package is `0.7.17`.
- Registry references 7 files missing from packed payload:
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/documenting/tests/integration_test.sh`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/documenting/tests/test_changelog.py`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/documenting/tests/test_drift_detector.py`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/documenting/tests/test_orchestrator.py`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/documenting/tests/test_validate_metadata.py`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/using-service-skills/scripts/test_skill_activator.py`
  - `packages/pi-extensions/.serena/.gitignore`

Cause: `package.json.files` excludes tests, but registry still lists them.

## Phase 2 - Devil's Advocate

Sufficiency risk higher than first glance:

- `installFromRegistry()` logs missing registry source and continues. Missing tests may not break normal runtime, but registry/source mismatch weakens doctor/update trust.
- Registry version stale (`0.7.16`) may confuse drift/version reporting.
- `package.json` has dependency `"xtrm-tools": "^2.0.2"` inside `xtrm-tools@0.7.17`. This is suspicious and likely wrong. It can pull nested newer/older package, bloat install, confuse resolution, or mask missing deps.
- Root package dependencies differ from `cli/package.json`. If `cli/dist/index.cjs` leaves external requires, root package must provide them directly, not rely on workspace-only deps or transitive hoists.
- Payload includes `packages/pi-extensions/.serena/project.yml`; local editor/project metadata should not ship.
- Payload includes many eval/workspace artifacts:
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/*/evals/**`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/quality-gates/workspace/**`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/sync-docs-workspace/**`
  - benchmark/grading/timing/output files
- Packed content contains `/home/dawid` strings in 24 files, mostly eval/workspace outputs plus `CHANGELOG.md` and `vaultctl/SKILL.md`.
- Pack includes vendored `node_modules` under:
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/last30days/scripts/lib/vendor/bird-search/node_modules/**`
  This may be intentional vendoring, but must be explicit. Otherwise high bloat/supply-chain risk.

## Phase 3 - Synthesis

Package is operational but noisy.

Good:

- npm install has core package assets. No repo clone/symlink needed for baseline `xt/xtrm init/update/doctor`.
- No packed symlinks found.
- Worktree dirs under `.xtrm/worktrees` exist locally but are not packed.
- `.wolf`, `.beads`, broad reports/scratch not packed.

Bad:

- Package not minimal due eval/workspace/local metadata.
- Registry/package mismatch must be fixed or intentionally documented.
- Self-dependency must be removed unless there is rare deliberate bootstrap reason.
- License file absent.
- CI lacks pack-level guardrails.

## Changes

No changes made. Proposed `package.json.files` changes:

```json
[
  "LICENSE",
  "!packages/pi-extensions/.serena/**",
  "!.xtrm/skills/**/evals/**",
  "!.xtrm/skills/**/workspace/**",
  "!.xtrm/skills/**/sync-docs-workspace/**",
  "!.xtrm/skills/**/benchmark.*",
  "!.xtrm/skills/**/grading.json",
  "!.xtrm/skills/**/timing.json",
  "!**/*.combined.log",
  "!**/*.stdout.log",
  "!**/*.stderr.log",
  "!**/node_modules/**"
]
```

Only add `!**/node_modules/**` if `last30days` vendored dependency not required. If required, narrow allowlist and document why.

Proposed dependency change:

- Remove root dependency:
  - `"xtrm-tools": "^2.0.2"`
- Align root runtime deps with actual `cli/dist` externals, or fully bundle externals.

## Verification

Commands/evidence used:

- `gitnexus_query(...)`
- `gitnexus_context(resolvePackageRoot)`
- `npm pack --dry-run --json`
- registry vs pack comparison
- packed symlink scan
- `/home/dawid` content scan
- `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope=all)`

Result:

- GitNexus code changes: none.
- Beads metadata changed due mandated claim/close workflow.

## Risks

High:

- self-dependency `xtrm-tools@^2.0.2` in root package.

Medium:

- registry references files excluded from pack.
- stale registry asset versions.
- eval/workspace files with local absolute paths ship to npm.
- `.serena` project metadata ships.
- absent root license file.

Low:

- source map included; useful for debug, increases size.
- `CHANGELOG.md` includes local paths; acceptable only if intentional.

## Follow-ups

Recommended CI npm-pack assertions:

1. `npm pack --dry-run --json` parses clean.
2. Required paths exist:
   - `package.json`
   - `README.md`
   - `CHANGELOG.md`
   - `LICENSE`
   - `cli/dist/index.cjs`
   - `.xtrm/registry.json`
   - `.xtrm/config/hooks.json`
   - `.xtrm/config/pi/settings.json.template`

title: Implement broken-symlink repair in runInstall before installFromRegistry
description:
PROBLEM: runInstall (cli/src/commands/install.ts) calls installFromRegistry directly without first repairing .xtrm/skills/default if it's a broken symlink. Only init.ts calls scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage. Result: xt update fails on machines where the legacy dev symlink target is gone.

SUCCESS:
- runInstall calls scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage(packageRoot, userXtrmDir, dryRun) BEFORE installFromRegistry, so any broken symlink at <userXtrmDir>/skills/default is removed and replaced with a copy from packageRoot.
- Same path used by xt update (since update calls runInstall).
- Behavior preserved: valid symlinks (dev mode) untouched; real directories untouched; broken symlinks replaced with real copy from npm package root.

SCOPE:
- cli/src/commands/install.ts: insert scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage call inside runInstall, after registry load and target resolution, before installFromRegistry. Import from ../core/registry-scaffold.js.
- cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts: no behavior change required, but verify scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage handles all three cases (broken symlink, valid symlink, real dir, missing). Already does.
- cli/src/tests/registry-scaffold.test.ts: add coverage for the install-time broken-symlink-repair flow if missing.
- cli/test/doctor.test.ts or new cli/test/install.test.ts: end-to-end test that runInstall against a tmp project with a broken .xtrm/skills/default symlink succeeds and reinstates the directory.

NON_GOALS:
- Do not change init.ts.
- Do not change skills-materializer.ts (active/* relative symlinks are fine).
- Do not introduce new flags.
- Do not modify drift.ts.

CONSTRAINTS:
- packageRoot must come from resolvePackageRoot() (npm package). Never accept a repo-mode override here.
- Idempotent: safe to call repeatedly.
- dryRun must be honored (scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage already returns 'noop' in dryRun).
- Logging: emit a single dim line when the broken symlink is replaced; silent on noop.

VALIDATION:
- npm run lint
- npx tsc --noEmit
- npm test (cli tests)
- New test reproduces original failure pre-fix and passes post-fix.

OUTPUT: Diff summary across install.ts and tests; lint/tsc/test results; manual reproduction notes.

title: Fix installer leaving stale .xtrm/skills/default symlink to repo path
description:
PROBLEM: On machines where xtrm-tools was previously installed via a repo-mode symlink (e.g. .xtrm/skills/default -> /home/dawid/projects/xtrm-tools/.xtrm/skills/default), the symlink target may no longer exist (repo deleted, moved, or never present on this machine). xt update then fails because runInstall -> installFromRegistry copies into paths under the broken symlink without repairing it first. Only init calls scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage (cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts:83) which handles broken-symlink replacement. The npm package must always be the install source — never a sibling repo path.

SUCCESS: xt install / xt update succeed even when .xtrm/skills/default is a broken symlink, automatically replacing it with a real directory copied from the resolvePackageRoot() (npm package). Existing valid dev-mode symlinks are left alone. No manual rm + cp workaround required.

SCOPE: cli/src/commands/install.ts (runInstall), cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts (scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage / installFromRegistry), and any related tests in cli/src/tests/registry-scaffold.test.ts and cli/test/.

NON_GOALS: Do not introduce a new symlink mode for skills/default. Do not change drift/registry semantics. Do not change init flow ordering beyond what is required.

CONSTRAINTS: Source must always be the npm package root resolved via resolvePackageRoot(). Preserve existing behavior for valid symlinks (dev mode) and real directories. Idempotent. No destructive operations beyond removing a confirmed-broken symlink at .xtrm/skills/default.

VALIDATION: Add/extend unit test covering: (a) broken symlink at .xtrm/skills/default during install/update is replaced from package; (b) valid dev symlink left untouched; (c) real directory left untouched. npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm test.

OUTPUT: Diff summary, test additions, validation results.
close_reason: Fixed: runInstall now repairs broken .xtrm/skills/default symlink before installFromRegistry, sourcing from npm package root. 3 unit tests added. Reviewer PASS. Merged d8066dec.

title: xt update: refresh alias with --root multi-repo support
description:
PROBLEM: After doctor (xtrm-sjbc.7) detects drift, operator needs a one-command refresh — single repo and bulk across many repos. cli/src/commands/install.ts already handles single-repo refresh via --force. No alias, no multi-repo, no dry-run UX.

GOAL: xt update is a thin, well-documented command that drives existing install/sync machinery for one repo or many.

SUCCESS:
- xt update [--apply] [--root <dir>] [--repo <path>]
  - default (no flags): dry-run on current repo. Prints what xt update --apply would change. Reuses checkDrift output, no writes.
  - --apply: runs runInstall with --force on the target repo(s).
  - --root <dir>: discovers all xtrm-managed repos under <dir> (heuristic: contains .xtrm/registry.json) and runs update on each.
  - --repo <path>: targets one specific repo path instead of cwd.
- Per-repo atomic refresh: existing runInstall already handles file copy. Add tmp-dir + rename for the registry refresh write only if not already atomic. If a repo fails, others continue; final exit code reflects any failures.
- Output: per-repo table {repo path | status: refreshed / already-current / failed | reason if failed}. --json supported.
- Idempotent: running --apply twice on same repo = second run reports already-current.

SCOPE:
- cli/src/commands/update.ts (NEW — wraps install)
- cli/src/cli.ts or wherever commands are registered (add update)
- cli/src/core/repo-discovery.ts (NEW small helper for --root walk)
- cli/test/update.test.ts (NEW)

NON_GOALS:
- No new install logic — strictly orchestrate existing runInstall.
- No interactive prompts.
- No remote-repo support.
- No partial refresh (specific skills only) — full registry refresh per repo.

CONSTRAINTS:
- TypeScript strict.
- Multi-repo loop must not abort on single-repo failure.
- Performance: --root walk should skip node_modules/, .git/, large irrelevant trees. Use existing find-up or a simple walker.
- No new dep unless trivial.
- Existing xtrm-tools commands (install, init) keep working unchanged. update is purely additive.

VALIDATION:
- npm test passes.
- Manual: in a repo with drift, xt update prints drift table, exits 0 without writing.
- Manual: xt update --apply refreshes drifted files, second invocation reports already-current.
- Manual: in a tmpdir with 3 fake repos (2 drifted, 1 in-sync), xt update --apply --root <tmpdir> updates 2, skips 1, exits 0.

title: Vendor specialists-canonical skills + drop xtrm-tools divergent copies
description:
PROBLEM: 8 skill names overlap between xtrm-tools/.xtrm/skills/default/ and /home/dawid/dev/specialists/config/skills/. Per operator decision, ownership is:
- xtrm-tools owns: releasing (1 skill — bytes already identical between repos)
- specialists owns (canonical authoring): update-specialists, using-kpi, using-nodes, specialists-creator, using-specialists, using-specialists-v2, using-script-specialists (7 skills)

Today the 7 specialists-canonical skills are duplicated in xtrm-tools/.xtrm/skills/default/ with diverged content (4 of them) or stale identical copies (3 of them) — except using-script-specialists which is missing from xtrm-tools entirely.

GOAL: Make specialists the single source of truth for its 7 skills, keep them shipped to consumer projects via xtrm-tools npm package (no extra registry, no multi-package runtime aggregation — just a build-time vendor copy).

SUCCESS:
1. xtrm-tools repo no longer contains static copies of the 7 specialists-canonical skill dirs in .xtrm/skills/default/. They are absent at rest in this repo.
2. A vendor script (e.g. cli/scripts/vendor-specialists-skills.mjs or scripts/prepublish-vendor.mjs) runs in package.json prepublishOnly. It copies the 7 specialists-canonical skill dirs from /home/dawid/dev/specialists/config/skills/<name>/ → .xtrm/skills/default/<name>/ during publish.
3. After vendor copy, the script regenerates .xtrm/registry.json hashes for the affected skill files (reuse existing gen-registry path — currently npm run gen-registry).
4. Vendor script is robust:
   - configurable specialists path via env (SPECIALISTS_REPO_PATH default ../specialists)
   - clear error if specialists repo missing or skill dir missing
   - idempotent: running twice = same result
   - logs which skills it vendored and from what source path
5. Companion cleanup in specialists project: specialists/config/skills/releasing/ deleted from specialists repo (cross-repo bead — see CROSS-LINK below).
6. Documentation: short note in xtrm-tools docs/ explaining ownership model (releasing → xtrm-tools, 7 others → specialists vendored at publish).

SCOPE:
- xtrm-tools/.xtrm/skills/default/{update-specialists, using-kpi, using-nodes, specialists-creator, using-specialists, using-specialists-v2}/ — DELETE these 6 dirs (using-script-specialists not present)
- xtrm-tools/scripts/ — NEW vendor script
- xtrm-tools/package.json scripts.prepublishOnly — add vendor step before existing gen-registry + build
- xtrm-tools/.xtrm/registry.json — regenerated after vendor (output of gen-registry, no manual edit)
- xtrm-tools/docs/ — short ownership doc (1 page max)

NON_GOALS:
- No multi-package runtime registry aggregation.
- No new manifest.json schema.
- No header markers in SKILL.md files.
- No specialists package modifications in this bead — handled in companion bead in specialists project.
- Do NOT vendor releasing from specialists. specialists copy will be removed from there.
- No drift detection wiring (separate bead).

CONSTRAINTS:
- Vendor script must work in CI (no interactive prompts, exits non-zero on missing source).
- prepublishOnly must remain reliable: existing flow is gen-registry && build. Vendor must run BEFORE gen-registry so registry hashes reflect vendored content.
- Atomic: if vendor fails, package.json is not in a partially-broken state. Tmp-dir + rename pattern preferred.
- Existing user repos that have .xtrm/skills/default/<one-of-the-7> from a prior xtrm-tools version: they will see those files marked extra-not-canonical by future doctor — but xt update will remove them after vendor lands and registry shrinks. Migration is automatic via existing registry refresh; no special migration code needed in THIS bead.

VALIDATION:
- npm run gen-registry succeeds after vendor.
- npm test passes.
- Manual: rm -rf .xtrm/skills/default/{listed-7-minus-script}, run vendor script, confirm 6 dirs reappear with content matching ../specialists/config/skills/.
- Manual: run vendor script with SPECIALISTS_REPO_PATH=/nonexistent — script fails with clear message.
- npm pack produces tarball containing the 7 specialists-canonical skills (verify via tar -tzf).

OUTPUT: Diff summary, registry diff before/after, vendor script source, prepublishOnly changes, validation results, residual risks (e.g. divergence drift if specialists repo path is wrong in CI).

CROSS-LINK: Companion bead must be filed in /home/dawid/dev/specialists project to:
1. Delete specialists/config/skills/releasing/ (xtrm-tools owns it).
2. Document that specialists/config/skills/ is the canonical authoring location for the 7 skills, vendored into xtrm-tools at publish time.
File this AFTER xtrm-sjbc.6 (this bead) lands or in parallel — note in epic.

REFERENCES:
- explorer findings: xtrm-kdhp result, ownership catalog table
- overthinker verdict: xtrm-9tl5 (Option B = build-time vendor)
- existing gen-registry path: xtrm-tools cli/src or scripts/ (executor inspects to extend)

`.xtrm/skills/default` is in the `files` array of package.json. After removing the 7 skill dirs and vendoring at prepublishOnly, the npm pack will include the vendored content. Good.

Now I have all the evidence needed. Let me compile the analysis.

## Summary

xtrm-tools vendor pipeline for 7 specialists-canonical skills. Executor needs to: delete 6 git-tracked skill dirs, create vendor script, wire prepublishOnly, add ownership doc. One critical finding: `using-specialists/SKILL.safe.md` and `SKILL.ultra.md` exist only in xtrm-tools (not in specialists) — will be lost on vendor.

## Status
Ready for executor. All evidence collected.

## Architecture

### Current State
- **6 skill dirs** in `.xtrm/skills/default/` are git-tracked and diverged/stale vs specialists canonical:
  - `update-specialists/` — identical to specialists
  - `using-kpi/` — identical to specialists  
  - `using-nodes/` — DIFFERENT (diverged)
  - `specialists-creator/` — DIFFERENT (diverged, also missing 2 scripts from specialists: `audit-spec-uniformity.mjs`, `scaffold-specialist.ts`)
  - `using-specialists/` — DIFFERENT (diverged, has extra `SKILL.safe.md`, `SKILL.ultra.md` not in specialists)
  - `using-specialists-v2/` — DIFFERENT (diverged)
  - `using-script-specialists/` — NOT in xtrm-tools, exists only in specialists
- `releasing/` — identical between both repos, owned by xtrm-tools

### gen-registry.mjs
- Located at `scripts/gen-registry.mjs`
- Scans `.xtrm/skills/default/` (and other asset dirs), hashes every file, writes `.xtrm/registry.json`
- Uses `sha256` hash per file
- Skips `__pycache__`, respects `.gitignore` via `git check-ignore`

### prepublishOnly Flow
```
"prepublishOnly": "npm run sync:cli-version && npm run gen-registry && npm run build"
"gen-registry": "node scripts/gen-registry.mjs"
```
Vendor step must insert BEFORE `gen-registry`.

### package.json files array
Includes `.xtrm/skills/default` — vendored content will be in npm tarball.

## Key Symbols
- `scripts/gen-registry.mjs` — registry hash generator (no exports, CLI script)
- `package.json#scripts.prepublishOnly` — publish pipeline entry
- `.xtrm/registry.json` — 1643-line manifest of all asset hashes
- `.xtrm/skills/default/` — 45 entries (46 minus README.txt)

## Execution Plan for Executor

### Step 1: Delete 6 skill dirs from git
```bash
git rm -r .xtrm/skills/default/{update-specialists,using-kpi,using-nodes,specialists-creator,using-specialists,using-specialists-v2}/
```
These 6 dirs (10 files total) must be removed from git tracking.

### Step 2: Create `scripts/vendor-specialists-skills.mjs`
Requirements:
- Env `SPECIALISTS_REPO_PATH` (default `../specialists`)
- Copies 7 skill dirs: `update-specialists`, `using-kpi`, `using-nodes`, `specialists-creator`, `using-specialists`, `using-specialists-v2`, `using-script-specialists`
- Source: `$SPECIALISTS_REPO_PATH/config/skills/<name>/`
- Dest: `.xtrm/skills/default/<name>/`
- Idempotent: rm dest + copy (tmp-dir + rename for atomicity)
- Exit non-zero with clear message if source missing
- Log each vendored skill + source path

### Step 3: Wire prepublishOnly
```json
"prepublishOnly": "npm run sync:cli-version && node scripts/vendor-specialists-skills.mjs && npm run gen-registry && npm run build"
```

### Step 4: Add `.xtrm/skills/default/*` for vendored dirs to `.gitignore`
Since vendored content is generated at publish time, the dirs should NOT be git-tracked after initial deletion. Add to `.gitignore`:
```
# Vendored at publish time from specialists repo
.xtrm/skills/default/update-specialists/
.xtrm/skills/default/using-kpi/
.xtrm/skills/default/using-nodes/
.xtrm/skills/default/specialists-creator/
.xtrm/skills/default/using-specialists/
.xtrm/skills/default/using-specialists-v2/
.xtrm/skills/default/using-script-specialists/
```
Wait — but `gen-registry.mjs` uses `git check-ignore`. If vendored dirs are gitignored, they won't be in registry. Let me re-check...

Actually, re-reading gen-registry.mjs: it filters OUT gitignored files (`if (isIgnoredByGit(relativePath)) continue`). So if we gitignore the vendored dirs, their hashes won't be in registry.json. That breaks the contract.

**Resolution:** The vendored dirs must NOT be in `.gitignore`. They need to be in registry.json for consumer verification. But they shouldn't be committed to git (they're generated). This is a tension.

Options:
1. **Don't gitignore** — vendored dirs committed after each vendor run. Messy but works.
2. **Modify gen-registry** — skip the `git check-ignore` filter for vendored skills. More correct but touches gen-registry.
3. **Gitignore but override in gen-registry** — use `.gitignore` with negation pattern.

**Recommended:** Option 1 is simplest and matches the current pattern (all of default/ is committed today). The vendor script replaces content in-place. CI runs vendor + gen-registry during prepublishOnly. The git diff after vendor will show changes. Developer commits those changes. This matches the "build-time vendor" model where the working tree is always publishable.

Actually wait — the task says "xtrm-tools repo no longer contains static copies... They are absent at rest in this repo." This means they should NOT be committed. But gen-registry filters by gitignore...

**Critical decision for executor:** Either modify gen-registry to not skip gitignored files for skills dir, or accept that vendored content is committed. The task description explicitly says "absent at rest" — so gen-registry must be modified to not use git check-ignore for the vendored skills, OR the git check-ignore should be removed entirely for the skills asset (since all of default/ is published content, not source code).

### Step 5: Ownership doc
Create `docs/skills-ownership.md` (1 page). Content: ownership table, vendor model explanation.

## Risks

1. **`SKILL.safe.md` and `SKILL.ultra.md` loss** — `using-specialists/` has these extra files in xtrm-tools not present in specialists. After vendor, they disappear. Must either: (a) add them to specialists canonical, (b) keep them as xtrm-tools overlay, or (c) accept loss. **Executor must decide.**
2. **`specialists-creator/scripts/` divergence** — specialists has 2 extra scripts (`audit-spec-uniformity.mjs`, `scaffold-specialist.ts`) not in xtrm-tools. After vendor, xtrm-tools gains these. This is desired behavior.
3. **gen-registry + gitignore conflict** — If vendored dirs are gitignored (to be "absent at rest"), gen-registry's `isIgnoredByGit` filter skips them, breaking registry. **Must modify gen-registry** or keep dirs committed.
4. **CI path** — `SPECIALISTS_REPO_PATH` must be correct in CI. If CI checks out only xtrm-tools, specialists won't be adjacent. CI config needs to clone specialists too, or vendor step runs only when publishing from a machine that has both repos.
5. **using-script-specialists** — not in xtrm-tools today. Vendor will create it. Registry will grow by 1 skill.

## Follow-ups
- Companion bead in specialists: delete `releasing/`, document canonical authoring location
- Drift detection wiring (separate bead per NON_GOALS)
- CI pipeline config for dual-repo checkout during publish

title: xt update: refresh alias with --root multi-repo support
description:
PROBLEM: After doctor (xtrm-sjbc.7) detects drift, operator needs a one-command refresh — single repo and bulk across many repos. cli/src/commands/install.ts already handles single-repo refresh via --force. No alias, no multi-repo, no dry-run UX.

GOAL: xt update is a thin, well-documented command that drives existing install/sync machinery for one repo or many.

SUCCESS:
- xt update [--apply] [--root <dir>] [--repo <path>]
  - default (no flags): dry-run on current repo. Prints what xt update --apply would change. Reuses checkDrift output, no writes.
  - --apply: runs runInstall with --force on the target repo(s).
  - --root <dir>: discovers all xtrm-managed repos under <dir> (heuristic: contains .xtrm/registry.json) and runs update on each.
  - --repo <path>: targets one specific repo path instead of cwd.
- Per-repo atomic refresh: existing runInstall already handles file copy. Add tmp-dir + rename for the registry refresh write only if not already atomic. If a repo fails, others continue; final exit code reflects any failures.
- Output: per-repo table {repo path | status: refreshed / already-current / failed | reason if failed}. --json supported.
- Idempotent: running --apply twice on same repo = second run reports already-current.

SCOPE:
- cli/src/commands/update.ts (NEW — wraps install)
- cli/src/cli.ts or wherever commands are registered (add update)
- cli/src/core/repo-discovery.ts (NEW small helper for --root walk)
- cli/test/update.test.ts (NEW)

NON_GOALS:
- No new install logic — strictly orchestrate existing runInstall.
- No interactive prompts.
- No remote-repo support.
- No partial refresh (specific skills only) — full registry refresh per repo.

CONSTRAINTS:
- TypeScript strict.
- Multi-repo loop must not abort on single-repo failure.
- Performance: --root walk should skip node_modules/, .git/, large irrelevant trees. Use existing find-up or a simple walker.
- No new dep unless trivial.
- Existing xtrm-tools commands (install, init) keep working unchanged. update is purely additive.

VALIDATION:
- npm test passes.
- Manual: in a repo with drift, xt update prints drift table, exits 0 without writing.
- Manual: xt update --apply refreshes drifted files, second invocation reports already-current.
- Manual: in a tmpdir with 3 fake repos (2 drifted, 1 in-sync), xt update --apply --root <tmpdir> updates 2, skips 1, exits 0.
- Manual: xt update --apply --root <tmpdir-where-one-repo-is-broken> continues and reports the broken one as failed; exit code reflects failure.
- xt update --json output is valid JSON.

OUTPUT: Diff summary, sample CLI output (single + multi repo), validation results, residual risks (e.g. partial refresh between hooks regen and skills materialization if interrupted).

DEPENDS ON: xtrm-sjbc.7 (doctor wiring) for shared drift-check primitives. Hard-depends on existing runInstall remaining stable.

REFERENCES:
- explorer findings: xtrm-kdhp gap analysis row 4 (PARTIAL — install --force exists but no alias/dry-run/multi-repo)
- existing runInstall in cli/src/commands/install.ts

title: Wire skills+hooks drift check into xt doctor
description:
PROBLEM: cli/src/commands/doctor.ts only checks CLAUDE.md fragments today. drift.ts already implements full sha256-based comparison (missing/upToDate/drifted) against .xtrm/registry.json, and skills-runtime-views.ts validates active view + pointers. Neither is wired into doctor — operators have no single command to verify Cat B health.

GOAL: xt doctor reports skills + hooks drift, runtime view validity, and registry health, reusing existing machinery.

SUCCESS:
- xt doctor (default invocation, no flags) includes a Cat B section in its output:
  - Skills: per-skill status (in-sync / drifted / missing-from-snapshot / extra-not-canonical) using checkDrift over .xtrm/registry.json skills asset.
  - Hooks: per-hook same statuses for skills_optional and hooks assets in registry.
  - Runtime view: assertRuntimeSkillsViews result (activeReady, claudePointerReady, piPointerReady).
  - Duplicate canonical name guard: scan .xtrm/skills/default/ + ../specialists/config/skills/ (when SPECIALISTS_REPO_PATH is set) — fail if any name appears in both.
- Output formats:
  - Default: human table grouped by section, color-coded.
  - --json: structured object {skills:[...], hooks:[...], runtimeView:{...}, duplicates:[...], summary:{ok|warnings|errors counts}}.
- --check-drift flag: same content as default but exits non-zero if any drifted/missing/extra/duplicate found. Useful in CI.
- Multi-repo aggregation deferred — single repo for now.

SCOPE:
- cli/src/commands/doctor.ts (extend createDoctorCommand)
- cli/src/core/drift.ts (consume — no changes)
- cli/src/core/skills-runtime-views.ts (consume — no changes)
- cli/src/core/skill-discovery.ts (use to list installed skills for duplicate check)
- cli/test/doctor.test.ts (NEW or extended)

NON_GOALS:
- No fix actions (xt update is separate bead).
- No --root multi-repo flag (separate bead).
- No new drift taxonomy beyond the 4 states drift.ts already returns.
- No hook semantic validation (just file presence/hash).

CONSTRAINTS:
- Performance: <2s on this repo (current registry has ~294 files).
- TypeScript strict.
- No new deps. Reuse chalk/cli-table or whatever doctor.ts already uses for output.
- Idempotent and side-effect-free (doctor must never write).

VALIDATION:
- npm test passes (new doctor tests + existing).
- Manual: edit .xtrm/skills/default/clean-code/SKILL.md → xt doctor reports drifted with specific file path.
- Manual: rm -rf .xtrm/skills/default/some-skill → xt doctor reports missing.
- Manual: touch .xtrm/skills/default/fake-skill/SKILL.md → xt doctor reports extra-not-canonical.
- Manual: with SPECIALISTS_REPO_PATH=../specialists set, doctor reports duplicate-canonical-name=0 after vendor bead .6 lands. Before .6, reports the 7 expected duplicates.
- xt doctor --json output is valid JSON parsable by jq.
- xt doctor --check-drift exits 0 on clean repo, non-zero on drift.

OUTPUT: Diff summary (doctor.ts + tests), sample human output, sample --json output, validation results.

DEPENDS ON: xtrm-sjbc.5 (bug fixes) for runtime view consistency. Soft-depends on .6 (vendor) for clean duplicate check baseline, but doctor itself is independent.

- explorer findings: xtrm-kdhp current state map, gap analysis row 3 (doctor MISSING for Cat B)

title: Bug fix: skills layout flat, stale per-runtime tests, dead .pi entry
description:

2. cli/test/skills-runtime-sync.test.ts:105-106,143-144 expects activeClaudeReady/activePiReady fields. cli/src/core/skills-runtime-views.ts only exports activeReady (single boolean). Tests are stale from pre-flat-layout era and currently fail or are skipped (HIGH).

DECISION (per operator): layout is FLAT. .xtrm/skills/active/ is the single materialized view, no per-runtime split. SKILLS_RUNTIMES iteration in layout helpers is dead code from earlier model.

SUCCESS:
- skills-layout.ts no longer iterates SKILLS_RUNTIMES creating per-runtime active/claude/ and active/pi/ subdirs. ensureSkillsTreeStructure creates only active/ (or removes that responsibility entirely if covered elsewhere).

- releasing, specialists-creator, using-nodes, using-specialists, using-specialists-v2 (diverge)
- update-specialists, using-kpi (match)
- specialists-only (not in xtrm-tools default): using-script-specialists

Known internal inconsistency reported by overthinker:
- .pi/settings.json contains both ../.xtrm/skills/active and ../.xtrm/skills/active/pi
- cli/test/skills-runtime-sync.test.ts expects active/claude and active/pi paths and fields not present in current implementation

SUCCESS:
1. Map of existing Cat B machinery: which files implement what, which paths are live, which are dead/legacy.
2. Gap analysis: of the 5-point MVP scope (single owner, registry reuse, doctor drift, update alias, migration policy), what is already implemented, what is partially implemented with bugs, what is genuinely missing.

   - SHARED-CANONICAL: meant to be available to any xtrm-managed project — should have ONE owner; recommend which owner (xtrm-tools vs specialists) and why
   - DUPLICATE-DIVERGED: shipped in both with different bytes — recommend canonical version (which repo has the more up-to-date / authoritative content) and migration path
4. Concrete catalog of internal inconsistencies/bugs in current implementation (paths, tests, .pi settings) with severity.
5. Clear answer to: how should specialists-repo continue to author skills that should be available in user projects, given xtrm-tools is the npm distributor?
   - Option A: specialists ships its own .xtrm/registry + skills, xt install reads from any installed package with a registry
   - Option B: xtrm-tools vendors specialists skills at publish time (build-time copy from ../specialists)
   - Option C: specialists skills live ONLY in specialists repo and are not auto-distributed; users opt-in by adding specialists as dep

---

## 5. RECOMMENDED ANSWER — How Specialists Skills Ship

### Recommend: **Option B — Build-time vendor from specialists into xtrm-tools**

**Rationale:**

1. **xtrm-tools IS the npm distributor.** `package.json` already ships `.xtrm/skills/default/`. Users install `xtrm-tools` globally, not `specialists`. Adding a second npm package for skill distribution adds complexity without value.

2. **specialists already ships `config/` via npm** (package.json `files` includes `config/`). But this is for the specialists MCP server itself, not for xtrm skill distribution. Different install path, different consumers.

3. **Option A (multi-registry) adds runtime complexity.** `installFromRegistry` would need to scan `node_modules` for any package with a `.xtrm/registry.json`. This is solvable but adds a discovery layer, version conflict resolution, and ordering issues. Not worth it for ~3 skills.

4. **Option C (no auto-distribution) breaks the user experience.** Users expect `xt install` to give them specialists skills. Making them add specialists as a dep is friction.

### Implementation path:

1. **specialists remains canonical author for 3 skills**: `specialists-creator`, `using-specialists`, `using-specialists-v2`. Changes authored there first.

2. **Build script in xtrm-tools copies canonical versions at publish time**: `cp -r ../specialists/config/skills/{specialists-creator,using-specialists,using-specialists-v2} .xtrm/skills/default/` as a pre-publish or version bump step.

3. **Registry hash update**: After copy, regenerate `.xtrm/registry.json` hashes for affected files.

4. **specialists deletes its local copies** of xtrm-tools-owned skills (releasing, update-specialists, using-kpi, using-nodes). Those live only in xtrm-tools.

5. **using-script-specialists stays specialists-only**. Not vendored. Not in xtrm-tools. Users working ON specialists get it from the repo directly.

title: Overthinker critique: Cat B distribution epic xtrm-sjbc + proposed multi-source architecture
description:
PROBLEM: Before dispatching implementation work on epic xtrm-sjbc (Cat B distribution: filesystem-bound skills + hooks management for xtrm-managed projects), need a hard adversarial critique of (a) the original 4-bead plan as written, (b) the orchestrator-proposed extensions (optional/ tier, multi-source manifest, priority-based conflict resolution, extended doctor taxonomy), and (c) the entire problem framing.

User explicitly requested: do not overcomplicate. Critique should aggressively challenge whether each piece of complexity is justified or premature.

CONTEXT — original epic (xtrm-sjbc):
- Goal: xtrm-tools owns Category B (filesystem-bound) asset distribution. Skills must live on disk in .claude/skills/active/ for Claude Code to read them; hooks similar.
- Children:
  - .1 .xtrm/skills/default/ as managed snapshot of canonical skills (originally framed as sourced from specialists package)
  - .2 .xtrm/hooks/default/ same for hooks
  - .3 xt doctor --check-drift: byte-compare local snapshot vs canonical
  - .4 xt update [--apply] [--root <dir>] [--repo <path>]: bulk refresh
- Layout proposed: .xtrm/skills/{default,user}/ → .claude/skills/active/ as symlink farm (copy fallback Windows)
- Header marker per file: <!-- xtrm-managed v=X source=<pkg>/<v> -->

CONTEXT — orchestrator extensions during planning:
1. Add optional/ tier: .xtrm/skills/{default,optional,user}/ with available-optional doctor state and xt install <name> / xt remove <name>
2. Multi-source: canonical sources are BOTH xtrm-tools npm package AND dev/specialists/config/skills/. Some skills overlap (specialists-creator, releasing exist in both).
3. Manifest-driven: each canonical package ships .xtrm/manifest.json declaring {skills:{default,optional}, hooks, priority}
4. Header marker carries source=<owner>/<v> and tier=default|optional
5. Priority-based conflict resolution + .xtrm/overrides.json for local override
6. Extended doctor taxonomy: in-sync, drifted, missing-from-snapshot, available-optional, extra-not-canonical, conflict-overridden, orphan-owner

CONTEXT — concrete state:
- /home/dawid/dev/specialists/config/skills/ contains: releasing, specialists-creator, update-specialists, using-kpi, using-nodes, using-script-specialists, using-specialists, using-specialists-v2 (8 skills)
- /home/dawid/dev/xtrm-tools/.xtrm/skills/default/ contains ~30+ skills
- Overlap: at minimum specialists-creator, releasing
- User has many repos suffering from skill/hook drift

SUCCESS: Overthinker produces a critique covering at minimum:
1. Whether the underlying problem (Cat B drift across repos) is even worth solving at this level of formality, or whether a simpler solution dominates (e.g. just rsync, just a postinstall hook, just one canonical owner with no manifest).
2. Whether the original 4-bead plan is over- or under-scoped given its goals.
3. Each orchestrator extension judged on cost vs benefit:
   - Is optional/ tier needed now, or YAGNI?
   - Is multi-source aggregation real or solvable by consolidation?
   - Is manifest.json premature when header markers already encode source?
   - Is priority/override system over-engineering when conflicts could be banned by convention?
   - Is the extended doctor taxonomy useful or theoretical?
4. Hidden assumptions or missing concerns:
   - Symlink farm semantics on macOS, Linux, Windows; what happens on git operations, fresh clones, npm reinstalls
   - Versioning of xtrm-tools npm package itself: who bumps when, what triggers republish
   - Bootstrap problem: how does .xtrm/skills/default/ get populated before xt is installed
   - Migration: existing repos with .claude/skills/active/ already populated — destructive vs preserve
   - The overlap problem (specialists-creator, releasing) — should it be resolved by deduplication at source rather than runtime priority
   - Test strategy: how do you test multi-repo scenarios without burning real fs operations
5. Concrete simpler alternatives that should be considered before building this:
   - Single source of truth (delete dev/specialists/config/skills/ overlaps, xtrm-tools owns Cat B period)
   - No manifest, just walk node_modules/<pkg>/.xtrm/skills/default/
   - No optional tier, all canonical = default, user opts out via removing from .xtrm/skills/user-disabled/
   - npm postinstall hook does the refresh, no xt update command
6. If the whole approach should be replaced with something else, propose it.

SCOPE: Critique only. No code, no bead modifications during this run.

NON_GOALS:
- No implementation
- No bead edits (orchestrator will apply chosen direction afterward)
- No mild critique. User explicitly asked for HEAVY criticism.

CONSTRAINTS:
- READ_ONLY behavior expected
- May inspect /home/dawid/dev/xtrm-tools and /home/dawid/dev/specialists to ground claims
- May read xtrm-tools cli/lib/ to understand current install pipeline
- Cite files/paths where claims are grounded
- Do not propose grand new architecture without first justifying that the simpler alternative was rejected

VALIDATION: Output addresses each of the 6 critique axes above with specific concerns and at minimum 3 concrete simplifications that should be evaluated before the proposed plan is dispatched.

OUTPUT: Structured critique with:
- Overall verdict (PROCEED / SIMPLIFY / REPLACE / ABANDON) with one-paragraph rationale
- Per-bead and per-extension cost/benefit table
- Top 5 risks or hidden assumptions
- Top 3 concrete simpler alternatives, each with what it sacrifices
- Recommended minimum viable scope for the epic

Italian or English output is fine — user reads both.
notes:
Completed read-only critique. Key finding: Cat B framing stale; xtrm-tools already ships registry-based skills/hooks distribution, optional tier, and runtime materializer. Main risk is adding multi-source/priority/doctor complexity before consolidating canonical ownership and resolving runtime model inconsistencies.
### Specialist Output — overthinker (job e1898a) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Phase 1 - Initial Analysis

### Problem shape

Cat B drift problem real. Repos drift. Skills/hooks live on disk. Runtime reads filesystem, not package graph.

But framing stale.

Why stale:
- Skills distribution already exists in `xtrm-tools`.
- Hooks distribution already exists in `xtrm-tools`.
- Optional tier already exists in code and package payload.
- Drift engine already exists.
- Proposed epic reads like greenfield. Repo says not greenfield.

### Grounded evidence

Current package already ships Cat B payload:
- `package.json` publishes `.xtrm/hooks`, `.xtrm/skills/default`, `.xtrm/skills/optional`, `.xtrm/registry.json`
- `package.json` `prepublishOnly` runs `npm run gen-registry && npm run build`

Current registry-based distribution already exists:
- `.xtrm/registry.json` has asset groups for `hooks` and `skills`, with per-file `hash` + `version`
- `cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts` installs from registry
- `cli/src/core/drift.ts` classifies `missing | upToDate | drifted`

Current runtime materialization already exists:
- `cli/src/core/skills-layout.ts` defines tiers `default | optional | user`
- `cli/src/core/skills-state.ts` persists enabled packs
- `cli/src/core/skill-discovery.ts` discovers default skills and optional/user packs
- `cli/src/core/skills-materializer.ts` builds `.xtrm/skills/active`
- `cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts` points `.claude/skills -> ../.xtrm/skills/active`

Current hooks wiring already exists:
- `cli/src/core/claude-runtime-sync.ts` reads `.xtrm/config/hooks.json`
- rewrites commands to project `.xtrm/hooks`
- `installFromRegistry` already copies `.xtrm/hooks/*`

Current overlap already exists:
- `../specialists/config/skills/` has 8 skills
- `.xtrm/skills/default/` has 46 skill dirs
- overlaps not theoretical. Checksums differ for `releasing`, `specialists-creator`, `using-nodes`, `using-specialists`, `using-specialists-v2`
- some match exactly: `update-specialists`, `using-kpi`

### First-pass judgment

Problem worth solving. But not with fresh architecture fantasy.

Core issue not “how invent manifest/priorities/owners.”  
Core issue “who owns canonical bytes, how package ships them, how repos refresh safely.”

If owner unclear, every extra layer compounds drift.

---

## Phase 2 - Devil's Advocate

### Biggest attack: maybe this whole epic solving wrong problem

Maybe simpler dominates:
- publish one package
- make package payload canonical
- run refresh on `xtrm init` / `xtrm install`
- detect drift with existing registry hashes
- stop

If that works, optional tiers, multi-source manifests, priority resolution, override taxonomies all noise.

### Attack original 4-bead plan

#### Bead .1 `.xtrm/skills/default/ as managed snapshot`
Problem: already exists.
- payload already shipped in package
- registry already tracks files
- scaffold/install already copy it

So bead either duplicate or badly specified.

#### Bead .2 `.xtrm/hooks/default/`
Problem: bad fit with current hooks model.
- current hooks live flat under `.xtrm/hooks`
- hook generation comes from `.xtrm/config/hooks.json`
- `claude-runtime-sync.ts` already rewrites hook commands to project-local `.xtrm/hooks`

Forcing `hooks/default/` symmetry smells aesthetic, not functional.

#### Bead .3 `xt doctor --check-drift`
Need small add, not new subsystem.
- drift logic already in `cli/src/core/drift.ts`
- install/status already use drift calculation
- current `doctor.ts` only checks `CLAUDE.md` fragments

Real task: wire existing drift report into doctor. Not invent new drift model.

#### Bead .4 `xt update`
Likely duplicate of `xtrm install` / `xtrm init`.
- `cli/src/commands/install.ts` already refreshes registry assets
- `runInstall()` already does hooks, registry copy, pi sync, runtime rebuild

If user pain is discoverability, rename or alias command. Not new engine.

### Attack orchestrator extensions

#### 1. `optional/` tier
Premature as proposal. Worse: already exists.
- `skills-layout.ts` ships `optional`
- package publishes `.xtrm/skills/optional`
- `skills-state.ts` persists enabled packs

So either:
- feature already landed and epic docs are stale, or
- team not aligned on actual behavior

Adding `xt install <name>` now may be okay. Adding new tier model is not.

#### 2. Multi-source aggregation
Hard no at runtime. Maybe yes at build time.

Why:
- consumer repos install `xtrm-tools` package
- they do not have sibling repo `../specialists`
- package payload in `package.json` does not include specialists repo directly

So runtime multi-source means package would need vendored second source anyway. If vendored, runtime multi-source disappears. Build step solved it.

#### 3. `manifest.json` per canonical package
Looks redundant.

You already have:
- package publish list in `package.json`
- per-file registry in `.xtrm/registry.json`
- frontmatter versions inside many `SKILL.md` files
- pack metadata in `PACK.json`

New manifest adds fifth truth source.

#### 4. Header marker `source=<owner>/<v> tier=...`
Looks neat. Adds file churn. Weak payoff.
- registry already stores version/hash
- headers mutate content for every managed file
- markdown okay, scripts less okay
- local edits now mix content drift with metadata drift

If ownership matters, expose via doctor/report from registry. Do not stamp every file.

#### 5. Priority conflict resolution + overrides
Over-engineering.

Current better rule:
- duplicate canonical skill names forbidden
- consolidate overlap at source
- fail build/publish if collision exists

`skills-materializer.ts` already throws on duplicate runtime names. Good. Keep that spirit.

Priority systems hide ownership rot.

#### 6. Extended doctor taxonomy
Mostly theoretical.

Useful states:
- in-sync
- missing
- drifted
Maybe:
- duplicate-canonical-name
- broken-symlink / invalid-runtime-view

Less useful now:
- orphan-owner
- conflict-overridden
- available-optional
- extra-not-canonical

Those require mature owner model, override model, optional UX. Not there.

### Attack whole framing

Framing assumes design space still open. Repo says design space already partially chosen.

Worse: current implementation inconsistent.

Examples:
- `skills-layout.ts` resolves active root to `.xtrm/skills/active`
- `skills-runtime-views.ts` validates `.pi/settings.json` contains `../.xtrm/skills/active`
- repo `.pi/settings.json` currently contains both `../.xtrm/skills/active` and `../.xtrm/skills/active/pi`
- `cli/test/skills-runtime-sync.test.ts` still expects `active/claude` and `active/pi`, plus fields not present in current implementation

That means architecture not even internally clean yet. Adding multi-source conflict logic on top now reckless.

---

## Phase 3 - Synthesis

## Summary

Verdict: **SIMPLIFY**

Problem real. Proposed complexity not earned. Epic should shrink to ownership consolidation + doctor/update UX around already-existing registry/materializer system.

## Status

Read-only critique complete. No file changes.

## Architecture

Current actual architecture:
1. package publishes Cat B payload  
   `package.json`, `.xtrm/registry.json`
2. install copies managed files into repo `.xtrm/*`  
   `cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts`
3. hooks wiring generated into Claude settings  
   `cli/src/core/claude-runtime-sync.ts`
4. skills runtime view materialized from `default + enabled optional/user packs`  
   `cli/src/core/skills-materializer.ts`
5. `.claude/skills` points to `.xtrm/skills/active`  
   `cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts`

So epic should not ask “what architecture?”  
Epic should ask “what minimum cleanup makes current architecture reliable?”

## Changes

No code changes. Findings only.

## Verification

Grounded files:
- `package.json`
- `.xtrm/registry.json`
- `cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts`
- `cli/src/core/drift.ts`
- `cli/src/core/skills-layout.ts`
- `cli/src/core/skills-state.ts`
- `cli/src/core/skill-discovery.ts`
- `cli/src/core/skills-materializer.ts`
- `cli/src/core/skills-runtime-views.ts`
- `cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts`
- `cli/src/core/claude-runtime-sync.ts`
- `cli/src/commands/install.ts`
- `cli/src/commands/doctor.ts`
- `cli/test/skills-runtime-sync.test.ts`
- `../specialists/config/skills/*`

## Risks

### Top 5 hidden risks / assumptions

1. **Ownership ambiguity**  
   Same skill exists in two repos with divergent bytes. Runtime priority cannot fix source governance.

2. **Windows story not done**  
   Code always calls `fs.symlink`. Tests skip symlink behavior on win32. “copy fallback Windows” not implemented in `skills-scaffold.ts` or materializer tests.

3. **Architecture drift inside source repo**  
   Active runtime model inconsistent across code, tests, repo state, `.pi/settings.json`.

4. **Build-time vs runtime confusion**  
   Multi-source from `../specialists` impossible in consumer repos unless vendored during publish.

5. **Migration blast radius**  
   `ensureSkillsSymlink()` removes real `.claude/skills` directories. Existing hand-managed repos can lose local content unless migration rules explicit.

## Follow-ups

### Per-bead + per-extension cost/benefit table

| Item | Value | Cost | Critique |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Bead .1 skills snapshot | Medium | Low | Already exists. Re-scope to cleanup/docs, not creation. |
| Bead .2 hooks snapshot | Low | Medium | Current hooks model not symmetric with skills. Avoid fake `hooks/default/` elegance. |
| Bead .3 doctor drift | High | Low | Worth doing. Reuse `drift.ts`. No new taxonomy yet. |
| Bead .4 xt update | Medium | Low-Med | Likely alias/reframe of existing `xtrm install`/`init`, not new engine. |
| Optional tier | Low now | Medium | Already exists technically. UX can wait until real demand. |
| Multi-source manifest | Low | High | Runtime multi-source wrong layer. Solve by source consolidation or build vendoring. |
| Per-package `manifest.json` | Low | High | Another truth source. Registry already exists. |
| Header source/tier markers | Low | Medium | Registry already tracks version/hash. Markers add churn, not control. |
| Priority conflict resolution | Very low | High | Ban duplicate canonical names instead. |
| Overrides file | Low | Medium | Same. Smells like patching governance failure. |
| Extended doctor taxonomy | Low-Med | Medium | Keep 3-5 states max. More states before owner model solid = theater. |

## Acceptance Criteria

Minimum good outcome for epic:
- one canonical owner for Cat B bytes
- package publish reliably vendors all canonical Cat B assets
- `doctor` reports `in-sync | missing | drifted | duplicate | invalid-runtime-view`
- refresh command reuses existing install path
- migration policy for existing `.claude/skills` stated and tested
- Windows explicitly unsupported or explicitly implemented

## Top 3 concrete simpler alternatives

### 1. Single source of truth
**Action:** xtrm-tools owns Cat B. Mirror or copy specialists skills into package at publish time. Ban runtime dual ownership.  
**Sacrifice:** specialists repo loses direct canonical authority for those skill files.

### 2. No manifest, no priority, no overrides
**Action:** use existing `.xtrm/registry.json` + existing optional `PACK.json`. Build fails on duplicate canonical skill names.  
**Sacrifice:** no fancy per-owner metadata in runtime files.

### 3. No new `xt update`
**Action:** make `xt update` thin alias to existing `xtrm install` or `xtrm init --sync-only`; add better help text.  
**Sacrifice:** less “clean-sheet” command naming.

## Recommended minimum viable scope for epic

1. **Freeze owner model**
   - choose single canonical owner for every Cat B skill/hook
   - resolve overlap at source, not runtime

2. **Use existing registry pipeline**
   - keep `.xtrm/registry.json` as truth
   - no new package manifests
   - no header markers

3. **Small doctor upgrade**
   - plug `drift.ts` into `doctor`
   - add runtime-view validation
   - add duplicate canonical name detection

4. **Small refresh UX**
   - alias existing install/sync path
   - no brand new update engine

5. **Migration/test hardening**
   - explicit behavior for existing `.claude/skills`
   - win32 stance explicit
   - multi-repo fixture tests using temp dirs only

## Machine-readable block


title: xt update: bulk refresh xtrm-managed assets across one or many repos
description:
PROBLEM: After sibling .3 detects drift, the operator needs a single command to apply the fix across N repos.

SUCCESS:
- xt update [--apply] [--root <dir>] [--repo <path>]:
  - Without --apply: dry-run, prints what would change.
  - With --apply: refreshes .xtrm/skills/default/ and .xtrm/hooks/default/ from canonical for each detected repo.
  - --root <dir>: discovers all xtrm-managed repos under <dir> and processes each.
  - --repo <path>: targets a single repo.
- Per-repo atomic refresh: write to .xtrm/skills/default.tmp/, swap on success, leave existing on failure.
- Output: per-repo table (refreshed / already-current / failed reason).
- Failures in one repo do not abort the loop.

SCOPE: xtrm-tools (xt update command), repo-discovery helper.

NON_GOALS:
- No specialist-asset refresh (Category A is loader-live, no refresh needed).
- No .claude/skills/active/ symlink rebuild — symlinks should auto-follow .xtrm/skills/default/ (or rebuilt only if symlink target moved).

CONSTRAINTS:
- Reviewer PASS gate.
- Atomic per repo.
- Idempotent: running twice produces same result on second.

VALIDATION:
- 3 fake repos under tmpdir, two with drift, one in-sync. xt update --apply --root <tmpdir> updates 2 / skips 1.

OUTPUT: Diff summary, sample multi-repo run, residual risks.

title: P2: Restructure Pi extension sources into packages/pi-extensions
description:
## Context

The current runtime depends on 15 extension directories under `.xtrm/ext-src/`. They need to move under the new package while preserving extension behavior, imports, themes, and internal shared utilities.

Layer signal: boundary/package contract. This phase moves the source-of-truth for extension code and establishes the import surface later used by the package entrypoint.

## What to build

- Migrate the 15 managed extensions from `.xtrm/ext-src/` into `packages/pi-extensions/`.
- Fold the current `@xtrm/pi-core` helpers into internal package modules while preserving imports used by extensions.
- Carry forward non-code assets such as themes and package-local metadata.
- Leave clear mapping notes for any extension path changes that ripple into policies, tests, registry generation, or packaging.

## File paths expected to change

- `.xtrm/ext-src/**`
- `packages/pi-extensions/extensions/**`
- `packages/pi-extensions/src/core/**`
- `packages/pi-extensions/src/shared/**`
- `packages/pi-extensions/themes/**`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All managed extension source files have a defined destination under `packages/pi-extensions/`.
- [ ] Shared `pi-core` helpers are internalized without requiring a separate published `@xtrm/pi-core` package.
- [ ] Theme/assets used by `xtrm-ui` and similar extensions are preserved in the new package layout.
- [ ] The migration plan identifies every legacy path that still needs follow-up updates.

## Approach notes

Preserve extension behavior first; do not mix runtime-install changes into this phase. Favor a layout that keeps extension modules easy to register from one top-level entrypoint.

title: P3: Add single @xtrm/pi-extensions entrypoint and extension registry
description:
## Context

The finalized design calls for one package entrypoint (`src/index.ts`) that delegates to sub-modules instead of publishing 15 separate Pi packages. This phase turns the migrated sources into a loadable package contract.

Layer signal: boundary + shell. It defines how Pi discovers and registers extensions from the package.

## What to build

- Implement `packages/pi-extensions/src/index.ts` as the single public entrypoint.
- Create the internal extension registry/delegation layer that loads all managed extensions from sub-modules.
- Ensure package metadata points Pi at the correct entrypoint/extension registration path.
- Capture any compatibility shims needed so extension modules keep their event wiring semantics.

## File paths expected to change

- `packages/pi-extensions/src/index.ts`
- `packages/pi-extensions/src/extensions/**`
- `packages/pi-extensions/package.json`
- `packages/pi-extensions/src/registry.ts`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The package exposes one top-level entrypoint for Pi.
- [ ] The entrypoint delegates to all managed extension modules without reintroducing multiple publishable packages.
- [ ] The package manifest and source layout agree on how Pi should discover extensions.
- [ ] The design preserves raw TypeScript runtime loading; no build artifact is required at runtime.

## Approach notes

Keep this phase focused on package-level registration semantics. Runtime install flows in `cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts` are handled separately in the next phase.
notes:
Implemented ✅ `P3` package-level registration contract with a single Pi entrypoint.

### Changed files

- `packages/pi-extensions/package.json`
- `packages/pi-extensions/src/index.ts`
- `packages/pi-extensions/src/registry.ts`
- `packages/pi-extensions/src/extensions/*.ts` (14 extension shim modules)

### What was added

1. **Single public entrypoint**
   - `packages/pi-extensions/src/index.ts` now exports one default registration function:
     - `registerXtrmPiExtensions(pi)`
   - This is the only top-level Pi entrypoint for the package.

2. **Internal extension registry/delegation**
   - `packages/pi-extensions/src/registry.ts` defines:
     - `managedPiExtensions` (full managed extension list)
     - `registerManagedPiExtensions(pi)` (iterates + registers all)
   - Added per-extension registration guard (`try/catch`) so one failing extension won’t prevent others from wiring (compatibility behavior).

3. **Compatibility shim layer**
   - `packages/pi-extensions/src/extensions/*.ts` wrappers forward to existing extension modules under:
     - `config/pi/extensions/<name>/index.ts`
   - This preserves each module’s existing event wiring semantics while exposing a unified package contract.

4. **Package metadata aligned for Pi discovery**
   - `packages/pi-extensions/package.json` now includes:
     - `pi.extensions: ["./src/index.ts"]`
     - `exports` pointing to `./src/index.ts` and `./src/registry.ts`
     - `keywords` includes `pi-package`
   - Keeps **raw TypeScript runtime loading** (no build artifact required).

---

If you want, I can do the next pass to switch runtime installer wiring (`cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts`) from directory-style extension sync to this single-package contract.

---

title: P4: Replace Pi extension symlink flow with pi install in pi-runtime
description:
## Context

`runPiRuntimeSync()` currently has CRITICAL blast radius through `runPiInstall`, `createPiCommand`, `runProjectInit`, and `runInstall`. It still relies on `linkExtensionsToGlobal()` and `ensureNpmPackageExtensionSymlinks()` instead of package installation. This phase is the heart of the migration.

Layer signal: shell/orchestration. The work rewires install-time behavior rather than changing extension feature logic.

## What to build

- Remove or isolate the old `linkExtensionsToGlobal()` / symlink-specific flows from the main project-install path.
- Ensure runtime inventory, plan rendering, and sync reporting reflect the new package-based model.
- Preserve existing Pi package installs that are intentionally global (`pi-gitnexus`, `pi-serena-tools`, etc.).

## File paths expected to change

- `cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts`
- `cli/src/commands/pi-install.ts`
- `cli/src/commands/pi.ts`
- `cli/src/commands/install.ts`
- `cli/src/commands/init.ts`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Project Pi runtime sync no longer depends on repo-to-agent extension symlinks for managed XTRM extensions.
- [ ] `runPiRuntimeSync()` installs or verifies `npm:@xtrm/pi-extensions` in the intended Pi scope.
- [ ] Existing required external Pi packages remain installed and reported correctly.
- [ ] The blast radius callers (`runPiInstall`, `xt pi`, `xt init`, `xt install`) still route through a coherent sync flow.

## Approach notes

GitNexus CLI impact on `runPiRuntimeSync` returned CRITICAL risk. Watch direct caller `runPiInstall` and command entrypoints in `cli/src/commands/{pi,init,install}.ts` when defining acceptance criteria and test coverage.
