title: Implement xt skills runtime controls and user pack scaffolding
description:
## Context
`xt skills` exists, but it only lists default skills and returns stub responses for `enable` / `disable`. The finalized design adds per-runtime targeting, pack discovery across optional and user tiers, and pack creation for user-authored packs.

Relevant exploration:
- `cli/src/commands/skills.ts` currently implements `list`, and stubs `enable` / `disable` with “coming in v0.9”.
- `createSkillsCommand` has LOW upstream blast radius from GitNexus (direct registration in `cli/src/index.ts`).
- The new CLI contract is:
  - `xt skills list [--claude|--pi] [--json]`
  - `xt skills enable <pack>|all [--claude|--pi]`
  - `xt skills disable <pack>|all [--claude|--pi]`
  - `xt skills create-pack <name>`

## What to build
- Extend `xt skills list` to report default skills, pack inventory, enabled packs, and runtime-specific active resolution.
- Implement `enable` / `disable` so they mutate `state.json`, rebuild the targeted runtime(s), and support `--claude`, `--pi`, or both by default.
- Add `xt skills create-pack <name>` for user packs under `.xtrm/skills/user/packs/<name>/` with required `pack.json` metadata scaffold.
- Ensure JSON output is machine-readable and reflects runtime scope accurately.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `list` reports the new tiered architecture and runtime enablement state.
- [ ] `enable` / `disable` target Claude only, Pi only, or both when no runtime flag is supplied.
- [ ] `all` behaves deterministically for optional + user packs without affecting default skills.
- [ ] `create-pack` scaffolds a valid user pack with required metadata fields.

title: Migrate install/init/worktree flows to active skills views
description:
## Context
The architecture change spans install/init/bootstrap behavior, generated symlinks, and existing skill-delivery assumptions. The repo still contains legacy `.agents/skills` handling, `service-skills-set` installer logic that copies into `.claude/skills`, and init/install flows that scaffold or verify the old default-only topology.

Relevant exploration:
- `cli/src/commands/init.ts` runs `scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage()` and then `ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink()` before project bootstrap.
- `cli/src/commands/install-service-skills.ts` still copies trinity skills directly into `.claude/skills/...` and writes hook commands that assume those paths.
- `cli/src/utils/worktree-session.ts` symlinks `.claude/skills` into worktrees.

## What to build
- Update install/init/bootstrap flows to provision the new skills tree, initialize `state.json`, and rebuild active views instead of assuming `default/` is the runtime root.
- Migrate service-skill delivery so the trinity skills live as flattened default skills and runtime hook commands continue to resolve through `.claude/skills` active symlinks.
- Update worktree/runtime compatibility paths so generated active Claude skills are available inside worktree sessions.
- Deprecate `.agents/skills` in code paths, docs/messages, and verification logic without making it part of the new runtime contract.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Fresh install/init flows leave `.claude/skills` and `.pi/settings.json.skills` pointed at active runtime views.
- [ ] Service-skill scripts invoked from Claude hooks still resolve correctly after flattening.
- [ ] Worktree Claude sessions continue to see the generated skills view.
- [ ] Legacy `.agents/skills` handling is removed from authoritative paths or clearly marked deprecated-only.
- [ ] Any verification/status output reflects the new active-view architecture.

## Approach notes
- Watch for code paths in init/install/status/cleanup that still special-case `.agents/skills` or `skills/default`.
- Boundary + shell integration risk is medium even though the command-layer GitNexus blast radius is LOW.

title: Test: tiered skills model and discovery invariants — unit + property tests
description:
## What implementation it covers
Covers: implementation tasks for tier model/discovery and rebuild invariants.
Primary target: the task that defines `state.json`, tier discovery, direct-child skill detection, and collision/invariant logic.

## Layer classification
Layer: core
Why: the risky pieces here are deterministic parsing, validation, pack-name normalization, collision detection, and invariant enforcement independent of runtime I/O.

## Strategy chosen
Strategy: unit + property-based tests.
Use example-based tests for concrete layouts and property/invariant checks for determinism (same inputs => same ordered active set, no duplicate names allowed).

## Test file structure
- `cli/test/skills-state.test.ts`
- `cli/test/skills-discovery.test.ts`

## What to assert
Layer: core
Strategy: unit | property-based
Covers: <impl issue IDs filled during execution>

Assertions required:
- [ ] Positive path: valid default/optional/user trees produce the expected discovered skill + pack model.
- [ ] Negative path: invalid pack metadata, nested runtime roots, and duplicate skill names fail with clear errors.
- [ ] Edge cases explicitly enumerated: empty optional/user tiers, `all`, missing `pack.json`, non-skill child dirs, case-sensitive collisions.
- [ ] Invariants/properties included: deterministic ordering, default skills always present, filesystem authoritative for skill membership.

Fixture policy:
- [ ] No shared mutable state between tests
- [ ] Deterministic fixtures (temp dirs only)
- [ ] Each test constructs its own input independently

Critical paths covered:
- state parsing + enabled-pack resolution — risk: wrong runtime state activates the wrong skills
- collision detection before rebuild — risk: duplicate names leak into active views and recreate Pi discovery bugs

Known deferred paths (with follow-up refs):
- Windows symlink semantics for generated active dirs → follow-up only if platform-specific failures appear during implementation

Done when: all assertions above are implemented and passing in CI.

title: Define tiered skills model and flatten default skill inventory
description:
## Context
The current skills surface is split across `.xtrm/skills/default`, real `.claude/skills/` content, `.agents/skills`, and Pi pointing directly at `.xtrm/skills/default`. The finalized design replaces this with tiered storage plus runtime-specific active views, and it must also flatten the `service-skills-set` payload into direct children under `default/` to avoid nested runtime roots and fix Pi duplicate discovery (`xtrm-0fbj`).

Relevant exploration:
- `cli/src/commands/skills.ts` currently only lists default skills and has stubbed `enable` / `disable` handlers.
- `cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts` and `cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts` assume a single `skills/default` tree and symlink `.claude/skills` + `.agents/skills` to it.
- `.xtrm/skills/default/service-skills-set/` still packages nested `.claude/skills/...` content that must be flattened.

## What to build
- Define the tier/runtime state model around:
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/<skill>/`
  - `.xtrm/skills/optional/<pack>/<skill>/`
  - `.xtrm/skills/user/packs/<pack>/<skill>/`
  - `.xtrm/skills/active/{claude,pi}/`
  - `.xtrm/skills/state.json`
- Add helpers for pack discovery, `state.json` read/write/default initialization, pack metadata validation, and direct-child skill detection (`SKILL.md` as authoritative signal).
- Flatten `service-skills-set` into concrete default skills so there are no nested runtime roots inside any pack/skill tree.
- Capture and document collision/invariant checks the rebuild layer will enforce.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A single canonical state model exists for runtime enablement (`schemaVersion: '1'`, per-runtime `enabledPacks`).
- [ ] Skill discovery treats only direct child dirs containing `SKILL.md` as skills.
- [ ] `service-skills-set` no longer requires nested `.claude/skills/` content inside the skills tree.
- [ ] Pack metadata is validated, but the filesystem remains authoritative for the skill list.
- [ ] Invariants from the finalized design are encoded for downstream rebuild logic.

## Approach notes
- Keep this as the foundation for later runtime materialization and CLI work.
- Follow existing repo patterns in `cli/src/core/*` for small pure helpers + fs utilities.
- GitNexus fallback CLI exploration used (index is stale): `createSkillsCommand` has LOW upstream risk and only direct registration in `cli/src/index.ts`.

title: Build runtime active-view materializer for Claude and Pi
description:
## Context
Today Pi points at `../.xtrm/skills/default`, `.claude/skills` may be a real directory, and `ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink()` still wires both `.agents/skills` and `.claude/skills` to `default/`. The new architecture requires deterministic, atomic materialization into `.xtrm/skills/active/{claude,pi}` and runtime pointers that target those active views.

Relevant exploration:
- `cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts:updatePiSettings()` currently hard-codes `../.xtrm/skills/default`.
- `cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts` currently symlinks `.claude/skills` and `.agents/skills` to `../.xtrm/skills/default`.
- `cli/src/utils/worktree-session.ts` mirrors `.claude/skills` into worktrees, so generated active views and symlink shape must remain worktree-safe.

## What to build
- Implement the active rebuild algorithm for each runtime:
  1. read `state.json`
  2. collect valid default skills
  3. collect enabled pack skills
  4. detect collisions and fail fast
  5. populate a sorted temp dir with symlinks
  6. atomically swap into `active/<runtime>`
  7. ensure runtime pointers (`.claude/skills`, `.pi/settings.json.skills`) target the active view
- Replace the old `.agents/skills` assumption with the new generated runtime model, leaving `.agents/skills` deprecated rather than authoritative.
- Make rebuilds deterministic, idempotent, and safe across reruns.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `active/claude` and `active/pi` are fully generated materialized views, never hand-edited.
- [ ] Rebuilds fail fast on duplicate skill names within a runtime view.
- [ ] `.claude/skills` points to `../.xtrm/skills/active/claude`.
- [ ] `.pi/settings.json.skills` points to `../.xtrm/skills/active/pi` while preserving unrelated settings/packages.
- [ ] Rebuild execution is atomic and idempotent across repeated runs.

## Approach notes
- Preserve existing atomic/config-writing patterns already used in runtime sync code.
- Treat filesystem writes here as boundary-layer work; tempdir + swap behavior should be explicitly tested.
- GitNexus context shows LOW upstream risk around `resolveScopeRoot` / `createSkillsCommand`, but this task also touches `pi-runtime.ts`, `skills-scaffold.ts`, and worktree session wiring.
notes:
Implemented ✅ runtime active-view materialization for Claude + Pi, with atomic rebuilds and runtime pointer rewiring.

### What I changed

#### 1) Added runtime materializer
**File:** `cli/src/core/skills-materializer.ts`

Implemented:
- `selectRuntimeSkills(runtime, skillsRoot)`  
  - reads `state.json`
  - collects default skills + enabled pack skills
  - validates enabled packs exist
  - sorts deterministically
  - fails fast on duplicate skill names within a runtime view
- `rebuildRuntimeActiveView(runtime, skillsRoot)`  
  - builds sorted temp dir of symlinks
  - atomically swaps into `.xtrm/skills/active/<runtime>`
- `rebuildAllRuntimeActiveViews(skillsRoot)`

Atomic behavior:
- temp dir creation
- rename swap with backup rollback path
- cleanup of temp/backup on success/failure

---

#### 2) Rewired skills scaffold to generated runtime model
**File:** `cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts`

`ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink(projectRoot)` now:
- validates invariants via `validateSkillsInvariants`
- rebuilds active runtime views (`claude`, `pi`)
- ensures `.claude/skills -> ../.xtrm/skills/active/claude`
- treats `.agents/skills` as deprecated (logs if present, not authoritative)

Also changed symlink behavior:
- if `.claude/skills` is a real directory/path, it is now replaced with managed symlink (required by new model)

---

#### 3) Pi settings now point to active Pi view
**File:** `cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts`

Updated `updatePiSettings()`:
- `skills` entry changed from:
  - `../.xtrm/skills/default`
  to
  - `../.xtrm/skills/active/pi`
- preserves unrelated settings + packages as before

Also in project-scoped Pi runtime sync:
- validates invariants
- rebuilds `active/pi` before writing settings (when skills tree exists)

---

#### 4) Worktree Claude wiring now uses worktree-local active view
**File:** `cli/src/utils/worktree-session.ts`

Changed Claude worktree setup:
- `.claude/skills` now points to `../.xtrm/skills/active/claude` inside the worktree
- replaces stale/incorrect existing path when needed
- keeps worktree behavior deterministic/safe for generated runtime view model

---

#### 5) Updated/added tests
**Files:**
- `cli/src/tests/skills-materializer.test.ts` (new)
- `cli/src/tests/registry-scaffold.test.ts` (updated)
- `cli/src/tests/install-integration.test.ts` (updated)

Coverage includes:
- materialization of default + enabled pack skills
- collision fail-fast on duplicate skill names
- idempotent reruns
- `.claude/skills` pointer to `active/claude`
- `.agents/skills` no longer treated as required authoritative target

---

### Validation run

Passed:
```bash
cd cli && npm run test -- src/tests/skills-materializer.test.ts src/tests/registry-scaffold.test.ts src/tests/install-integration.test.ts
```

Typecheck status:
- `cd cli && npm run typecheck` still fails due pre-existing unrelated TS issues in other modules (not introduced by this task).

title: Design: skills tier architecture — default/optional/user packs + dual-runtime enable/disable
description: Redesign .xtrm/skills/ into three tiers: default (always-on flat), optional (xtrm-shipped packs, opt-in), user/packs (user-created). Skills from service-skills-set get flattened into default/. Enable/disable controls both .pi/settings.json skills array AND .claude/skills symlinks. New flags: --claude, --pi (target one runtime), default = both. New command: xt skills create-pack <name>. xtrm-0fbj (Pi duplicate discovery) is solved by flattening + no nested .claude/skills/ inside packs.
notes:
DESIGN FINALIZED by overthinker f77623.

## Final Architecture

Storage:
  .xtrm/skills/default/<skill>/           always-on
  .xtrm/skills/optional/<pack>/<skill>/   xtrm-shipped packs (flat)
  .xtrm/skills/user/packs/<pack>/<skill>/ user-created packs (flat)
  .xtrm/skills/active/claude/             materialized view (symlinks)
  .xtrm/skills/active/pi/                 materialized view (symlinks)
  .xtrm/skills/state.json                 canonical desired state

Runtime wiring:
  .claude/skills -> ../.xtrm/skills/active/claude
  .pi/settings.json.skills = ['../.xtrm/skills/active/pi']
  .agents/skills = DEPRECATED

state.json schema:
  { schemaVersion:'1', runtimes: { claude: { enabledPacks: ['optional:X','user:Y'] }, pi: { enabledPacks: [...] } } }

pack.json schema (required: schemaVersion, name, description, tier):
  { schemaVersion, name, title, description, tier, owner, version, createdBy, createdAt }
  filesystem is authoritative for skill list (pack.json is metadata only)

CLI contracts:
  xt skills list [--claude|--pi] [--json]
  xt skills enable <pack>|all [--claude|--pi]
  xt skills disable <pack>|all [--claude|--pi]
  xt skills create-pack <name>

Active rebuild algorithm:
  1. read state.json for runtime
  2. collect valid default skills (child dirs with SKILL.md)
  3. collect enabled pack skills (child dirs with SKILL.md, ignore pack.json/README)
  4. detect name collisions -> fail fast
  5. create fresh temp dir, populate sorted symlinks
  6. atomically swap into active/<runtime>
  7. ensure runtime pointer (claude symlink, pi settings)

Invariants:
  1. No nested runtime roots inside any skill/pack tree
  2. Skill = direct child dir containing SKILL.md
  3. Default skills always-on, cannot be disabled
  4. Skill names globally unique within each runtime active view
  5. active/claude + active/pi are generated only (never hand-edited)
  6. Rebuilds deterministic, idempotent, atomic
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Test: install + init decoupled behavior — integration tests

## Context

Strategy: integration tests (real file system in temp dir, subprocess-level for CLI; module-level for orchestrator)

title: Test: core/registry-scaffold.ts and core/skills-scaffold.ts — unit tests
description:
## Context

Covers: xtrm-own9.1, xtrm-own9.2

Layer: core
Strategy: unit tests + example-based (file I/O with temp dirs, no external services)

These core modules contain pure helpers (hash comparison, path normalization, symlink management) that should be tested independently of the CLI command that uses them.

## Test file

`cli/src/tests/registry-scaffold.test.ts`

## What to assert

**`toPosix()`**:
- [ ] Windows-style paths converted to posix separators
- [ ] Already-posix paths returned unchanged

**`stripXtrmPrefix()`**:
- [ ] `.xtrm/foo/bar` → `foo/bar`
- [ ] `.xtrm/` prefix stripped correctly
- [ ] Path without prefix returned unchanged

**`toUserRelativePath()`**:
- [ ] Combines sourceDir + filePath correctly via posix join
- [ ] Strips `.xtrm/` prefix from sourceDir before joining

**`isSkillsDefaultPath()`**:
- [ ] `skills/default/...` returns true
- [ ] `hooks/...` returns false
- [ ] `config/...` returns false

**`scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage()`** (temp dir tests):
- [ ] Returns 'symlink' when targetDir does not exist (happy path)
- [ ] Returns 'noop' when targetDir already exists
- [ ] Returns 'noop' in dry-run mode

**`ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink()`** (temp dir tests):
- [ ] Creates `.agents/skills` → `.xtrm/skills/default` symlink when `.xtrm/skills/default` exists
- [ ] Creates `.claude/skills` → `.xtrm/skills/default` symlink when `.xtrm/skills/default` exists
- [ ] Skips silently when `.xtrm/skills/default` does not exist
- [ ] Is idempotent: second call with correct symlink already in place logs "already in place" and skips
- [ ] Does NOT overwrite an existing real directory (warns + skips)

## Critical paths covered

- `isSkillsDefaultPath` controls drift-skip logic in `installFromRegistry` — wrong classification silently skips drift reporting
- `ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink` idempotency — re-running `xtrm install` must not break existing symlinks
- `scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage` noop-on-exist — prevents overwriting user-customized skills

## Known deferred paths

- `installFromRegistry` full integration (drift detection, hash comparison, force flag) → covered by shell layer test xtrm-own9.6b (existing install-integration.test.ts)
- Symlink creation failure (EPERM) → P4 follow-up

## Fixture policy

- [ ] Each test uses its own `fs.mkdtemp` temp dir, cleaned up in afterEach
- [ ] No shared mutable state between tests
- [ ] No real git repo needed — tests use standalone temp dirs

## Done when

All unit assertions above are implemented and passing. Tests run with `pnpm test` in `cli/`.

title: Update init.ts to call core scaffold modules directly — remove runInstall delegation
description:
## Context

The core coupling problem: `init.ts` currently imports and calls `runInstall({ skipMachineBootstrap: true, skipClaudeRuntimeSync: true })` in Phase 6. This one-way delegation means:
- New logic added to `init` (e.g., ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink) is invisible to standalone `install`
- New logic added to `install` silently lands in `init` via delegation
- The skip flags exist only to avoid double-running phases that `init` already handles

This task replaces the delegation with direct calls to core modules, giving `init` full ownership of each scaffold phase.

Depends on: xtrm-own9.1, xtrm-own9.2, xtrm-own9.3

## What to build

In `cli/src/commands/init.ts`:

**Phase 6 replacement** — replace the `runInstall(...)` call with direct calls:
```typescript
// Phase 6: Registry scaffold (.xtrm files copy)
const registryPath = path.join(packageRoot, '.xtrm', 'registry.json');
const registry = await fs.readJson(registryPath) as RegistryManifest;
await installFromRegistry({ packageRoot, registry, userXtrmDir, dryRun: false, force: false, yes: true });
await scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage({ packageRoot, userXtrmDir, dryRun: false });

// Phase 6b: Skills symlinks (must run before gitnexus init)
await ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink(projectRoot);
```

**Phase 6 Pi runtime** — call `runPiInstall` directly (it's already importable from `./pi-install.ts`)

**Imports to add**: `installFromRegistry`, `scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage`, `RegistryManifest` from `core/registry-scaffold.ts`; `ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink` from `core/skills-scaffold.ts`

**Remove**: `import { runInstall }` from `./install.js` — init no longer calls install

Update `init-phases.test.ts` to reflect the new wiring:
- Remove the `vi.mock('../src/commands/install.js', ...)` mock
- The test for phase ordering now verifies direct core module calls rather than `runInstall` delegation
- Mock `core/registry-scaffold.ts` and `core/skills-scaffold.ts` instead

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `init.ts` does NOT import `runInstall` from `install.ts`
- [ ] Phase 6 calls `installFromRegistry` and `scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage` from `core/registry-scaffold.ts` directly
- [ ] Phase 6b calls `ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink` from `core/skills-scaffold.ts` directly
- [ ] `init-phases.test.ts` updated and passing (no mock of `runInstall`)
- [ ] Full `xtrm init --yes` run works end-to-end in a temp project
- [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly
- [ ] `skipMachineBootstrap` / `skipClaudeRuntimeSync` flags are no longer passed anywhere (removed from internal call sites)

## Approach notes

The `userXtrmDir` in init is obtained from `getContext({ createMissingDirs: true, projectRoot })` — same pattern as install. Re-use this.
`packageRoot` in init currently comes from `resolvePkgRoot()` (module-level const `PKG_ROOT`) — re-use it.
`init-phases.test.ts` test "runs machine bootstrap before runtime sync" will need to be updated to assert direct scaffold calls instead of `mocked.runInstall`
notes:

- Removed `runInstall` delegation:
  - ❌ `import { runInstall } from './install.js'`
- Kept only type import from install:

- Added direct phase dependencies:

  - `runPluginEraCleanup` from `../core/plugin-era-cleanup.js`

title: Extract registry scaffold helpers into core/registry-scaffold.ts
description:
## Context

This is the foundation task for decoupling install/init. Currently, `installFromRegistry`, `scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage`, and all supporting types/helpers live directly in `cli/src/commands/install.ts`. Extracting them into a shared core module allows both `install` and `init` to call them directly without one delegating to the other.

This task unblocks T2, T3, T4.

## What to build

- Type definitions: `RegistryFileEntry`, `RegistryAsset`, `RegistryManifest`, `InstallStats`
- Pure helpers: `toPosix()`, `stripXtrmPrefix()`, `toUserRelativePath()`, `isSkillsDefaultPath()`, `hashFile()`, `buildExpectedHashes()`
- Main functions: `installFromRegistry()`, `scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage()`
- Helper: `resolvePackageRoot()` (currently duplicated between install.ts and init.ts — consolidate here)

All extracted functions should be exported. No behavior changes — just relocation.

- [ ] `install.ts` and `init.ts` can import from it (no circular deps)
- [ ] No behavior changes — functions identical to originals
- [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit`)
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

## Approach notes

Source: `cli/src/commands/install.ts` lines covering all functions before `runInstall()`
Pattern: follow existing core modules like `cli/src/core/claude-runtime-sync.ts` — pure functions, no Commander imports
`resolvePackageRoot` is currently duplicated in both install.ts (named `resolvePackageRoot`) and init.ts (named `resolvePkgRoot`) — merge into one canonical export in the new core module

title: Epic: decouple install/init — shared scaffold core
description: install and init are coupled via runInstall() being called inside runProjectInit(). This creates one-way dependency drift: new logic added to init (e.g. ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink) is invisible to install. The fix: extract shared scaffold steps into core modules. install = scaffold layer (registry copy + Claude hooks + Pi). init = scaffold + project bootstrap. Both call shared core, neither calls the other. install can stay as the machine/tooling setup command; init is the full project onboarding. No monolith — just clean separation with shared core.
notes:
CONTEXT FOR PLANNER:

Key files:
- cli/src/commands/install.ts — contains runInstall(), installFromRegistry(), scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage(), registry helpers. Currently called INSIDE init as Phase 6.
- cli/src/commands/init.ts — runProjectInit() calls runInstall() with skipMachineBootstrap+skipClaudeRuntimeSync flags. Phase 6b ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink() is init-only.

The problem: one-way coupling. init calls install. Logic added to init (ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink) is invisible when running install standalone. Logic added to install lands in init via delegation.

Desired architecture:
- install = scaffold layer: registry copy (.xtrm files) + Claude hooks wiring + Pi runtime sync + ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink. Standalone: run on existing projects to refresh tooling.
- init = install (scaffold) + project bootstrap (bd init, gitnexus, instruction headers). init calls shared core modules — NOT runInstall().
- Shared core: extract installFromRegistry (+ helpers) into core/registry-scaffold.ts. Both install and init call it directly.
- No skipMachineBootstrap/skipClaudeRuntimeSync flags needed once init owns the phases directly.

Constraints: 
- Do not break existing install command behavior
- Keep install deprecated label but make it functional as standalone scaffold
- ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink must run in BOTH install and init
- All existing tests must pass

title: Fix ensureCorePackageSymlink: use lstat to detect existing broken symlink
description: ensureCorePackageSymlink uses fs.pathExists which follows symlinks. If the symlink exists but points at a stale/non-existent target, pathExists returns false and we attempt to create a duplicate symlink, hitting EEXIST. Fix: use fs.lstat which detects the symlink file itself regardless of target validity.
close_reason: Fixed: use fs.lstat instead of fs.pathExists to detect existing symlink regardless of target validity

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information about skills management. Let me compile a detailed analysis:

## ✅ Skills Management Architecture Analysis

### Summary
The xtrm-tools project implements a **two-tier skills distribution system**: (1) **source skills** in `/skills/` directory for development; (2) **distributed skills** in `.xtrm/skills/default/` that are installed via npm package registry. All 10 mandatory skills are present and correctly configured. GitNexus skills (including `gitnexus-debugging`) come from the npm `pi-gitnexus` package.

---

## Architecture Overview

### Directory Structure & Installation Flow

```
xtrm-tools/
├── skills/                          ← Source skills (dev)
│   ├── sync-docs/
│   ├── planning/
│   ├── prompt-improving/
│   ├── using-quality-gates/
│   ├── skill-creator/
│   ├── test-planning/
│   ├── xt-debugging/
│   ├── xt-end/
│   ├── xt-merge/
│   ├── gitnexus-*/ (exploring, impact-analysis, refactoring, PR-review)
│   └── ... (optional skills)
│
├── project-skills/
│   ├── quality-gates/
│   └── service-skills-set/
│
├── .xtrm/
│   ├── skills/
│   │   └── default/               ← Installed skills (from npm package)
│   │       ├── sync-docs/         (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── planning/          (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── prompt-improving/  (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── using-quality-gates/ (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── skill-creator/     (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── test-planning/     (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── service-skills-set/ (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── xt-debugging/      (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── xt-end/            (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── xt-merge/          (MANDATORY)
│   │       ├── gitnexus-*/ (npm:pi-gitnexus)
│   │       └── ... (optional skills)
│   └── registry.json               ← Asset manifest
│
├── .agents/
│   └── skills/  → ../.xtrm/skills/default/ (symlink)
│
└── .pi/
    └── settings.json
        ├── skills: [ "../.xtrm/skills/default" ]
        └── packages: [ "npm:pi-gitnexus", ... ]
```

---

## Installation Mechanism

### 1. **init.ts → ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink()**
Creates the Pi compatibility symlink:
```typescript
const agentsSkillsLink = path.join(agentsDir, 'skills');
const symlinkTarget = path.join('..', '.xtrm', 'skills', 'default');
// Creates: .agents/skills → ../.xtrm/skills/default
```

### 2. **install.ts → scaffoldSkillsDefaultFromPackage()**
Seeds `.xtrm/skills/default/` during `xtrm install`:
```typescript
// Attempts symlink first, falls back to copy:
await fs.ensureSymlink(sourceDir, targetDir);  // package root → user project
```

### 3. **install.ts → installFromRegistry()**
Syncs all files listed in `.xtrm/registry.json`:
- Source: package root `.xtrm/skills/default/`
- Target: user project `.xtrm/skills/default/` (or symlink)
- Mode: `copy` (per registry config) — preserves local drifts unless `--force`

### 4. **.pi/settings.json Configuration**
```json
{
  "skills": ["../. xtrm/skills/default"],
  "packages": [
    "npm:pi-gitnexus",            // GitNexus skills
    "npm:pi-serena-tools",
    "npm:lsp-pi",
    "npm:@zenobius/pi-worktrees",
    "npm:@robhowley/pi-structured-return"
  ]
}
```

**Skills path resolution:**
- `../. xtrm/skills/default` → project-local `.xtrm/skills/default/`
- NPM packages → installed into `.pi/npm/node_modules/` (e.g., `pi-gitnexus/.claude/skills/gitnexus/`)

---

### 🔗 EXTERNAL SKILLS (from npm packages)

Installed via `.pi/settings.json` **packages** list:

| `npm:pi-gitnexus` | gitnexus-debugging, gitnexus-exploring*, gitnexus-impact-analysis*, gitnexus-refactoring*, gitnexus-pr-review | ✓ |

*Duplicated in both `.xtrm/skills/default/` (local) and npm package (NPM versions take precedence).

---

## ⚠️ Findings & Status

### ✅ No Issues Found

1. **All mandatory skills are present** in `.xtrm/registry.json`
2. **Symlink structure is correct:**
   - `.agents/skills/` → `../.xtrm/skills/default/` (Pi auto-discovery)
   - `.pi/settings.json` references `../. xtrm/skills/default` (correct relative path)
3. **Installation gates are functional:**
   - `ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink()` runs in Phase 7 (Project Bootstrap) of `init.ts`
   - `installFromRegistry()` syncs from `.xtrm/registry.json` manifest
4. **GitNexus skills are properly sourced:**
   - `gitnexus-debugging`, `gitnexus-exploring`, `gitnexus-impact-analysis`, `gitnexus-refactoring` come from `npm:pi-gitnexus`
   - Local copies in `.xtrm/skills/default/` serve as fallback/reference
5. **Drift handling is conservative:**
   - Skills modified locally are preserved by default (unless `--force`)
   - Only non-skill drifts block installation

### 📋 cli/src/commands/skills.ts Status

The CLI skill management is **functional but incomplete:**
- `xt skills list` — ✓ Works (shows installed skills)
- `xt skills enable <pack>` — ⚠️ Stub (not implemented in v0.8)
- `xt skills disable <pack>` — ⚠️ Stub (not implemented in v0.8)

**Note:** Skill pack management is deferred to v0.9.

---

## Summary Table

| Aspect | Status | Details |
|--------|--------|---------|
| **Mandatory Skills Count** | ✅ 10/10 | All present in registry |
| **Symlink Chain** | ✅ Correct | `.agents/skills` → `.xtrm/skills/default/` |
| **Pi Settings Resolution** | ✅ Correct | `../. xtrm/skills/default` (relative path) |
| **Installation Mechanism** | ✅ Functional | Registry-driven sync with drift detection |
| **GitNexus Skills** | ✅ Sourced | From `npm:pi-gitnexus` package |
| **Missing Skills** | ✅ None | All expected skills present |
| **Miscategorization** | ✅ None | Classifications align with reality |
| **CLI Completeness** | ⚠️ Partial | Pack management deferred to v0.9 |

---

## Conclusion

**xtrm-tools implements a robust, dual-source skills system:**
1. **Local distribution** (`.xtrm/skills/default/`) for mandatory + optional project skills
2. **npm packages** (`.pi/settings.json`) for specialized/shared skills (GitNexus, Serena, LSP)
3. **All 10 mandatory skills are correctly installed, discoverable, and validated**
4. **No missing or miscategorized skills detected**

The system is production-ready and well-architected for skill extensibility.

title: Explore: xt init end-to-end flow validation
description: Map the complete xt init flow: what phases run, what gets wired, how .pi/settings.json is generated, how hooks land in .claude/settings.json, how .xtrm/ is scaffolded, how machine bootstrap works. Produce a phase-by-phase trace with gaps or mismatches flagged.
notes:
Perfect! I now have comprehensive visibility into the entire xt init flow. Here's the **phase-by-phase trace with gap analysis**:

---

## **XT INIT END-TO-END FLOW VALIDATION**

### **Executive Summary**

The `xt init` flow orchestrates **9 sequential phases** from `cli/src/commands/init.ts::runProjectInit()`. The flow is well-structured, modular, and properly implements a **read-only inventory → plan → single confirmation gate → parallel execution → verification** pattern. Hooks are correctly wired from `.xtrm/config/hooks.json` into both project and global `.claude/settings.json`. 

**No critical gaps detected.** Minor observations about cross-phase consistency noted below.

---

## **PHASE BREAKDOWN**

### **Phase 1: Preflight / Inventory (Read-Only)**
**File:** `cli/src/commands/init.ts::runPreflight()`  
**Mutation Level:** None (read-only system state)

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Input** | projectRoot, InstallOpts |
| **Outputs** | `InitInventory` (bootstrapPlan, skillsChanges, needsBdInit, needsGitNexus, projectTypes) |
| **What It Reads** | System PATH (bd/dolt/bv/pi/pnpm/gitnexus), .beads/, gitnexus status, tsconfig.json, pyproject.toml, docker-compose.yml |
| **What It Writes** | Nothing |
| **Key Functions Called** | `inventoryDeps()`, `calculateDiff()`, `detectProjectFeatures()`, `gitnexus status` |

**Expected State:**
- `bootstrapPlan`: Lists all managed deps (bd, dolt, bv, pi, pnpm, gitnexus, deepwiki) with installed/missing status
- `skillsChanges`: Count of missing/outdated files in `.xtrm/skills/default`
- `needsBdInit`: Boolean (checks `.beads/` exists)
- `needsGitNexus`: Boolean (checks gitnexus status for stale/missing index)
- `projectTypes`: Array of ["TypeScript", "Python", "Docker"] based on detected config files

✅ **Status:** Correct. Properly inventory-only.

---

### **Phase 2: Plan Rendering**
**File:** `cli/src/commands/init.ts::renderInitPlan()`  
**Mutation Level:** None (console output only)

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Input** | `InitInventory` from Phase 1 |
| **Output** | Formatted console output |
| **Rendering Sections** | Machine bootstrap, Claude runtime, Pi runtime, Project bootstrap, Verification |
| **What's Shown** | Summary of each phase's pending changes |

**Expected Output:**
```
  xtrm init — Installation Plan
  ─────────────────────────────
  Machine Bootstrap
    ✓ beads
    + dolt (will install)
    ...
  Claude Runtime Sync
    • read canonical hooks: .xtrm/config/hooks.json
    • resolve project hooks dir: <project>/.xtrm/hooks
    ...
  Pi Runtime
    ↻ extensions + packages sync
  Skills
    ↑ N changes pending
  Project Bootstrap
    • bd init
    • gitnexus analyze
    • AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md
  Verification
    ✓ unified summary after execution
```

✅ **Status:** Correct. Shows all phases that will execute.

---

### **Phase 3: Confirmation Gate**
**File:** `cli/src/commands/init.ts::confirmInitPlan()`  
**Mutation Level:** None (user confirmation only)

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Input** | `yes` flag (can be overridden with `--yes` or `-y`) |
| **Output** | Boolean (proceed or cancel) |
| **Behavior** | Single gate before ANY mutations occur. If user cancels, execution stops here. |

**Expected State:**
- If `yes=true` or user confirms: proceeds to Phase 4
- If cancelled: prints "Init cancelled.\n" and returns

✅ **Status:** Correct. Single point of no-return.

---

### **Phase 4: Machine Bootstrap**
**File:** `cli/src/core/machine-bootstrap.ts::runMachineBootstrapPhase()`  
**Mutation Level:** HIGH (installs system tools)

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Registry** | `MANAGED_DEPS` array (bd, dolt, bv, pi, pnpm, gitnexus, deepwiki) |
| **Installation Logic** | Uses `spawnSync` to run install commands per platform (macOS uses brew, others use npm/curl) |
| **What Gets Installed** | System CLIs required by workflow gates and runtime |
| **Required vs Recommended** | Required: bd, dolt, bv, pi, pnpm. Recommended: gitnexus, deepwiki |
| **Official Plugins** | Also installs Claude plugins (serena, context7) via `claude plugin install` |

**Expected Final State:**
```
$ bd --version        # Works
$ dolt version        # Works
$ bv --version        # Works
$ pi --version        # Works
$ pnpm --version      # Works
$ gitnexus --version  # Works (recommended, may fail gracefully)
```

✅ **Status:** Correct. Well-modularized with platform detection.

---

### **Phase 5: Claude Runtime Sync (Hooks Wiring)**
**File:** `cli/src/core/claude-runtime-sync.ts::runClaudeRuntimeSyncPhase()`  
**Mutation Level:** HIGH (modifies `.claude/settings.json`)

**Flow:**
1. Read canonical hooks from `.xtrm/config/hooks.json`
2. Resolve absolute paths to `.xtrm/hooks/<script>`
3. Generate hook entries (command hooks wrapped with matcher + timeout)
4. Read existing `.claude/settings.json` (if exists)
5. Merge hooks into settings, preserving existing permissions/model/skillSuggestions
6. Write merged settings

**Key Functions:**
- `generateHooks()`: Converts canonical hook defs (script name) → command hooks (absolute path)
- `buildScriptCommand()`: Routes to correct interpreter (node, bash, python3) based on extension
- `deepMergeHooks()`: Intelligently merges incoming hooks with existing, deduplicating by script filename

**Canonical Source (`.xtrm/config/hooks.json`):**
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      { "script": "using-xtrm-reminder.mjs" },
      { "script": "beads-compact-restore.mjs", "timeout": 5000 }
    ],
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Edit|Write|...",
        "script": "worktree-boundary.mjs",
        "timeout": 2000
      }
    ],
    ...
  }
}
```

**Generated Output in `.claude/settings.json`:**
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      {
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "node \"/absolute/path/.xtrm/hooks/using-xtrm-reminder.mjs\""
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Edit|Write|...",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "node \"/absolute/path/.xtrm/hooks/worktree-boundary.mjs\"",
            "timeout": 2000
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Hooks Events Wired (6 total):**
- `SessionStart` (4 hooks: reminder, compact-restore, quality-check-env, session-logger)
- `PreToolUse` (3 hooks: worktree-boundary, beads-edit-gate, beads-commit-gate)
- `PostToolUse` (5 hooks: beads-claim-sync, quality-check.cjs, quality-check.py, gitnexus, tool-logger)
- `Stop` (2 hooks: stop-gate, memory-gate)
- `PreCompact` (1 hook: compact-save)

**Settings Path Resolution:**
- Global mode (`--global`): `~/.claude/settings.json`
- Project mode (default): `<project>/.claude/settings.json`

**Preservation Logic:**
- ✅ Existing `permissions.allow` preserved
- ✅ Existing `model` preserved
- ✅ Existing `skillSuggestions.enabled` preserved
- ⚠️ **HOOKS SECTION FULLY REPLACED** (not merged from file, but merged per-event from incoming)

✅ **Status:** Correct. Hooks properly wired with absolute paths, matchers preserved, interpreters auto-detected.

---

### **Phase 6: Pi Runtime Sync (Extensions + Packages)**
**File:** `cli/src/commands/install.ts::runInstall()`  
**Related:** `cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts`  
**Mutation Level:** HIGH (modifies `.pi/extensions`, installs packages)

**Subphase 6a: Extension Inventory & Sync**

**Managed Extensions Registry:**
```typescript
const MANAGED_EXTENSIONS: ManagedExtension[] = [
  { id: 'core', displayName: '@xtrm/pi-core', isLibrary: true, required: true },
  { id: 'beads', displayName: 'beads', required: true },
  { id: 'quality-gates', displayName: 'quality-gates', required: true },
  { id: 'session-flow', displayName: 'session-flow', required: true },
  { id: 'xtrm-loader', displayName: 'xtrm-loader', required: true },
  { id: 'xtrm-ui', displayName: 'xtrm-ui', required: true },
  { id: 'custom-footer', displayName: 'custom-footer', required: true },
  { id: 'auto-session-name', displayName: 'auto-session-name', required: false },
  { id: 'git-checkpoint', displayName: 'git-checkpoint', required: false },
  { id: 'lsp-bootstrap', displayName: 'lsp-bootstrap', required: false },
  { id: 'pi-serena-compact', displayName: 'pi-serena-compact', required: false },
  { id: 'service-skills', displayName: 'service-skills', required: false },
  { id: 'compact-header', displayName: 'compact-header', required: false },
  { id: 'auto-update', displayName: 'auto-update', required: false },
  { id: 'custom-provider-qwen-cli', displayName: 'custom-provider-qwen-cli', required: false },
];
```

**Source Dir:** `.xtrm/extensions/`  
**Target Dir:** `.pi/extensions/`

**Installation Mode:** Symlink (if possible) → fallback to copy  
**Stale Detection:** If target is real copy (not symlink) or symlink points wrong place, replace with correct symlink

**Expected State After Phase 6a:**
```
.pi/extensions/
  core -> ../../.xtrm/extensions/core
  beads -> ../../.xtrm/extensions/beads
  quality-gates -> ../../.xtrm/extensions/quality-gates
  xtrm-ui -> ../../.xtrm/extensions/xtrm-ui
  ...
```

**Subphase 6b: Package Inventory & Sync**

**Managed Packages Registry:**
```typescript
const MANAGED_PACKAGES: ManagedPackage[] = [
  { id: 'npm:pi-gitnexus', displayName: 'pi-gitnexus', required: true },
  { id: 'npm:pi-serena-tools', displayName: 'pi-serena-tools', required: true },
  { id: 'npm:@zenobius/pi-worktrees', displayName: 'pi-worktrees', required: true },
  { id: 'npm:@robhowley/pi-structured-return', displayName: 'pi-structured-return', required: true },
  { id: 'npm:@aliou/pi-guardrails', displayName: 'pi-guardrails', required: false },
  { id: 'npm:@aliou/pi-processes', displayName: 'pi-processes', required: true },
];
```

**Installation:** Via `pi install <package-id>`  
**Scope:** Project-scoped (written to `.pi/npm/node_modules/`)

**Subphase 6c: Pi Settings Update**

**File:** `.pi/settings.json`

**Expected Content:**
```json
{
  "extensions": [
    "../.xtrm/extensions"
  ],
  "skills": [
    "../.xtrm/skills/default"
  ],
  "packages": [
    "npm:pi-gitnexus",
    "npm:pi-serena-tools",
    "npm:@zenobius/pi-worktrees",
    "npm:@robhowley/pi-structured-return"
  ]
}
```

✅ **Status:** Correct. Extensions properly symlinked, packages registered, settings.json created.

---

### **Phase 6b (Continued): Skills Sync**

**File:** Part of `runInstall()` via registry-based sync  
**Mutation Level:** MEDIUM (copies/synlinks skill files)

**Registry Source:** `.xtrm/registry.json` (asset: `.xtrm/skills/default`)  
**Target:** `<project>/.xtrm/skills/default`

**Installation Mode:** Copy (not symlink for skills — users may customize)  
**Drift Detection:** Compares file hashes; preserves local modifications unless `--force` used

**Registry Tracking:**
```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "assets": {
    "skills/default": {
      "source_dir": ".xtrm/skills/default",
      "install_mode": "copy",
      "files": {
        "clean-code/SKILL.md": { "hash": "...", "version": "0.7.0" },
        ...
      }
    }
  }
}
```

✅ **Status:** Correct. Drift-aware, preserves user customizations.

---

### **Phase 7: Project Bootstrap**
**File:** `cli/src/commands/init.ts::runProjectBootstrap()`  
**Mutation Level:** HIGH (project-level initialization)

**Subphase 7a: Beads Workspace Initialization**

```typescript
await runBdInitForProject(projectRoot);
```

**Spawns:** `bd init` in project directory  
**Output:** Creates `.beads/` directory with Dolt backend  
**Idempotent:** Checks for "already initialized" and skips if present

✅ **Status:** Correct.

**Subphase 7b: Instruction Headers**

```typescript
await injectProjectInstructionHeaders(projectRoot);
```

**Targets:** `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`  
**Source Templates:** `.xtrm/config/instructions/agents-top.md`, `claude-top.md`  
**Injection Logic:** 
- Wraps template in `<!-- xtrm:start -->` / `<!-- xtrm:end -->` markers
- Upserts block at beginning of file (or creates file)
- Preserves existing non-managed content

**Expected Result:**
```markdown
<!-- xtrm:start -->
[AGENTS workflow content]
<!-- xtrm:end -->

[Existing project-specific content, if any]
```

✅ **Status:** Correct. Managed block prevents drift.

**Subphase 7c: GitNexus Index Bootstrap**

```typescript
await runGitNexusInitForProject(projectRoot);
```

**Checks:** `gitnexus status`  
**Action:** If stale/missing, runs `gitnexus analyze` (timeout: 120s)

✅ **Status:** Correct. Only indexes if needed.

**Subphase 7d: Service Skills Hook Wiring**

```typescript
await installServiceSkillHooks(projectRoot);
```

**Current Implementation:**
```typescript
async function installServiceSkillHooks(_projectRoot: string): Promise<void> {
    // service-skills hooks are opt-in via `xt install service-skills`, not auto-wired on init.
}
```

⚠️ **Status:** Intentionally no-op. Service skills hooks are opt-in via separate command. This is by design.

**Subphase 7e: Pi Symlink Setup**

```typescript
await ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink(projectRoot);
```

**Purpose:** Link `.agents/skills → ../.xtrm/skills/default` for Pi compatibility  
**Logic:**
- Check if source exists: `.xtrm/skills/default/`
- Create symlink in `.agents/skills/` pointing to source
- If `.agents/skills/` exists as real directory, warn and skip
- If exists as wrong symlink, remove and recreate

**Expected Result:**
```
.agents/skills -> ../.xtrm/skills/default
```

✅ **Status:** Correct. Properly handles symlink vs directory distinction.

---

### **Phase 8: Verification**
**File:** `cli/src/core/init-verification.ts::runInitVerification()`  
**Mutation Level:** None (read-only check)

**Subphase 8a: Machine Bootstrap Verification**

Checks: bd, dolt, bv, pi, pnpm installed + required versions available

**Subphase 8b: Claude Runtime Verification**

- File exists: `.claude/settings.json` (project or global)
- Has hooks section: `settings.hooks` exists
- Has xtrm hooks: At least one hook command contains `.xtrm/hooks/`
- Counts events + commands

**Subphase 8c: Pi Runtime Verification**

- Extensions inventory via `listInstalledExtensions()`
- Packages inventory via `pi list` command
- Checks required extensions/packages installed

**Subphase 8d: Project Bootstrap Verification**

- Beads: Checks `.beads/` exists
- GitNexus: Runs `gitnexus status`, checks not stale/missing
- Headers: Checks `AGENTS.md` or `CLAUDE.md` exists

**Expected Result:**
```typescript
interface VerificationResult {
  machineBootstrap: {
    allRequiredPresent: boolean;
    missingRequired: string[];
  };
  claudeRuntime: {
    hooksWired: boolean;
    hooksEvents: number;
    hookCommands: number;
    settingsPath: string;
  };
  piRuntime: {
    allRequiredPresent: boolean;
    missingExtensions: string[];
    missingPackages: string[];
  };
  projectBootstrap: {
    beadsInitialized: boolean;
    gitnexusIndexed: boolean;
    instructionHeaders: boolean;
  };
  allPassed: boolean;
}
```

✅ **Status:** Correct. Comprehensive across all phases.

---

### **Phase 9: Summary & Next Steps**
**File:** `cli/src/commands/init.ts::runProjectInit()` (end)  
**Mutation Level:** None (console output + conditional guidance)

**Output:**
```
  Next steps:
    • Quality gates are active globally
    • Run `xt pi` or `xt claude` to start a worktree session
    • Project types: TypeScript, Python
```

✅ **Status:** Correct. Context-aware guidance based on verification result.

---

## **CROSS-PHASE CONSISTENCY CHECKS**

| Check | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| **Hooks canonical source → target wiring** | ✅ | `.xtrm/config/hooks.json` → `.claude/settings.json` with absolute paths |
| **Extensions source → target symlinking** | ✅ | `.xtrm/extensions/ → .pi/extensions/` |
| **Skills registry-driven install** | ✅ | `.xtrm/registry.json` → drift-aware copy to `.xtrm/skills/default` |
| **Settings preservation** | ✅ | permissions/model/skillSuggestions not overwritten in Phase 5 |
| **Machine bootstrap idempotence** | ✅ | Checks if already installed, no-ops appropriately |
| **Project bootstrap idempotence** | ✅ | bd init, gitnexus, headers all handle pre-existing state |
| **.pi/settings.json generation** | ✅ | Points to extensions symlinks & package IDs |
| **Managed block markers** | ✅ | `<!-- xtrm:start/end -->` prevent drift in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md |
| **Hook merger (deepMergeHooks)** | ✅ | Deduplicates by script filename, preserves user hooks |
| **Stale hook pruning** | ✅ | `pruneStaleHooks()` removes old versions before merge |

---

## **GAPS & MISMATCHES FOUND**

### **1. Minor: Pi Settings Extensions Path**
**Location:** Phase 6c (Pi Settings Update)  
**Issue:** `.pi/settings.json` hardcodes relative path `../.. xtrm/extensions` which assumes specific directory nesting.

**Current:**
```json
{
  "extensions": ["../.xtrm/extensions"]
}
```

**Risk:** If project structure changes or extensions are moved, symlinks break.  
**Mitigation:** Currently handled by Phase 6a which validates symlinks exist before use.  
**Action:** None needed (acceptable, documented symlink assumption).

### **2. Minor: GitNexus Timeout Handling**
**Location:** Phase 7c  
**Issue:** `gitnexus analyze` has hard-coded 120s timeout. Large repos may exceed this.

**Current:**
```typescript
const analyze = spawnSync('gitnexus', ['analyze'], {
    timeout: 120000,  // 120s
});
```

**Risk:** Large repos fail silently with timeout.  
**Mitigation:** Logs `⚠ gitnexus analyze exited with code X` but doesn't block init.  
**Action:** Acceptable (non-blocking, user can re-run manually).

### **3. Minor: Hook Wrapper Indirection**
**Location:** Phase 5 (Hooks Generation)  
**Issue:** Hooks are wrapped in `HookWrapper[]` structure which adds one level of indirection.

**Canonical:**
```json
{ "script": "hook.mjs" }
```

**Generated:**
```json
{
  "hooks": [
    { "type": "command", "command": "node ..." }
  ]
}
```

**Impact:** Correct by design (Claude's expected format), but adds complexity.  
**Action:** None needed (matches Claude settings.json schema).

### **4. ⚠️ POTENTIAL GAP: Service Registry Creation**
**Location:** Phase 1 / init.ts  
**Issue:** `detectProjectFeatures()` and `ensureServiceRegistry()` may create `service-registry.json` but Phase 1 counts this as a "project type" change, not reflected in final verification.

**Current Flow:**
```typescript
// Phase 1
const detected = await detectProjectFeatures(projectRoot);
const { generated: registryGenerated } = await ensureServiceRegistry(projectRoot, [...]);

// BUT: init.ts doesn't call ensureServiceRegistry in runProjectInit
// It's only called from syncProjectMcpServers() which runs BEFORE init
```

**Actual Gap:** `ensureServiceRegistry()` is called during MCP server sync (Phase 4), NOT during project bootstrap. This is fine, but **Phase 1 should not list Docker services as a "project type" if they're not used in verification.**

**Recommendation:** Move service registry creation to Phase 7 (Project Bootstrap) for logical grouping, or document why it's early.

**Current Status:** Functional but logically inconsistent. Services are detected but not formally initialized as part of project bootstrap.

### **5. ⚠️ POTENTIAL ISSUE: Orphaned Extensions Not Cleaned**
**Location:** Phase 6a (Extension Sync)  
**Issue:** If a user has `.pi/extensions/old-ext/` that's not in `MANAGED_EXTENSIONS`, it won't be removed.

**Inventory Tracks:**
```typescript
orphanedExtensions: string[];  // Found but not in registry
```

**But Sync Doesn't Clean:**
```typescript
// No deletion of orphaned extensions happens
```

**Risk:** Users can accumulate stale extensions.  
**Current Behavior:** Extensions list shows them as "orphaned" in logs but doesn't remove.  
**Recommendation:** Add `--prune` flag to explicitly clean orphaned extensions (already supported in some code paths).

**Current Status:** By design (preserve user customizations), but not explicit in verification output.

---

## **FINAL VALIDATION CHECKLIST**

| Phase | Reads | Writes | Idempotent | Verified |
|-------|-------|--------|-----------|----------|
| 1. Preflight | System PATH, configs | None | N/A | ✅ |
| 2. Plan | Inventory | Console | N/A | ✅ |
| 3. Confirm | stdin | None | N/A | ✅ |
| 4. Machine | System | System paths | ✅ (checks installed) | ✅ |
| 5. Claude Sync | hooks.json, existing settings | .claude/settings.json | ✅ (merges intelligently) | ✅ |
| 6. Pi Extensions | .xtrm/extensions/ | .pi/extensions/, symlinks | ✅ (detects stale) | ✅ |
| 6b. Packages | npm registry | .pi/npm/node_modules/ | ✅ (pi handles dupes) | ✅ |
| 6c. Skills | registry.json | .xtrm/skills/default/ | ✅ (drift-aware) | ✅ |
| 7a. Beads | None | .beads/ | ✅ (bd init checks) | ✅ |
| 7b. Headers | AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md | Same files | ✅ (managed blocks) | ✅ |
| 7c. GitNexus | Repo | .gitnexus/ | ✅ (gitnexus status) | ✅ |
| 7d. Service Hooks | None | None (opt-in) | N/A | ✅ |
| 7e. Pi Symlink | .xtrm/skills/ | .agents/skills | ✅ (checks destination) | ✅ |
| 8. Verification | All above | None | N/A | ✅ |
| 9. Summary | Verification | Console | N/A | ✅ |

---

## **CONCLUSION**

**Overall Assessment:** ✅ **HEALTHY**

The `xt init` flow is **well-architected and comprehensive**:

1. ✅ **Phasing is clean:** Read → Plan → Gate → Execute → Verify
2. ✅ **Hooks wiring is correct:** Canonical source properly transformed to absolute paths in settings.json
3. ✅ **Extensions properly symlinked:** Source directory → target with stale detection
4. ✅ **Skills registry-driven:** Drift-aware, user modifications preserved
5. ✅ **Idempotency:** All phases handle pre-existing state gracefully
6. ✅ **Verification comprehensive:** Covers machine, Claude, Pi, and project layers
7. ✅ **Settings preservation:** Existing permissions/model/skills not overwritten

**Minor observations** (not blockers):
- Service registry creation is early (Phase 4) but functional
- Orphaned extensions not auto-cleaned (by design)
- GitNexus timeout is hard-coded (acceptable for typical repos)

**No critical gaps or mismatches detected.** The flow is production-ready.

---

title: fix(prune): surgical plugin dir cleanup + install 4 official plugins
description:
Two issues in plugin-era-cleanup.ts and xt install flow:

## Fix 1: Surgical prune (NOT wholesale delete)

plugin-era-cleanup.ts currently deletes ~/.claude/plugins/ entirely. This wipes serena, context7, and any other user-installed official plugins.

Change planGlobalOperations() to be surgical:
- DELETE ~/.claude/plugins/data/xtrm-tools-xtrm-tools/ (only this entry)
- DELETE ~/.claude/plugins/cache/xtrm-tools/ (if exists)
- DELETE ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/xtrm-tools/ (if exists)
- UPDATE ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json: remove only 'xtrm-tools@xtrm-tools' key (if file exists)
- UPDATE ~/.claude/plugins/known_marketplaces.json: remove only 'xtrm-tools' key (if file exists)
- PRESERVE everything else (serena, context7, other user plugins)
- Do NOT delete ~/.claude/plugins/ directory itself

## Fix 2: Install 4 official plugins/MCPs during xt install

xt install (machine-bootstrap or install phase) must ensure these are present:
- serena (claude plugin from claude-plugins-official marketplace)
- context7 (claude plugin from claude-plugins-official marketplace)
- deepwiki (MCP server — already handled via .mcp.json write)
- github-grep (MCP server — already handled via .mcp.json write)

For serena + context7: use 'claude plugin marketplace install <name>' or 'claude plugin install <name>' if not already installed. Check installed state before installing (idempotent). Add to runMachineBootstrapPhase() or a new ensureOfficialPlugins() step in install.ts.

Check: what command installs a plugin from the official marketplace? 'claude plugin marketplace install serena' or 'claude plugin install serena'? Check help output.

## Key constraint
Only touch xtrm-tools-specific entries. Never delete other plugins' data.
notes:
Implemented ✅

#### 1) Surgical plugin prune in `plugin-era-cleanup.ts`
Updated `planGlobalOperations()` and cleanup logic to **only remove xtrm-tools plugin artifacts** under `~/.claude/plugins`, preserving everything else.

**Now removes only:**

title: bug(install): checkDrift reports all files up-to-date when target .xtrm/ is empty
description:
E2E test: xt init -y on a fresh project reports 'Installed 0, Up-to-date 260, Drift skipped 0' but the target project's .xtrm/ directory is completely empty after install. No files are scaffolded.

Expected: missing files (all 260) should be scaffolded into the target .xtrm/.
Actual: checkDrift() returns all 260 as upToDate, nothing is written.

Root cause hypothesis: checkDrift() in cli/src/core/drift.ts resolves the userXtrmDir incorrectly — likely comparing the source package's .xtrm/ against itself (same path) rather than against the target project's .xtrm/. The registry.json is read from the xtrm-tools package root, but the userXtrmDir passed to checkDrift may also be resolving to the package root instead of the target project root.

Check: install.ts line that calls checkDrift() — verify the second argument is the TARGET project's .xtrm/ path, not the package's .xtrm/ path.

Repro: mkdir /tmp/test-project && cd /tmp/test-project && git init && xt init -y
Verify: ls /tmp/test-project/.xtrm/ should contain hooks/, config/, skills/, registry.json — currently empty.
close_reason: False positive: getProjectRoot() uses process.cwd() which was xtrm-tools repo in previous E2E (Bash tool cwd). Current code correctly scaffolds 110 files + symlinks skills/default on fresh project. Verified 2026-04-01.

title: feat(install): xt install --prune — clean up plugin-era artifacts
description:
Add --prune flag to xt install that removes all stale plugin-era artifacts after migrating to .xtrm/ hook-wiring model.

## Context
xtrm-gl8b moved all hooks/skills/config to .xtrm/ and eliminated the Claude plugin. Existing installs still have plugin-era artifacts that xt install currently does not remove.

## What --prune must clean

### Global scope
- DELETE ~/.claude/plugins/ (entire dir: cache, marketplaces, installed_plugins.json, known_marketplaces.json)
- DELETE keys from ~/.claude/settings.json: enabledPlugins, extraKnownMarketplaces (preserve permissions, model, hooks, skillSuggestions, statusLine)
- DELETE ~/.claude/skills/ (old global skill copies — new model uses .xtrm/skills/default/ only)
- DELETE ~/.claude/hooks/ (legacy hook files — new model uses .xtrm/hooks/)
- DELETE ~/.pi/agent/extensions/ xtrm-managed entries ONLY (beads, session-flow, quality-gates, service-skills, xtrm-loader, custom-footer, lsp-bootstrap, plan-mode, auto-session-name, auto-update, compact-header, git-checkpoint, pi-serena-compact, xtrm-ui) — preserve any non-xtrm custom extensions
- DELETE ~/.agents/skills/ xtrm-managed entries ONLY — preserve custom user skills

### Project scope
- DELETE keys from .claude/settings.json: enabledPlugins, extraKnownMarketplaces
- DELETE stale .claude/hooks/ files: hook-config.json, quality-check.cjs, quality-check.py, specialists-complete.mjs, specialists-session-start.mjs — ONLY if duplicated in .xtrm/hooks/ already; preserve project-custom hooks

### Settings merge: replace not add
In claude-runtime-sync.ts, --prune mode should also:
- Replace hooks wholesale (remove stale CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT-style commands, write fresh .xtrm/hooks/ paths)
- Delete enabledPlugins and extraKnownMarketplaces keys from merged output

## CLI interface
- xt install --prune (interactive: shows diff of what will be deleted, confirms before each scope)
- xt install --prune --yes (non-interactive bypass)
- xt install --prune --dry-run (show what would be removed without doing it)
- Must use confirmDestructiveAction() from cli/src/utils/confirmation.ts

## Implementation locations
- cli/src/commands/install.ts — add --prune flag, call runPluginEraCleanup()
- cli/src/core/claude-runtime-sync.ts — add prune?: boolean option, delete plugin keys from merged settings
- cli/src/core/plugin-era-cleanup.ts (new) — PLUGIN_ERA_ARTIFACTS array + runPluginEraCleanup() function

## XTRM-managed Pi extension IDs (for selective delete in ~/.pi/agent/extensions/)
beads, session-flow, quality-gates, service-skills, xtrm-loader, custom-footer, lsp-bootstrap, plan-mode, auto-session-name, auto-update, compact-header, git-checkpoint, pi-serena-compact, xtrm-ui, core

## Acceptance criteria
- xt install --prune --dry-run shows all targets without modifying anything
- xt install --prune --yes removes all plugin-era artifacts without prompting
- ~/.claude/settings.json retains permissions/model/skillSuggestions/statusLine/hooks after prune
- hooks in settings.json use .xtrm/hooks/ absolute paths after prune
- enabledPlugins and extraKnownMarketplaces gone from settings.json
- ~/.claude/plugins/ does not exist after prune
- Custom user extensions/skills not in xtrm managed list are preserved
notes:
Implemented ✅ `xt install --prune` with plugin-era cleanup + prune-aware Claude settings sync.

### Changed files

- **`cli/src/core/plugin-era-cleanup.ts`** *(new)*
  - Added:
    - `PLUGIN_ERA_ARTIFACTS` constants
    - `runPluginEraCleanup(opts)` with:
      - `dryRun`, `yes`, `scope`, `repoRoot`
      - per-scope planning
      - dry-run preview output
      - destructive confirmation per scope via `confirmDestructiveAction()`
  - Global cleanup:

title: Test: xt install .xtrm/ scaffold + drift detection E2E — integration tests (shell layer)
description:
## Context

Test companion to xtrm-gl8b.7 (xt install rewrite) and xtrm-gl8b.6 (claude-runtime-sync rewrite). The install command is shell-layer: it orchestrates drift detection (core), file scaffold (I/O), and settings.json hook wiring. Integration tests run the full install flow in a temp directory and assert observable outcomes.

Layer: shell
Strategy: integration (function-level wiring — call createInstallCommand handler directly or via subprocess in temp dir)
Covers: xtrm-gl8b.6, xtrm-gl8b.7
Gates: xtrm-gl8b.9 (do not do final cleanup until these tests pass)

## What to assert

### Fresh install — empty project
- [ ] End-to-end: install into empty temp dir creates \`.xtrm/hooks/\`, \`.xtrm/config/\`, \`.xtrm/skills/default/\`, \`.xtrm/extensions/\`
- [ ] settings.json written with hook entries using absolute path to the temp dir's .xtrm/hooks/
- [ ] Each hook entry is of the form \`node "/abs/path/.xtrm/hooks/<name>.mjs"\`

### Idempotent re-install — up-to-date files
- [ ] Running install twice: second run skips all files (reported as upToDate), does not overwrite

### Drift detection — user-modified file
- [ ] Create a user project with a modified .xtrm/hooks/beads-edit-gate.mjs (different content)
- [ ] Install without --force: file skipped, drift warning printed to console, exit code 0
- [ ] Install with --force: file overwritten with upstream version, no drift warning

### Dry-run
- [ ] \`xt install --dry-run\`: prints planned actions (scaffold list), creates NO files

### Hook wiring (claude-runtime-sync)
- [ ] After install, settings.json hooks section contains entries for all hooks in .xtrm/config/hooks.json
- [ ] settings.json does NOT contain any plugin marketplace or \`claude plugin\` references
- [ ] Existing permissions.allow[] in settings.json preserved after re-install (merge, not replace)

## Test file structure

\`\`\`
cli/src/tests/install-integration.test.ts
\`\`\`

## Test setup

\`\`\`ts
import os from 'os';
import fs from 'fs-extra';
import path from 'path';

let tmpDir: string;
beforeEach(() => { tmpDir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'xtrm-install-test-')); });
afterEach(() => { fs.removeSync(tmpDir); });
\`\`\`

## Critical paths covered

- Full scaffold: risk of missing .xtrm/ files in a fresh user project (agent workflow broken)
- Drift warning: risk of silently overwriting user customizations (data loss risk)
- Hook path injection: risk of wrong .xtrm/hooks/ path in settings.json breaking all hooks
- Merge-not-replace: risk of xt install wiping user's permissions.allow[] (security regression)

## Known deferred paths

- Test install with --global flag (writes to ~/.agents/skills, ~/.claude/settings.json) → follow-up P3
- Test install with corrupt registry.json → follow-up P4

## AC

Layer: shell
Strategy: integration (function-level wiring)
Done when: all assertions pass in CI, covering fresh install, idempotent re-install, drift detection, dry-run, and hook wiring merge.
notes:
Implemented ✅ for **xtrm-gl8b.12**.

Covers all requested shell-layer integration paths:

2. **Idempotent re-install**

3. **Drift detection**

4. **Dry-run**

5. **Hook wiring / merge behavior**

title: Test: hook scripts after CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT purge — shell layer integration tests
description:
## Context

Test companion to xtrm-gl8b.4 (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT purge). Hook scripts are shell-layer code: they receive a JSON payload from Claude Code on stdin and produce JSON on stdout. The right testing strategy is integration tests that invoke the hook scripts as subprocesses with crafted payloads, asserting on stdout and exit codes.

Layer: shell
Strategy: integration (subprocess — invoke hook scripts via node CLI)
Covers: xtrm-gl8b.4

## What to assert

### using-xtrm-reminder.mjs
- [ ] End-to-end: given cwd with .xtrm/skills/default/using-xtrm/SKILL.md, hook outputs additionalSystemPrompt containing skill content
- [ ] Failure-mode: cwd without .xtrm/skills/default/ → exits 0 with no output (fail open)
- [ ] Edge: cwd with .xtrm/memory.md → memory content appended after SKILL.md content
- [ ] No CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT usage: hook works correctly when CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var is absent

### quality-check-env.mjs
- [ ] End-to-end: cwd with .xtrm/hooks/quality-check.cjs present → checks for tsc/eslint/ruff
- [ ] End-to-end: cwd without .xtrm/hooks/quality-check.cjs → exits 0 immediately (hook not applicable)
- [ ] Failure-mode: tsc missing → output contains warning about tsc not found
- [ ] No CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT: hook operates identically whether env var set or not

### General hook contract
- [ ] All hooks exit 0 in all tested paths (fail open contract)
- [ ] Output (when present) is valid JSON with \`hookSpecificOutput\` key

## Test file structure

\`\`\`
cli/src/tests/hooks-integration.test.ts
\`\`\`
Or in the hooks/ directory:
\`\`\`
.xtrm/hooks/tests/hooks.test.mjs    ← vitest or node:test
\`\`\`

## Test invocation pattern

\`\`\`ts
import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';

function invokeHook(scriptPath: string, payload: object): { stdout: string; exitCode: number } {
  const result = spawnSync('node', [scriptPath], {
    input: JSON.stringify(payload),
    encoding: 'utf8',
    env: { ...process.env, CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT: undefined }  // ensure not set
  });
  return { stdout: result.stdout, exitCode: result.status ?? 0 };
}
\`\`\`

## Critical paths covered

- SKILL.md injection (using-xtrm-reminder.mjs): risk of agent starting session without xtrm workflow context
- Quality gate env check: risk of silently degraded enforcement (missing tsc/eslint)
- Fail-open contract: risk of broken hook blocking Claude Code session start

## Known deferred paths

- Test all hooks (beads-edit-gate, beads-commit-gate, beads-stop-gate) with mock bd commands → follow-up P3

## AC

Done when: subprocess integration tests pass for both hooks with and without CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT set.
notes:
Implemented ✅

### Added
- `cli/test/hooks-integration.test.ts`

### Coverage included

#### `using-xtrm-reminder.mjs`
- end-to-end SKILL.md injection from:
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/using-xtrm/SKILL.md`
- fail-open when skill path missing:
  - exit `0`, no output
- memory append edge case:
  - appends `.xtrm/memory.md` after SKILL content
- no `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` dependency:
  - tested with env var absent

#### `quality-check-env.mjs`
- end-to-end with `.xtrm/hooks/quality-check.cjs` present:
  - verifies checks/warnings for `tsc`, `eslint`, `ruff`
- hook-not-applicable path:
  - missing `.xtrm/hooks/quality-check.cjs` exits `0` with no output
- failure mode:
  - `tsc` missing produces `tsc not found` warning
- no `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` dependency:
  - behavior asserted identical with and without env var set

#### General contract
- all tested paths assert exit code `0` (fail-open)
- whenever output exists, it is parsed as JSON and validated to contain `hookSpecificOutput`

### Verification
Ran:

```bash
cd cli && npm test -- test/hooks-integration.test.ts
```

Result: **7 tests passed**.

title: Test: .xtrm/registry.json schema + drift detection — unit tests (core layer)
description:
## Context

Test companion to xtrm-gl8b.3 (registry.json) and xtrm-gl8b.7 (drift.ts). The registry schema and drift detection logic are pure functions (core layer): read a JSON file, hash files on disk, compare hashes. No HTTP, no Claude subprocess, no plugin. Unit tests with example-based strategy.

Layer: core
Strategy: unit + example-based
Covers: xtrm-gl8b.3, xtrm-gl8b.7

## What to assert

### registry.json schema (xtrm-gl8b.3)
- [ ] Positive: valid registry.json parses without error
- [ ] Negative: registry missing \`version\` field raises or returns error
- [ ] Negative: registry with non-SHA-256 hash format is detected as invalid
- [ ] Edge: registry with empty assets {} is accepted (valid but empty)
- [ ] Invariant: gen-registry.mjs output is idempotent (running twice produces same hash)

### drift detection in cli/src/core/drift.ts (xtrm-gl8b.7)
- [ ] Positive: file with matching hash → reported as upToDate
- [ ] Positive: file missing from user .xtrm/ → reported as missing
- [ ] Positive: file with different content → reported as drifted
- [ ] Edge: empty registry → DriftReport has empty arrays
- [ ] Edge: user .xtrm/ dir doesn't exist → all files reported as missing (not an error)
- [ ] Property: every file from registry appears in exactly one of missing|upToDate|drifted

### SHA-256 hashing consistency
- [ ] Same file content → same hash regardless of filename
- [ ] Empty file → specific known hash (not empty string)
- [ ] Hash of a symlink follows the symlink (hashes file content, not symlink path)

## Test file structure

\`\`\`
cli/src/tests/drift.test.ts       ← drift detection unit tests
cli/src/tests/registry.test.ts    ← registry schema parse + gen-registry idempotence
\`\`\`

Use existing test infrastructure: check \`cli/src/tests/\` for current test runner setup.

## Critical paths covered

- Missing file detection: risk of xt install silently skipping scaffold for new assets
- Drift detection: risk of xt install --force overwriting non-drifted files (hash compare must be correct)
- Registry schema: risk of gen-registry.mjs producing invalid output that breaks xt install

## Known deferred paths

- Test gen-registry.mjs with a directory containing symlinks that point outside the package root → follow-up P3 (edge case for npm pack)

## AC

Done when: all assertions above are implemented and passing in CI (npm test).

Fixture policy:
- Use tmp directories (os.tmpdir()) for file system assertions — clean up after each test
- No shared mutable state between tests
- Deterministic fixtures: hardcoded file content, no Date.now() or random
notes:
Implemented ✅ core-layer unit tests for the requested coverage.

### Files added
- `cli/src/tests/drift.test.ts`
- `cli/src/tests/registry.test.ts`

### What’s covered

#### `cli/src/tests/registry.test.ts`
- Valid registry manifest parses successfully
- Missing `version` is rejected
- Non-SHA-256 hash is rejected
- Empty `assets: {}` is accepted
- `scripts/gen-registry.mjs` idempotence verified by running twice in an isolated temp git fixture and asserting identical output hash/content

#### `cli/src/tests/drift.test.ts`
- Matching hash → `upToDate`
- Missing file → `missing`
- Different content → `drifted`
- Empty registry → all arrays empty
- Missing user `.xtrm` dir → all files reported as `missing` (no error)
- Partition property: every registry file appears in exactly one of `missing | upToDate | drifted`

#### SHA-256 consistency (via drift behavior tests)
- Same content across different filenames resolves as `upToDate`
- Empty file uses known SHA-256 (`e3b0...b855`) and is `upToDate`
- Symlink path hashes target file content (symlink-following behavior)

---

### Test runs

Targeted new suites:
```bash
npm test --workspace cli -- src/tests/drift.test.ts src/tests/registry.test.ts
```
Result: **2 files passed, 10 tests passed**

Full workspace suite:
```bash
npm test --workspace cli
```
Result: fails due **pre-existing unrelated tests** in this branch (not from these new test files).

- Claimed state already present

title: Update package.json — remove prepack/postpack, update files[] for .xtrm/ layout
description:
## Context

Phase 3 of xtrm-gl8b. The npm package currently uses prepack/postpack scripts to handle the hooks/ and skills/ symlink problem (npm pack doesn't follow symlinks, so prepack copies the real content into plugins/xtrm-tools/ before packing). With plugins/ eliminated and assets moved to .xtrm/, we need to update the package manifest to include .xtrm/ directly and drop the workaround scripts.

Depends on: xtrm-gl8b.6, xtrm-gl8b.7

## What to build

### package.json changes

1. **scripts**: remove \`prepack\` and \`postpack\` entries (no more symlink workaround needed)
2. **files[]**: update the publish manifest:
   - Remove: \`"config"\`, \`"hooks"\`, \`"skills"\`, \`"project-skills"\`, \`".claude-plugin"\`, \`"plugins"\`
   - Add: \`".xtrm"\` (include the whole .xtrm/ tree)
   - Keep: \`"README.md"\`, \`"CHANGELOG.md"\`, \`"cli/dist"\`, \`"cli/package.json"\`
   - Note: .xtrm/ contains symlinks — verify npm pack follows them correctly via \`npm pack --dry-run\`
3. **scripts**: remove \`prepack\` and \`postpack\` from the scripts section

### Symlink handling in npm pack
- npm pack follows symlinks when packing but the symlink target must be within the package root
- .xtrm/skills/default → ../../skills/ — this points OUTSIDE the published package root, which breaks for npm consumers
- Resolution: at pack time, OR in the gen-registry step, ensure skills are included as real files (not symlinks) in .xtrm/skills/default/
- Modify the files[] or add a prepublishOnly step that materializes the skills symlink to a real copy for packaging

### scripts/prepack-plugin.mjs and scripts/postpack-plugin.mjs
- These files can be deleted after verifying nothing else references them
- Double-check: \`grep -r "prepack-plugin\|postpack-plugin" .\` — remove all references

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] \`npm pack --dry-run\` includes \`.xtrm/hooks/\`, \`.xtrm/config/\`, \`.xtrm/skills/default/\`, \`.xtrm/extensions/\`, \`.xtrm/registry.json\`
- [ ] \`npm pack --dry-run\` does NOT include \`plugins/\`, \`config/\` (the symlink), \`hooks/\` (the symlink)
- [ ] No \`prepack\`/\`postpack\` scripts in package.json
- [ ] \`scripts/prepack-plugin.mjs\` and \`scripts/postpack-plugin.mjs\` deleted
- [ ] Published package can be installed in a test project and xt install finds .xtrm/registry.json

## Approach notes

- Use \`npm pack --dry-run 2>&1 | grep "npm notice"\` to verify file list before committing
- Consider adding \`.npmrc\` flag or a prepublishOnly script that materializes symlinks before pack
- The .npmignore already has some exclusions — review and update to match new layout
notes:
Implemented ✅ for `xtrm-gl8b.8` and closed the bead.

### Changed files

- `package.json`
  - Removed `scripts.prepack` and `scripts.postpack`
  - Updated `files[]`:
    - removed legacy publish paths (`config`, `hooks`, `skills`, `project-skills`, `.claude-plugin`, `plugins`)
    - added `.xtrm`
    - kept `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `cli/dist`, `cli/package.json`
    - added `.xtrm`-specific exclude patterns for runtime/cache artifacts
- `scripts/prepack-plugin.mjs` **deleted**
- `scripts/postpack-plugin.mjs` **deleted**
- `.gitignore`
  - removed old prepack-plugin copy comments/rules
  - added `.xtrm`-path ignores for workspace/cache artifacts

### Symlink packaging resolution

To satisfy `npm pack` behavior, I materialized these as real directories in repo state for packing:

- `.xtrm/skills/default/` (real files, not symlink)
- `.xtrm/extensions/` (real files, not symlink)
