title: Port PidexEditor — setEditorComponent with density-aware input padding
description:
## Context

pi-dex registers a custom editor component (PidexEditor extends CustomEditor) via ctx.ui.setEditorComponent(). The key behaviour: it calls setPaddingX based on density — compact=1px, comfortable=2px. xtrm-ui currently skips setEditorComponent entirely, so the editor always uses the default padding regardless of density.

This is a visible difference: in compact mode, pi-dex has tighter horizontal padding on the input area.

## What to build

- Port the PidexEditor class (from /tmp/package/src/editor.ts) into xtrm-ui/index.ts as XtrmEditor
- Import CustomEditor from "@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent" (already available at runtime)
- XtrmEditor.setPrefs(prefs: XtrmUiPrefs): sets this.setPaddingX(prefs.density === "comfortable" ? 2 : 1)
- In applyXtrmChrome(), add:
  ctx.ui.setEditorComponent((tui, theme, keybindings) => {
    const editor = new XtrmEditor(tui, theme, keybindings);
    editor.setPrefs(prefs);
    return editor;
  });
- Place the XtrmEditor class definition near the top of index.ts, before applyXtrmChrome

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] XtrmEditor class defined, extends CustomEditor
- [ ] setPrefs() calls setPaddingX correctly: compact → 1, comfortable → 2
- [ ] applyXtrmChrome registers setEditorComponent with XtrmEditor instance
- [ ] Changing density via /xtrm-ui-density updates editor padding immediately (next chrome apply)
- [ ] Extension compiles — CustomEditor is importable from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent

## Approach notes

- Reference: /tmp/package/src/editor.ts — the complete PidexEditor class (only 25 lines)
- Reference: /tmp/package/src/chrome.ts → ctx.ui.setEditorComponent call site
- CustomEditor is exported from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent — check the existing import line in index.ts for the correct import path
- The prefs type used in XtrmEditor should be XtrmUiPrefs (not PidexPrefs)
- render() override can be kept (calls super.render) or omitted if not overriding behaviour

## Dependencies

- Should be done after xtrm-p38n.1 (getThinkingLevel fix) since both touch applyXtrmChrome

## Layer

Shell layer — UI component registration. No I/O.
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/165
close_reason: Port pi-dex features into xtrm-ui: XtrmEditor+setEditorComponent, input/context prefix handlers, session_shutdown theme restore, format.ts helpers, registerXtrmUiTools tool renderers

title: Add forceTheme pref — guard setTheme calls behind a boolean flag
description:
## Context

pi-dex has a forceTheme boolean in PidexPrefs that controls whether setTheme is called when chrome is applied. xtrm-ui always calls setTheme unconditionally. Without forceTheme=false support, xtrm-ui will override any externally set theme every time a session_switch, model_select, or command handler fires.

This matters when a user wants to use a custom theme while keeping xtrm-ui's header/tools. forceTheme=true (default) preserves existing behaviour; forceTheme=false skips setTheme.

## What to build

- Add forceTheme: boolean to XtrmUiPrefs interface (default: true)
- Add forceTheme to DEFAULT_PREFS
- Update normalizePrefs() to read source.forceTheme ?? true
- Update applyXtrmChrome(): wrap ctx.ui.setTheme(prefs.themeName) in if (prefs.forceTheme)
- Update /xtrm-ui status command output to include forceTheme line
- Add /xtrm-ui-forcetheme on|off command (or expose via /xtrm-ui-reset restore to default)
  - Recommend adding the command for discoverability
- Persist forceTheme in prefs via persistPrefs (already JSON-serialised, no extra work)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] XtrmUiPrefs.forceTheme exists, default true
- [ ] applyXtrmChrome only calls setTheme when prefs.forceTheme is true
- [ ] /xtrm-ui-forcetheme on|off command registered and working
- [ ] /xtrm-ui status output includes "Force theme: on/off"
- [ ] Prefs survive round-trip through persistPrefs/loadPrefs
- [ ] Extension compiles

## Approach notes

- Reference: /tmp/package/src/prefs.ts — PidexPrefs.forceTheme definition
- Reference: /tmp/package/src/chrome.ts — if (prefs.forceTheme) ctx.ui.setTheme(...)
- This task modifies applyXtrmChrome — do after Task A (getThinkingLevel fix) to avoid merge conflicts if working in parallel
- The /xtrm-ui-forcetheme command follows the same pattern as /xtrm-ui-header

## Dependencies

- Should be done after xtrm-p38n.1 (getThinkingLevel fix) since both tasks modify applyXtrmChrome

## Layer

Shell layer — pref management + UI wiring. No I/O.
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/165
close_reason: Add forceTheme pref to xtrm-ui — guard setTheme, add /xtrm-ui-forcetheme command, show in status

title: Fix getThinkingLevel() — pass real thinking level into header and footer
description:
## Context

xtrm-ui currently hardcodes the thinking level as the string 'standard' everywhere it renders the header model line and footer. pi-dex calls pi.getThinkingLevel() and passes the real value through. This means when a user switches thinking to 'extended' or turns it off, the header still shows 'standard'.

The fix requires threading pi (ExtensionAPI) into the chrome apply function so getThinkingLevel() can be called at render time, not at setup time.

## What to build

- Change applyXtrmChrome(ctx, prefs, getThinkingLevel) so that the third arg is already () => pi.getThinkingLevel() instead of () => "standard"
- Update every call site of applyXtrmChrome and refresh():
  - pi.on("session_start") callback
  - pi.on("session_switch") callback
  - pi.on("session_fork") callback
  - pi.on("model_select") callback
  - all /xtrm-ui-* command handlers that call applyXtrmChrome
- In the header render fn, call getThinkingLevel() at render time (it already does this, just needs the real fn)
- Mirror pi-dex's formatThinking: level === "off" ? "standard" : level (thinking 'off' displays as 'standard')

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] applyXtrmChrome no longer receives a hardcoded () => "standard" anywhere
- [ ] pi.getThinkingLevel() is passed as the factory; header reflects the real value
- [ ] formatThinking helper: "off" → "standard", else pass-through
- [ ] Extension compiles with tsc --noEmit (or equivalent)
- [ ] Switching /thinking extended updates the header line immediately

## Approach notes

- Reference: /tmp/package/src/chrome.ts → applyPidexChrome signature, formatThinking fn
- Reference: /tmp/package/src/index.ts → installPidex/refreshPidex call sites
- In xtrm-ui, the getThinkingLevel factory is the third param of applyXtrmChrome — change all callers in the closure at extension init time: const getThinkingLevel = () => pi.getThinkingLevel()
- pi.getThinkingLevel() returns ThinkingLevel ("off" | "standard" | "extended") — import type from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent if needed

## Layer

Shell layer — wires pi API into UI render callback. No I/O; pure call-site refactor.
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/165
close_reason: Thread real pi.getThinkingLevel() through xtrm-ui — add formatThinking helper, create factory closure at init, replace all hardcoded () => 'standard' call sites

title: Port all missing pi-dex features into xtrm-ui Pi extension
description:
## Overview

xtrm-ui is our custom Pi extension that replaced pi-dex to avoid a footer race condition with custom-footer. During that migration, several pi-dex features were intentionally omitted or simplified. This epic tracks porting those missing features back into xtrm-ui so it achieves full functional parity with pi-dex.

## Goals

- Port 6 discrete pi-dex features into config/pi/extensions/xtrm-ui/index.ts
- Maintain xtrm-ui's intentional differences (showFooter: false, custom-footer owns footer)
- Keep the extension as a single compilable TypeScript file (or minimal multi-file structure)

## Non-goals

- Changing the footer behaviour — custom-footer still owns it
- Adopting pi-dex's command names (we keep /xtrm-ui-* prefix)
- Porting the /pidex-doctor command verbatim (we have /xtrm-ui already)

## Features to port (child tasks)

1. Fix getThinkingLevel() — stop hardcoding "standard", use pi.getThinkingLevel()
2. Add forceTheme pref — boolean guard before setTheme calls
3. Port PidexEditor / setEditorComponent — density-aware input padding
4. Port input/context event handlers — prepend/strip '› ' prefix on user messages
5. Port theme restoration on session_shutdown — save/restore previous theme
6. Port format.ts helper functions — pure formatting utilities used by tool renderers
7. Port registerPidexTools → registerXtrmUiTools — compact tool renderers for bash/read/edit/write/find/grep/ls

## Success criteria

- [ ] All 7 tasks closed, extension compiles without errors
- [ ] pi.getThinkingLevel() reflected in header model line
- [ ] forceTheme=false allows external theme without overriding
- [ ] Input lines display '› ' prefix in TUI; model sees clean text
- [ ] Theme is restored to pre-xtrm-ui value on session_shutdown
- [ ] Tool calls show compact summary lines with duration, diff stats, match counts
- [ ] Density compact/comfortable controls editor padding (1px vs 2px)

## Key files

- config/pi/extensions/xtrm-ui/index.ts — the file to extend
- /tmp/package/src/tooling.ts — tool renderer reference
- /tmp/package/src/format.ts — format helper reference
- /tmp/package/src/editor.ts — PidexEditor reference
- /tmp/package/src/chrome.ts — chrome apply fn (partially ported already)
- /tmp/package/src/index.ts — pi-dex main (event handler reference)
close_reason: xtrm-ui work complete: pi-dex parity shipped, Serena MCP compaction merged (xtrm-oh7l), GitNexus deferred pending Pi API

title: Implement xt-debugging skill (unified debugging)
description:
Create a unified debugging skill that merges gitnexus-debugging, debug-helper, and bug-hunt capabilities.

## Skills to Combine

1. **gitnexus-debugging** (`~/.agents/skills/gitnexus-debugging/SKILL.md`)
   - GitNexus knowledge graph for call-chain tracing
   - `gitnexus_query`, `gitnexus_context`, `gitnexus_cypher`
   - Process execution flow analysis

2. **debug-helper** (`~/.pi/agent/skills/debug-helper/SKILL.md`)
   - Systematic debugging workflow
   - Error analysis (stack traces, reproduction)
   - Log analysis (pattern identification)
   - Performance profiling (measurement, optimization)

3. **bug-hunt specialist** (`config/specialists/bug-hunt.specialist.yaml`)
   - Autonomous investigation phases
   - GitNexus triage → File discovery → Root cause → Hypothesis → Remediation
   - Structured Bug Hunt Report output

## Target Outcome

One unified skill that:
- Guides agents through complete debugging workflow
- Uses GitNexus for code-aware investigation when available
- Falls back to traditional grep/find when GitNexus unavailable
- Covers error analysis, log interpretation, and performance profiling
- Produces structured reports with root cause and fix plans

## Location

Create as: `~/.agents/skills/unified-debugging/SKILL.md`
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/166
close_reason: Created skills/unified-debugging/SKILL.md merging gitnexus-debugging + debug-helper into 7-phase workflow (triage→graph→file discovery→root cause→logs→perf→remediation)

Pi should match: fire the memory gate prompt as agent-visible context only, no UI notification, no new turn forced. Need to investigate if Pi's agent_end supports an additionalContext-style return, or if there is another injection point (e.g. a tool_result append on the final bd close) that achieves silent agent-only visibility.

title: beads-claim-sync: auto-commit is unsafe (stageUntracked + --no-verify)
description:
autoCommit() in hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs has two safety problems:

1. stageUntracked() (line 108) runs git ls-files --others --exclude-standard and stages ALL untracked files before committing. Any temp file, local config, or scratch file in the working directory gets swept into the commit without the agent or user reviewing it.

2. git commit --no-verify -am (line 112) bypasses all pre-commit hooks (lint, typecheck, secrets scanning) via --no-verify.

Fix options:
- Drop stageUntracked entirely — only commit already-tracked/staged changes (-a covers modified tracked files, explicit git add covers intentionally staged new files)
- Remove --no-verify so pre-commit hooks run normally
- Or at minimum warn when untracked files are about to be staged
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/167
close_reason: Drop stageUntracked and --no-verify from autoCommit in beads-claim-sync.mjs

title: Fix statusLine not written on fresh installs
description: installUserStatusLine reads installed_plugins.json to find the plugin installPath — on a fresh machine or after a failed plugin install, the entry may be missing so entries.length===0 and we silently return without writing statusLine. Fix: resolve path from __dirname (same as xtrmPkgRoot pattern on line 337) — cli/dist/ is always __dirname, statusline.mjs is always at ../../hooks/statusline.mjs.
close_reason: Use __dirname to resolve statusline.mjs path — avoids installed_plugins.json dependency that fails on fresh machines

title: Fix statusline stale claim in worktree sessions
description:
Two bugs causing stale claim display in Claude Code worktree sessions:

1. statusline.mjs reads claimTitle from bd show but never checks if issue is closed — shows the closed issue's title forever.
2. Both statusline.mjs and beads-claim-sync.mjs use join(cwd, '.xtrm', 'statusline-claim') for the claim file. In worktree sessions, cwd is the worktree dir, so write and delete can target different paths — stale file persists.

Fix: use git rev-parse --git-common-dir to resolve the main repo root (absolute in worktrees, relative .git in main), use it as the canonical claim file location in both files. Add closed-issue self-healing check in statusline.mjs.
close_reason: Fix statusline stale claim in worktrees: use git common-dir for canonical mainRoot claim file path, add closed-issue self-healing in statusline.mjs

title: Fix stale beads claim in custom-footer worktree sessions
description:
Two bugs in ~/.pi/agent/extensions/custom-footer/index.ts:

1. sessionId assignment uses '??' which doesn't fall through on empty strings. If ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId() returns '' (which happens in worktree Pi sessions), sessionId stays '', !sessionId guard fires, refreshBeadState never runs, stale claim state persists forever. Fix: use '||' instead of '??'.

2. buildIssueLine hardcodes '◐' prefix regardless of actual issue status. Fix: use STATUS_ICONS[status] from beadState.
close_reason: Fix ?? → || for sessionId (empty string fallthrough), fix hardcoded ◐ icon in buildIssueLine to use STATUS_ICONS[status]

title: Test: integration parity for custom-footer with pi-dex refresh events
description:

title: Harden pi-dex coexistence and footer refresh lifecycle
description:
## Context

`pi-dex` and `custom-footer` both call `setFooter`. We already added delayed reclaim, but parity rollout increases render complexity and must remain robust across session/model transitions.

## What to build

- Validate and harden footer ownership reclaim on:
  - `session_start`
  - `session_switch`
  - `session_fork`
  - `model_select`
- Ensure timer lifecycle cleanup on shutdown/dispose
- Add guardrails to avoid duplicate subscriptions / stale render handles

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Footer consistently returns to XTRM after pi-dex chrome refresh
- [ ] No timer leaks or duplicate render callbacks over long sessions
- [ ] Branch-change reactivity still works after reapply cycles

## Approach notes

Preserve current delayed reclaim pattern, but ensure idempotent setup/teardown.
close_reason: Validated and retained delayed footer reclaim lifecycle for pi-dex coexistence with cleanup safeguards

title: Render two-line Pi footer with Claude-equivalent field layout
description:
## Context

Current Pi footer is single-line and lacks key statusline fields. Claude statusline shows two rows with environment + issue tracking details. We need render parity for operator ergonomics.

## What to build

Update `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer/index.ts` render output to two lines:

- Line 1: `XTRM`, model + context used%, host, repo-relative dir, branch + git flags, optional venv
- Line 2: claim-first issue row: `◐ <id> <title>`; fallback `○ <N open>` or `○ no open issues`
- Width-aware truncation for long issue titles and narrow terminals

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Footer returns two lines in standard-width terminals
- [ ] Line-1 ordering matches parity spec
- [ ] Line-2 claim/open fallback behavior matches Claude statusline
- [ ] Title truncation works without broken ANSI formatting

## Approach notes

Reuse existing chip/formatting selectively, but preserve readability in both light/dark themes.
close_reason: Implemented two-line footer layout matching Claude statusline field order with issue row fallback

title: Build status snapshot collectors for Pi footer parity
description:
## Context

Parity with Claude statusline requires richer metadata than the current Pi footer exposes. We need dedicated collectors for git flags, host, venv, and repo-relative cwd, plus claim title/open count snapshots.

## What to build

Implement/organize data collection in `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer/index.ts`:

- Git snapshot: branch, dirty flags (`* + -`), ahead/behind (`↑ ↓ ↕`)
- Environment snapshot: hostname, venv name, model id/context usage
- Path snapshot: repo-relative display path fallback to cwd short path
- Beads snapshot: claim id, status, title; fallback open count
- Shared cached state object with TTL to avoid expensive per-render shell calls

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Snapshot collectors return stable values when git/bd are unavailable
- [ ] Git status markers match Claude statusline semantics
- [ ] Claim title retrieval and open-count fallback are both implemented
- [ ] Caching strategy prevents repeated expensive subprocess calls within TTL

## Approach notes

Reference behavior from `hooks/statusline.mjs` and keep Pi extension non-blocking/fail-soft.
close_reason: Implemented runtime/beads snapshot collectors with cache, git flags, claim title, and fail-soft refresh in custom-footer

title: Pi custom-footer parity with Claude statusline
description:
## Overview

Bring Pi custom footer to feature parity with the Claude worktree statusline (`hooks/statusline.mjs`) while preserving pi-dex compatibility and XTRM issue telemetry.

## Goals

- Match the same two-line information density as Claude statusline
- Keep footer resilient when pi-dex re-applies chrome
- Preserve fast, non-blocking rendering via caching/fallbacks

## Non-goals

- No behavioral changes to beads claim lifecycle
- No fork of pi-dex in this phase (roadmap tracks long-term toggle)

## Success criteria

- [ ] Pi footer shows two lines with parity fields (model/context, host, dir, branch+git flags, venv, claim/open row)
- [ ] Works correctly with pi-dex active after session/model events
- [ ] Rendering remains responsive and non-blocking
- [ ] Tests cover field extraction/formatting and claim/open fallback behavior

## Context

- Source parity reference: `hooks/statusline.mjs`
- Target implementation: `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer/index.ts`
- Existing conflict mitigation: delayed reapply on session/model events

title: Wire cross-check subcommand into xtrm docs — CLI command, output formatter, --json flag
description:
## Context

This is the shell (orchestration) layer: it wires the boundary fetchers and core logic together into the user-facing \`xtrm docs cross-check\` CLI subcommand. Depends on all previous tasks. This task modifies the existing \`cli/src/commands/docs.ts\` file by adding a new \`.command('cross-check')\` to the \`createDocsCommand\` function.

## What to build

In \`cli/src/commands/docs.ts\`, add a new subcommand:

```typescript
docs
  .command('cross-check')
  .description('Validate docs against recent PR activity and bd issues')
  .option('--days <n>', 'Look-back window in days', '30')
  .option('--json', 'Output JSON', false)
  .action(async (opts: { days: string; json: boolean }) => {
    // 1. findRepoRoot()
    // 2. isGhAvailable() — warn if not, continue
    // 3. isBdAvailable() — warn if not, continue
    // 4. collectDocFiles(repoRoot) — existing function
    // 5. Read doc contents for reference scanning
    // 6. fetchRecentPrs() + fetchClosedBdIssues() — parallel via Promise.all
    // 7. detectStaleDocs() + detectCoverageGaps() + validateIssueReferences()
    // 8. buildReport()
    // 9. Output: --json → JSON.stringify, else formatted table
  });
```

Output formatter (human-readable):
- Header: \`N docs checked, N findings\`
- Group findings by severity using \`kleur\` colors: critical (red), warning (yellow), info (gray)
- For each finding: \`{sym} {docPath}  {message}\`
- Footer: \`✓ All docs current\` if no findings, else count by severity

Error handling:
- Any unhandled throw → \`kleur.red('✗ ...')\` + \`process.exit(1)\`
- gh/bd unavailable → warning only (proceed with partial data)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] \`xtrm docs cross-check\` is a valid subcommand (appears in \`xtrm docs --help\`)
- [ ] \`--days N\` option works, defaults to 30
- [ ] \`--json\` emits valid parseable JSON with the CrossCheckResult shape
- [ ] Human output groups findings by severity with correct colors
- [ ] Exits 0 on success (even with findings), exits 1 on internal error
- [ ] gh or bd unavailable produces warning + empty data (not crash)
- [ ] Compiles clean, lint passes

## Approach notes

Pattern for subcommand: follow \`docs.command('show')\` in docs.ts:141.
Output pattern: follow \`status.ts\` table output and \`kleur\` usage.
sym.ok / sym.warn / sym.fail from theme.ts for finding icons.
Promise.all for parallel gh + bd fetches.
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/161
close_reason: Wired cross-check subcommand into docs.ts. Added printCrossCheckReport formatter. CLI builds and runs correctly with --days and --json options.

title: Implement bd issues fetcher — query recently closed issues via bd CLI
description:
## Context

The coverage gap detector (xtrm-8jr5.3) needs a list of recently closed bd issues to check against docs. This is a second boundary layer (parallel to the gh fetcher xtrm-8jr5.2) — it shells out to the \`bd\` CLI which is already a known available tool in xtrm projects.

## What to build

A function in \`cli/src/commands/docs-cross-check-bd.ts\` (or alongside the gh fetcher):

- \`fetchClosedBdIssues(days: number): BdIssue[]\`
  - Runs: \`spawnSync('bd', ['query', \`status=closed AND updated_at>=${N_DAYS_AGO}\`, '--json'], ...)\` 
  - Or: \`spawnSync('bd', ['list', '--status=closed', '--days', String(days), '--json'], ...)\`
  - Investigate actual \`bd\` CLI flags at runtime — prefer \`bd query\` since that syntax is documented in CLAUDE.md
  - Parse JSON output into \`BdIssue[]\` array

- \`isBdAvailable(): boolean\` — \`spawnSync('bd', ['--version'], ...).status === 0\`

Error handling:
- If bd not available: return \`[]\` with warning (non-fatal)
- If bd returns non-zero: return \`[]\` with warning
- JSON parse failure: return \`[]\` with warning

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] \`fetchClosedBdIssues\` returns typed \`BdIssue[]\`, empty on failure
- [ ] Graceful fallback if bd CLI is not available
- [ ] Function is exported and importable by the CLI command
- [ ] Compiles clean

## Approach notes

Check bd CLI docs/help for the actual JSON flag. The \`bd query\` command is documented in CLAUDE.md.
Follow the same spawnSync pattern as xtrm-8jr5.2 (gh fetcher).
BdIssue needs: id, title, status, type, closedAt — filter for type=feature|task.
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/161
close_reason: Implemented docs-cross-check-bd.ts with isBdAvailable, fetchClosedBdIssues. Uses bd query with relative date. Graceful degradation on bd failure.

title: Implement cross-check core logic — staleness detector, coverage gap detector, reference validator
description:
## Context

This is the core (pure logic) layer: it takes data already fetched (doc entries, PRs, bd issues) and computes findings. No I/O. Depends on types (xtrm-8jr5.1) and can be developed in parallel with the gh fetcher (xtrm-8jr5.2) since it only takes typed inputs.

## What to build

A new file \`cli/src/commands/docs-cross-check-core.ts\` with three pure functions:

### 1. \`detectStaleDocs(docs: DocEntry[], prs: GhPr[], days: number): CrossCheckFinding[]\`
- For each doc, compare \`doc.lastModified\` against \`pr.mergedAt\` dates
- If any PR merged after \`doc.lastModified\`, emit a warning finding: \`{ severity: 'warning', kind: 'stale', docPath, message: 'Doc not updated since N PRs merged' }\`
- Also compare frontmatter \`updated_at\` (parse as Date) vs \`lastModified\` — if they differ by more than 7 days, emit \`{ severity: 'info', kind: 'stale', ... }\`

### 2. \`detectCoverageGaps(docs: DocEntry[], bdIssues: BdIssue[]): CrossCheckFinding[]\`
- For each closed bd issue with type \`feature\` or \`task\`, check if its ID appears in any doc's content (or frontmatter)
- If no doc mentions the issue ID → emit \`{ severity: 'warning', kind: 'coverage-gap', docPath: '', message: 'Feature issue <id> has no doc coverage' }\`
- Skip issues of type \`bug\`, \`chore\`, \`decision\`

### 3. \`validateIssueReferences(docs: DocEntry[], bdIssues: BdIssue[]): CrossCheckFinding[]\`
- Scan doc content for patterns matching bd issue IDs (\`xtrm-[a-z0-9]+\`)
- For each reference found, check if the issue is still open (status != closed)
- If open issue referenced in doc: emit \`{ severity: 'info', kind: 'ref-invalid', docPath, message: 'References open issue <id>' }\`
- Need to read doc file contents — accept \`docContents: Map<string, string>\` as parameter

### 4. \`buildReport(findings: CrossCheckFinding[], docsChecked: number): CrossCheckResult\`
- Aggregate all findings into CrossCheckResult shape
- Sort by severity: critical > warning > info

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All three detector functions are pure (no fs/network calls, only data in → findings out)
- [ ] Each returns \`CrossCheckFinding[]\` (empty array for no issues)
- [ ] \`buildReport\` produces a valid \`CrossCheckResult\`
- [ ] Edge cases handled: empty PR list, empty issue list, docs with no frontmatter
- [ ] Compiles clean, no \`any\` types

## Approach notes

Parse \`updated_at\` frontmatter: \`new Date(fm.updated_at)\` — handle invalid dates gracefully (isNaN check).
Issue ID regex: \`/xtrm-[a-z0-9]{4}/g\` (matches bd ID format).
This is the testable core — all logic here, no side effects.
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/161
close_reason: Implemented docs-cross-check-core.ts with detectStaleDocs, detectCoverageGaps, validateIssueReferences, buildReport. All pure functions, compiles clean.

title: Implement GitHub data fetcher — gh pr list and gh issue list boundary client
description:
## Context

The cross-check command needs to fetch recent PRs and issues from GitHub. This is the boundary layer: it crosses a system boundary (the GitHub API via gh CLI) and must handle availability failures gracefully. It depends on the types scaffold (xtrm-8jr5.1) for return shapes.

The existing pattern is in \`cli/src/commands/worktree.ts:56-68\` (getPrStatus function) — use spawnSync with gh CLI and parse JSON output.

## What to build

A new module \`cli/src/commands/docs-cross-check-gh.ts\` (or section in docs.ts) with:

- \`fetchRecentPrs(repoRoot: string, days: number): GhPr[]\` — runs \`gh pr list --state merged --limit 100 --json number,title,mergedAt,url,headRefName\`, filters to within \`days\` window
- \`fetchRecentIssues(repoRoot: string, days: number): GhIssue[]\` — runs \`gh issue list --state closed --limit 100 --json number,title,closedAt,url,labels\`
- \`isGhAvailable(): boolean\` — \`spawnSync('gh', ['--version'], { stdio: 'pipe' }).status === 0\`
- Both fetch functions return \`[]\` (not throw) if gh is unavailable or returns non-zero exit

Error handling requirements:
- If gh not installed: return [] and log a warning (\`kleur.yellow\`)
- If gh auth error (exit 4): return [] and log specific message about \`gh auth login\`
- If JSON parse fails: return [] and log warning
- Cache: no caching required for v1 (called once per cross-check run)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] \`fetchRecentPrs\` returns typed \`GhPr[]\` array, empty array on any gh failure
- [ ] \`fetchRecentIssues\` returns typed \`GhIssue[]\` array, empty on failure
- [ ] \`isGhAvailable\` correctly detects gh CLI presence
- [ ] All three functions are exported and importable
- [ ] No \`throw\` for gh unavailability — graceful degradation only
- [ ] Compiles clean

## Approach notes

Use \`spawnSync\` (same as worktree.ts:58) not \`execSync\` — simpler error handling.
Date window filter: \`new Date(pr.mergedAt) >= new Date(Date.now() - days * 86400000)\`
gh JSON fields: run \`gh pr list --help\` to confirm available \`--json\` fields.
notes: PR: https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools/pull/161
close_reason: Implemented docs-cross-check-gh.ts with fetchRecentPrs, fetchRecentIssues, isGhAvailable. All functions gracefully degrade on gh CLI failure. Compiles clean.

title: Implement xtrm docs list subcommand with table and JSON output
description:
## Context

This is the P2 shell/integration task that wires the scanner (xtrm-vwp0.2) and cache (xtrm-vwp0.3) together into a user-facing CLI subcommand. The existing docs.ts has a show subcommand; list is a new sibling that produces a columnar table output rather than the verbose per-file format.

## What to build

Add to cli/src/commands/docs.ts a new subcommand list via docs.command('list'):

Options:
- --dir <path>: filter to files under this directory (relative to repo root)
- --pattern <glob>: filter by filename pattern (substring match for phase 1)
- --filter <field=value>: filter by frontmatter field, e.g. --filter type=service
- --json: output as JSON array
- --no-cache: bypass cache and force fresh scan

Table output (using cli-table3, already a dep):
- Columns: Path | Size | Modified | Title (from frontmatter.title or "—") | Type (frontmatter.type or "—")
- Row color: use kleur.gray for files without frontmatter
- Footer: total count + "(N without frontmatter)" + "(cached)" indicator if from cache

JSON output:
- Same shape as existing docs show --json: array of { path, sizeBytes, lastModified, frontmatter, parseError }

Error handling:
- If no .md files found: print kleur.yellow message and exit 0
- If repoRoot cannot be found: throw (existing pattern in all other commands)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] xtrm docs list outputs a table of all .md files
- [ ] --dir, --pattern, --filter flags narrow results correctly
- [ ] --json outputs valid JSON array matching schema
- [ ] --no-cache forces fresh scan (skips cache read)
- [ ] Cache is used on second invocation within TTL
- [ ] Table footer shows count and cache status
- [ ] tsc --noEmit passes, eslint clean

## Approach notes

- Look at cli/src/commands/status.ts lines 91-123 for cli-table3 usage pattern
- Follow the same Command builder pattern: const list = docs.command('list [filter]').option(...)
- Import scanDocFiles from utils/docs-scanner.ts and cache utils from utils/docs-cache.ts
- This is the boundary/shell layer: all pure logic lives in the utils, this file only wires + formats
- Depends on: xtrm-vwp0.2 (scanner) and xtrm-vwp0.3 (cache)
close_reason: Added xtrm docs list subcommand with --dir/--pattern/--filter/--json/--no-cache; cli-table3 table; cache integration; smoke tested. Tests vwp0.5+vwp0.6 pending separately.

title: Define TypeScript types and interfaces for docs cross-check
description:
## Context

Before any implementation can start, the shared data shapes must exist. This is the scaffolding task that all other cross-check tasks depend on. It establishes the contract between the boundary layer (gh fetcher, bd fetcher), the core logic, and the CLI output formatter.

## What to build

A new file \`cli/src/commands/docs-cross-check-types.ts\` (or inline in \`docs.ts\`) containing:

- \`GhPr\` — shape of a GitHub PR from \`gh pr list --json\`: \`{ number, title, mergedAt, url, headRefName, files? }\`
- \`GhIssue\` — shape from \`gh issue list --json\`: \`{ number, title, closedAt, url, labels }\`
- \`BdIssue\` — minimal bd issue shape: \`{ id, title, status, closedAt, type }\`
- \`DocEntry\` — already exists in docs.ts, extend with \`mentionedIssueIds: string[]\`
- \`CrossCheckFinding\` — a single detected problem: \`{ severity: 'critical'|'warning'|'info', kind: 'stale'|'coverage-gap'|'ref-invalid', docPath: string, message: string, detail?: string }\`
- \`CrossCheckResult\` — top-level output: \`{ docsChecked: number, findingsTotal: number, findings: CrossCheckFinding[], generatedAt: string }\`
- \`CrossCheckOptions\` — CLI options bag: \`{ json: boolean, days: number, repoRoot: string }\`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All types exported from a single location (either new file or the docs.ts module)
- [ ] No runtime code — types/interfaces only (compile-time only)
- [ ] Compiles clean: \`tsc --noEmit\` passes
- [ ] Types are imported correctly by at least one stub usage in docs.ts or a placeholder comment

## Approach notes

Follow the pattern in \`cli/src/types/models.ts\` for interface shape conventions.
Keep types in the same file as the command if small, or extract to \`cli/src/commands/docs-cross-check-types.ts\`.
DocEntry already defined at docs.ts:12 — extend it rather than redeclaring.
close_reason: Created docs-cross-check-types.ts with GhPr, GhIssue, BdIssue, DocCrossCheckEntry, CrossCheckFinding, CrossCheckResult, CrossCheckOptions; tsc clean

title: xtrm docs cross-check: validate docs against PR activity and bd issues
description:
## Overview

The \`xtrm docs cross-check\` command validates project documentation against real-world activity: recently merged PRs, closed bd issues, and doc filesystem timestamps. It detects stale docs, coverage gaps (features shipped without docs), and dangling issue references — then outputs a severity-grouped report.

This is a subcommand of \`xtrm docs\` and builds on the existing \`docs show\` infrastructure in \`cli/src/commands/docs.ts\`.

## Goals

- Goal 1: Surface stale docs (last modified before recent PRs that touched related code)
- Goal 2: Detect coverage gaps — closed bd feature issues with no corresponding doc update
- Goal 3: Validate issue references embedded in docs (referenced IDs exist and are in expected state)
- Goal 4: Emit JSON-compatible output for CI pipelines

## Non-goals

- Not a doc writer — only reports, does not generate or update documentation
- Not a full Git blame analysis — uses file mtime and frontmatter \`updated_at\` only
- No automatic remediation (no --fix flag in v1)

## Success criteria

- [ ] \`xtrm docs cross-check\` runs end-to-end against a real repo and produces a severity-grouped report
- [ ] Works offline gracefully (gh CLI unavailable → warning, not crash)
- [ ] --json flag emits parseable JSON
- [ ] Unit tests for all core logic functions pass
- [ ] CLI integration test verifies exit codes and output shape

## Context / background

Parent: xtrm-uc0e (existing task with full requirements spec)
Related code: cli/src/commands/docs.ts (createDocsCommand), cli/src/commands/worktree.ts (gh CLI pattern with spawnSync)
gh CLI pattern: spawnSync('gh', [..., '--json', ...], { encoding: 'utf8', stdio: 'pipe' })
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Add doc scan result cache layer (file-backed, TTL-based)
description:
## Context

Scanning a large project tree for .md files on every invocation of xtrm docs list is slow. The list command should cache results to a JSON file in .xtrm/cache/docs-list.json and serve cached results when the cache is fresh (TTL: 60 seconds by default, configurable via --no-cache flag). Cache invalidation happens when any .md file's mtime is newer than the cache timestamp.

This is P1 core work: pure I/O logic with no CLI wiring. The cache module computes a freshness check, reads/writes the cache file, and the list command decides whether to use it.

## What to build

Add to cli/src/utils/docs-cache.ts:

- DocCache interface: { timestamp: number; repoRoot: string; entries: DocEntry[] }
- readCache(repoRoot: string): Promise<DocCache | null> — reads .xtrm/cache/docs-list.json; returns null if missing/corrupt
- writeCache(repoRoot: string, entries: DocEntry[]): Promise<void> — writes cache
- isCacheValid(cache: DocCache, entries: DocEntry[], ttlMs?: number): boolean
  - Returns false if cache age > ttlMs (default 60_000ms)
  - Returns false if any entry's lastModified > cache.timestamp
  - Returns true otherwise

The list command will:
1. Call scanDocFiles()
2. Try readCache() — if valid, skip re-parsing frontmatter for unchanged files
3. Write updated cache via writeCache()

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] cli/src/utils/docs-cache.ts exists with exported readCache, writeCache, isCacheValid
- [ ] Cache file written to .xtrm/cache/docs-list.json relative to repoRoot
- [ ] isCacheValid returns false when TTL expires
- [ ] isCacheValid returns false when any file is newer than cache timestamp
- [ ] writeCache creates parent directories if missing
- [ ] tsc --noEmit passes

## Approach notes

- Use fs-extra.ensureDir for creating .xtrm/cache/
- Cache structure: store serialized DocEntry[] including frontmatter; mtime stored as ISO string
- The conf package (already a dependency) could also be used but file-based is simpler for this case
- This task depends on xtrm-vwp0.1 (DocEntry type) but can be developed in parallel with xtrm-vwp0.2 (scanner)
close_reason: Added docs-cache.ts with readCache/writeCache/isCacheValid; TTL+mtime invalidation; cache at .xtrm/cache/docs-list.json; tsc clean

title: Implement recursive doc file scanner with .gitignore support
description:
## Context

The current collectDocFiles() in docs.ts only scans a hardcoded set of files (README.md, CHANGELOG.md) plus the top-level docs/ directory (shallow, non-recursive). The list command needs to scan the entire project tree, respecting .gitignore and skipping build artifacts and hidden dirs.

This is P1 core work: pure I/O logic that transforms a repo root path into an array of DocEntry objects. No CLI wiring yet — just the scanner function that the list command will call.

## What to build

Add to cli/src/utils/docs-scanner.ts:

- scanDocFiles(repoRoot: string, options: ScanOptions): Promise<DocEntry[]>
  - ScanOptions: { dir?: string; pattern?: string; filter?: FrontmatterFilter; recursive?: boolean }
  - Recursively walks the directory (default: project root), collecting all .md files
  - Respects .gitignore: read .gitignore at repoRoot, skip matching paths (use minimatch or manual prefix matching — no new deps needed)
  - Always skips: node_modules/, .git/, dist/, .xtrm/ directories
  - Applies dir filter (prefix match on relativePath) and pattern filter (glob/substring match on filename)
  - Applies FrontmatterFilter if provided: reads frontmatter and checks field === value
  - Populates DocEntry with filePath, relativePath, sizeBytes, lastModified, frontmatter (parsed), and summary (first paragraph)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] scanDocFiles() returns all .md files recursively from repoRoot
- [ ] Files matching .gitignore patterns are excluded
- [ ] node_modules/, .git/, dist/ are always excluded
- [ ] --dir filter narrows results to a directory prefix
- [ ] --pattern filter narrows by filename substring/glob
- [ ] --filter field=value narrows by frontmatter field
- [ ] tsc --noEmit passes with no new external dependencies

## Approach notes

- No new npm deps: use fs-extra (already available) for file I/O; implement .gitignore parsing with string prefix matching (the patterns in typical .gitignore are simple)
- minimatch is NOT in package.json — avoid it; implement simple glob matching or use substring for phase 1
- cli/src/utils/repo-root.ts shows the fs-extra walkUp pattern to follow
- DocEntry is defined in xtrm-vwp0.1 — this task depends on that scaffold
close_reason: Added scanDocFiles() + ScanOptions + gitignore support to docs-scanner.ts; recursive walk, ALWAYS_SKIP dirs, pattern/dir/FrontmatterFilter support; no new deps; tsc clean

title: Extract and harden frontmatter parser + DocEntry types in docs.ts
description:
## Context

The existing docs.ts contains an inline parseFrontmatter() function and DocEntry interface defined locally in the command file. Before the list subcommand can be built, these need to be extracted into a reusable utility module (cli/src/utils/docs-scanner.ts) so the core parsing logic can be independently tested and reused across future docs subcommands.

This is P0 scaffold work — the list subcommand, cache layer, and file scanner all depend on a shared DocEntry type and a robust frontmatter parser.

## What to build

- Move parseFrontmatter() and DocEntry interface out of docs.ts and into cli/src/utils/docs-scanner.ts
- Extend Frontmatter interface to capture a summary (first non-empty paragraph after frontmatter block, up to 120 chars) as an optional field
- Add FrontmatterFilter type: { field: string; value: string } for use by the list command
- Update docs.ts to import from the new util (no behaviour change, all existing show command logic preserved)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] cli/src/utils/docs-scanner.ts exists with exported parseFrontmatter, DocEntry, FrontmatterFilter
- [ ] docs.ts imports parseFrontmatter and DocEntry from docs-scanner.ts
- [ ] Existing xtrm docs show command still works after refactor
- [ ] tsc --noEmit passes

## Approach notes

- File to edit: cli/src/commands/docs.ts (lines 8-37 — the inline types and parser)
- New file: cli/src/utils/docs-scanner.ts
- Pattern to follow: see cli/src/utils/theme.ts for a minimal named-export util module
- Keep parseFrontmatter pure (no fs calls) — takes string, returns Frontmatter | null
close_reason: Extracted parseFrontmatter, DocEntry, Frontmatter, FrontmatterFilter to cli/src/utils/docs-scanner.ts; added summary extraction; docs.ts updated to import from new util; tsc clean, build passing

title: Create planning skill — structured bd-integrated planning workflow for Claude Code
description:
## Why

xtrm needs a Claude Code planning skill that mirrors the Pi plan-mode extension but is idiomatic to Claude Code. The Pi extension handles UI-level plan mode (tool gating, widgets, keyboard shortcuts). The skill handles the *methodology*: how to do exploration, structure issues, document them well, and integrate test-planning.

## What to build

A comprehensive skills/planning/SKILL.md that:
- Guides structured read-only exploration (GitNexus + Serena) before planning
- Uses XML/CoT patterns for the planning thinking phase
- Creates well-documented bd issues (epic + tasks) with rich descriptions
- Calls test-planning after the issue board is created
- Enables clean handoff (claim first issue, start implementation)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Skill file at skills/planning/SKILL.md
- [ ] Covers all 6 phases: clarify → explore → structure → create issues → test-planning → handoff
- [ ] Issue description templates with context/AC/notes fields
- [ ] Multishot examples (feature, bug, refactor)
- [ ] Explicitly invokes test-planning at the right moment
- [ ] Under 500 lines

## Notes

Parent epic xr9b has the Pi plan-mode extension (mostly done). This skill is the Claude Code counterpart — same concepts, different runtime idioms.
close_reason: Done: skills/planning/SKILL.md created — 6-phase workflow with bd integration, test-planning hook, CoT structure, multishot examples

title: fix(worktree): resolveStatuslineScript returns path to nonexistent file on fresh machines
description: The fallback in resolveStatuslineScript() always returns ~/.claude/hooks/statusline.mjs even when that file doesn't exist. On fresh installs, neither the plugin cache path nor the fallback exist, so Claude Code tries to run a missing file and silently fails. Fix: add existsSync checks to both paths, return null when neither exists.

title: [DRAFT] CLI Refactor: simplify help output, fix docs vs implementation drift
description:
## Overview

Major CLI cleanup to:
1. Simplify help output (match bd/overstory style - flat list, no fancy tables)
2. Fix all documentation vs implementation discrepancies
3. Remove or implement `install project` command

## Problems Identified

### 1. Help Output Too Fancy
Current: Multi-section output with tables, colors, skills listing (30 items)
Target: Simple flat list like `bd --help` or `overstory --help`

### 2. Version Drift (13 versions behind)
- README.md: 0.5.10
- XTRM-GUIDE.md: 0.5.10

title: [DRAFT] Specialist Integration: Plan Mode + Explore + Test-Planning

## Overview

Second specialist integration in the xtrm project. This combines three specialist roles into a cohesive plan-mode workflow.

## Context

There's existing plan-mode work for Pi, but it's buggy, heavy, and mostly not needed with this new implementation. Some parts can be extracted and reused.

## Plan Mode Workflow

### Phase 1: Epic Creation
- Creates the epic with full detail
- Breaks down all steps/subtasks
- Links dependencies and blockers
- Estimates effort where possible

### Phase 2: Explore Specialist
- **Replaces** canonical Claude Code explore agents
- Deep codebase exploration and context gathering
- Identifies affected symbols, execution flows, risk areas
- Uses GitNexus for graph-based exploration

### Phase 3: Test-Planning Specialist
- Runs after epic is created
- Suggests test locations and types (unit, integration, e2e)
- Identifies coverage gaps
- Creates companion test issues or AC gates

## Implementation Notes

- Extract reusable parts from existing Pi plan-mode implementation
- Consider integration with bd workflow (auto-create issues from plan)
- May need hook triggers for PlanModeStart/PlanModeEnd events
- Explore specialist should leverage gitnexus-query, gitnexus-context

## Status: DRAFT

This issue is a draft pending further exploration. The Pi plan-mode implementation should be audited for salvageable components before scoping this work.

title: Fix Pi extension installer: register each extension via pi install -l after copy (u8f0 follow-up)
description:
After u8f0 converted Pi extensions from flat .ts files to directory packages with package.json, the installer behaviour needs two fixes.

## Problem 1: pi install -l not run per extension

pi-install.ts (runPiInstall) does fs.copy(extensionsSrc, extensionsDst) which copies the tree to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/. But Pi doesn't auto-discover from that path — each extension needs to be registered in ~/.pi/settings.json via:

  pi install -l ~/.pi/agent/extensions/<name>

Without this step, extensions have the correct package.json but are never loaded by Pi.

Fix: after the fs.copy, iterate over each extension directory in extensionsDst and run spawnSync('pi', ['install', '-l', extPath]) for each one that has a package.json.

## Problem 2: diffPiExtensions counts .ts files, not extension packages

diffPiExtensions in install-pi.ts uses listTsFilesRecursive which enumerates all .ts files. Now that extensions are directories, the right unit to diff is the extension package (presence of <name>/package.json + hash of <name>/index.ts), not individual .ts files.

Fix: rewrite diffPiExtensions to enumerate directories in sourceDir that contain a package.json and compare index.ts hashes (and package.json hashes) rather than walking all .ts files.

## Files to change
- cli/src/commands/pi-install.ts — add pi install -l loop after copy
- cli/src/commands/install-pi.ts — rewrite diffPiExtensions to be directory-aware

## Testing
- xt pi status shows correct missing/stale counts after the fix
- xt pi install registers extensions so pi can load them
close_reason: Fixed in pi-install.ts (pi install -l per extension after copy) and install-pi.ts (diffPiExtensions now directory-aware with package.json+index.ts hash)

title: xt-end skill: autonomous session close flow for agents
description:
Create skills/xt-end/SKILL.md skill that guides an agent through the full session close flow autonomously. The skill wraps 'xt end' CLI but handles all edge cases the CLI cannot: pre-flight checks, conflict resolution guidance, and decision-making.

SKILL FLOW:
1. Pre-flight: check bd list --status=in_progress. If any open/in_progress issues remain, guide agent to close them first with 'bd close <id> --reason "..."'. Do not proceed until clean.
2. Uncommitted changes: run git status. If dirty, agent decides: commit with meaningful message (git add -A && git commit -m '...') or stash. Never run xt end with uncommitted work.
3. Run: xt end --dry-run first, show agent the PR preview (title, body, files). Agent confirms or adjusts.
4. Run: xt end (actual). If rebase conflict: skill guides agent through 'git status', resolve conflicts, 'git add', 'git rebase --continue', then re-run xt end.
5. Worktree cleanup: skill asks agent to decide -- keep worktree (for follow-up) or remove. Default recommendation: remove if PR opened cleanly.
6. Report: confirm PR URL, remind agent to monitor CI.

SKILL TRIGGERS:
- Agent says 'done', 'finished', 'wrap up', 'close session', 'ship it'
- All beads issues in session are closed
- User runs /xt-end explicitly

INSTALL: lives in skills/xt-end/SKILL.md, symlinked to .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/ by xtrm install.

DEPENDS ON: xtrm-ua3z (xt end CLI must exist first).
close_reason: Created skills/xt-end/SKILL.md: pre-flight checks, uncommitted-changes handling, dry-run preview, xt end execution with rebase conflict guidance, worktree cleanup, PR report; triggers on done/finished/wrap-up/ship-it signals

title: xt end: session close flow — rebase, push, open PR, beads linkage, cleanup
description: Implement 'xt end [--squash] [--draft] [--keep]' as the canonical session close command. Replaces deprecated xtrm finish. Must be run from inside an xt worktree (errors if run from main checkout). Flow: (1) Gate check: all beads issues closed? uncommitted changes staged? Abort if not. (2) Rebase: git rebase origin/main. If conflicts: report cleanly, leave worktree alive, abort xt end — session is not over until branch is clean. User or specialist resolves conflicts, then re-runs xt end. (3) Push: git push origin <branch> --force-with-lease (safe after rebase). (4) PR: open PR via gh pr create. Title derived from beads close reason(s). Body auto-generated from: issue IDs + descriptions, commit log summary, git diff --stat. (5) Beads linkage: bd update <id> --notes 'PR: <url>' for each closed issue in this session. (6) Cleanup: prompt to remove local worktree (git worktree remove <path>) unless --keep. Default: keep until user confirms. (7) Report: PR URL + CI status link. Merge: CI-driven auto-merge on green. mergeTarget configurable in xt.config.json (default: main, optional: merge-queue).
notes:
PR AUTO-GENERATION SPEC (mechanical, no LLM needed):
Sources: bd list --status=closed (session-scoped), git log origin/main..HEAD, git diff origin/main --stat, branch name.

Title: single issue → use close reason. Multiple → '<first reason> (+N more)'.

Body template:
## What
<issue title + description per closed issue>

## Why
<close reason(s) per issue>

## Changes
<git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline, max 20 lines>

## Files changed
<git diff origin/main --stat>

Closes: <space-separated issue IDs>

All data comes from bd show --json + git commands. No LLM call in xt end CLI itself — that lives in the skill layer.
close_reason: Implemented xt end in commands/end.ts: worktree gate (branch xt/*), no-uncommitted-changes gate, collect issue IDs from commit log, rebase origin/main, push --force-with-lease, gh pr create with mechanical body, bd update --notes PR URL, optional worktree removal

title: xt claude / xt pi: runtime management subcommands (install|status|doctor|reload)
description: Implement runtime management subcommands for both runtimes. xt claude install: install/refresh Claude plugin + official plugins (extract from current runGlobalInstall Claude path). xt claude status: is plugin installed? which version? are hooks wired and enabled? xt claude doctor: is claude CLI available? plugin enabled? beads connected? hooks firing (dry-run hook test)? xt claude reload: uninstall + reinstall plugin to refresh cached copy from live repo. xt pi install: copy extensions to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/ + run pi install for each package in install-schema.json (non-interactive, always runs as part of xtrm install). xt pi setup: interactive first-time only — API keys (DashScope/ZAI) + OAuth instructions. xt pi status: extension drift check (current --check logic from createInstallPiCommand). xt pi doctor: pi installed? extensions deployed? packages present? xt pi reload: re-copy extensions + reinstall packages.
close_reason: Created claude.ts (xt claude install/status/doctor/reload), pi.ts (xt pi install/setup/status/doctor/reload); registered in index.ts; exported installPlugin from install.ts; pi setup reuses createInstallPiCommand logic; pi install calls runPiInstall

title: xt claude / xt pi: session launcher with worktree + Dolt bootstrap
description: Implement bare 'xt claude [name]' and 'xt pi [name]' as session launchers. Flow: (1) Resolve worktree location: sibling to CWD, named '<cwd-basename>-xt-claude-<shortdate>' (e.g. xtrm-tools-xt-claude-20260320). For pi: '-xt-pi-' infix. (2) Resolve branch: 'xt/<name>' if name arg provided, else 'xt/<4-char-random>' fallback. (3) git worktree add <path> <branch> — create branch if new. (4) Dolt bootstrap: cd into worktree, run 'bd dolt stop', read main checkout .beads/dolt-server.port, write same port to worktree .beads/dolt-server.port. This redirects the worktree to the canonical beads db. (5) Launch: exec 'claude' (or 'pi') with cwd set to the worktree. No issue binding at this level — issue tracking is the specialists system's concern. If no .beads/ exists in repo, skip Dolt bootstrap silently.
close_reason: Implemented launchWorktreeSession in utils/worktree-session.ts; wired as default action on xt claude [name] and xt pi [name]; creates sibling worktree <basename>-xt-<runtime>-<date>, branch xt/<name|random4>, Dolt bootstrap via bd dolt stop + port redirect, then execs claude/pi in worktree

title: xt CLI redesign: unified session launcher + runtime management
description:
Full redesign of the xtrm/xt CLI to be a session-first launcher, not just an installer. Scope: everything up to but NOT including xt sp (specialists integration is a separate epic).

DECIDED DESIGN:

Runtime targets: Claude and Pi only. Gemini removed.

xtrm install (global, one-time):
  - Claude: claude plugin install xtrm-tools + official plugins
  - Pi: copy extensions to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/ + pi install packages (non-interactive)
  - Skills: sync to ~/.agents/skills/
  - System deps: beads+dolt, gitnexus

xt init (project bootstrap, one-time per repo):
  - bd init, gitnexus init, service registry, CLAUDE.md injection, project MCP sync

xt claude [name] / xt pi [name] (session launcher):
  - Creates sibling worktree: <parent>/<cwd-basename>-xt-claude-<shortdate>
  - Branch: xt/<name> if provided, xt/<4-char-random> fallback
  - Applies Dolt bootstrap (redirect to canonical beads db)
  - Launches claude/pi in the worktree
  - No issue binding — issue tracking is specialists system concern

xt claude/pi install|status|doctor|reload|setup (runtime management)

xt end (session close):
  - Gate: all issues closed? clean working tree?
  - Rebase on origin/main (conflicts abort, leave worktree alive)
  - Push branch, open PR (title/body from beads close reasons + commits)
  - Link PR to beads issues
  - Prompt worktree cleanup
  - Merge: CI-driven. mergeTarget: main (default) or merge-queue (xt.config.json)

xt worktree list|clean|remove (worktree lifecycle management)

xt finish: DEPRECATED, replaced by xt end

Pi packages (canonical): pi-gitnexus, pi-serena-tools, @zenobius/pi-worktrees, @robhowley/pi-structured-return
Pi packages (removed): @aliou/pi-guardrails (breaks non-interactive work)

CHILD ISSUES:
xtrm-in5e   xt alias in package.json
xtrm-m54d   add pi-structured-return
xtrm-x87v   remove pi-guardrails
xtrm-6gpf   remove Gemini, scope sync to skills only
xtrm-c1qd   collapse install all/basic to single install
xtrm-j2jk   remove install project + project namespace
xtrm-a875   rename init.ts + Pi install canonicalization
xtrm-2q8j   xt claude/pi session launcher + worktree + Dolt bootstrap
xtrm-bjvn   xt claude/pi runtime management subcommands
xtrm-ua3z   xt end close flow
xtrm-c5pi   xt worktree list/clean

OPEN QUESTIONS (not yet decided):
- xt end: squash policy (preserve commits vs --squash flag)
- xt end: worktree cleanup default (keep until merged, or prompt immediately after PR)
- xt end: rebase vs merge strategy
close_reason: All implementation issues (in5e, m54d, x87v, 6gpf, c1qd, j2jk, a875, bjvn, 2q8j, ua3z, o8tp, c5pi) and test issues (7lut, pqmp, rvvt, lzag) complete. xt CLI redesign shipped.

title: Main-guard blocks read-only Bash commands (cat, ls, head, tail, echo)
description:
## Problem
Main-guard.mjs blocks Bash commands like `cat`, `ls`, `head`, `tail`, `echo` on protected branches because they're not in SAFE_BASH_PREFIXES.

## Expected
Read-only commands should be allowed on protected branches.

## Current Behavior
```
⛔ Bash restricted on 'main'. Allowed: git status/log/diff/pull/stash, gh, bd.
```

## Fix
Add read-only commands to SAFE_BASH_PREFIXES in guard-rules.mjs:
- cat
- ls
- head
- tail
- echo
- pwd
- which
- type
- env
- printenv

Or add a READ_ONLY_BASH_PATTERNS list that's always allowed.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #114

comments.author: you@example.com
comments.text:
## Session-Flow Test Report — p2aq

### 1) Metadata
- Issue ID: jaggers-agent-tools-p2aq
- Runtime: Claude (claude-sonnet-4-6)
- Tester: Claude Code (this session)
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Branch/Commit tested: main @ e281670
- Environment: Linux 6.18.13-200.fc43.x86_64, Node.js v25.2.1

---

### 2) Scenarios

#### A. Claim flow — PARTIAL PASS ⚠️

**Command:** `bd update p2aq --claim` (via Bash, allowed by main guard)

**Expected:**
- claim recognized
- worktree created at `.worktrees/p2aq`
- `.xtrm-session-state.json` written with phase=claimed for p2aq

**Observed:**
- `bd update p2aq --claim` returned error: "Error claiming p2aq: issue already claimed by you@example.com" (exit 1)
- `beads-claim-sync` PostToolUse hook fires regardless of exit code per design ✓
- `bd kv set claimed:<sessionId> p2aq` should have been called — no visible kv entry for p2aq under any recognizable UUID in current kv list
- Worktree NOT created for p2aq: `git worktree list` shows only `hehp` and `xb13`
- `.xtrm-session-state.json` shows hehp (issueId: jaggers-agent-tools-hehp, phase: claimed, startedAt: 2026-03-18T19:25:44.332Z) — was NOT overwritten for p2aq

**Root cause hypothesis:** beads-claim-sync either (a) failed silently at `bd kv set` for the current session_id, or (b) the current session_id was missing from hook input (causes early exit 0 via stderr warning). In both cases, `ensureWorktreeForClaim` never ran.

**Pass/Fail:** FAIL — state file not updated, worktree not created for active issue

---

#### B. Stop gate phase behavior — PASS (by code analysis)

**Verification method:** Full read of beads-stop-gate.mjs + beads-gate-core.mjs + session-state.mjs

**Two-layer evaluation confirmed:**
1. decideStopGate: kv claim + in_progress → block (unclosed_claim)
2. evaluateSessionState: phase-based

| Phase | Expected | Code verdict |
|---|---|---|
| claimed | warn only | ✓ stopWarnActiveWorktreeMessage, allow: true |
| phase1-done | warn only | ✓ same branch |
| waiting-merge | block | ✓ stopBlockWaitingMergeMessage, allow: false |
| pending-cleanup | block | ✓ same branch |
| conflicting | block w/ files | ✓ stopBlockConflictingMessage, allow: false |
| cleanup-done | allow | ✓ allow: true |
| merged | allow | ✓ allow: true |

**Note:** If beads-claim-sync fails to set the kv (per Scenario A defect), the kv check passes and only session state is evaluated. Current session state shows hehp/claimed → warning only, NOT a block. p2aq work would not trigger a hard stop. This is a gap introduced by the Scenario A defect.

**Pass/Fail:** PASS (logic correct) — but residual risk from Scenario A propagates here

---

#### C. Compact continuity — PASS (by code analysis)

**Verification method:** Full read of beads-compact-save.mjs + beads-compact-restore.mjs

**PreCompact (save):**
- Runs `bd list --status=in_progress`, parses IDs with regex `/^[○◐●✓❄]\s+([\w-]+)\s/u`
- Reads `readSessionState(cwd)` → includes full sessionState in bundle
- Writes `.beads/.last_active` as JSON bundle with {ids, sessionState, savedAt}
- For waiting-merge phase: injects `additionalSystemPrompt` warning about pending PR ✓
- No `.beads/.last_active` exists now (compact not triggered this session) ✓

**SessionStart (restore):**
- Reads bundle, calls `bd update <id> --status in_progress` for each ID ✓
- Calls `writeSessionState(sessionState, {cwd})` to restore file ✓
- For waiting-merge/pending-cleanup: injects RESUME guidance with PR number + worktree path ✓
- Deletes `.last_active` after restore (clean-up) ✓
- Backward compat: handles legacy newline format ✓

**Pass/Fail:** PASS

---

#### D. Main guard + beads gates regression — PASS ✅

**Main guard (observed at runtime):**
- Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit/mcp__serena__* → BLOCKED on main ✓ (observed: write attempt blocked)
- Bash (general): BLOCKED on main ✓ (observed: multiple blocks this session for ls, cat)
- Bash (bd/git-read/gh): ALLOWED ✓ (observed throughout session)
- git commit on main: BLOCKED with specific message ✓ (code verified)
- git push to main: BLOCKED ✓ (code verified)
- git push to feature branch: ALLOWED ✓ (code verified)
- gh pr merge without --squash: BLOCKED ✓ (code verified)
- MAIN_GUARD_ALLOW_BASH=1 override: present ✓
- MAIN_GUARD_PROTECTED_BRANCHES env override: present ✓
- git worktree commands: ALLOWED (in SAFE_BASH_PREFIXES) ✓
- mcp__serena__ rename/replace/insert tools: in WRITE_TOOLS — blocked on main ✓

**Beads edit gate:**
- Blocks when session has no kv claim + totalWork > 0 ✓
- Allows when claimed ✓
- Fails open if bd unavailable ✓

**Beads commit gate:**
- Blocks git commit when session has unclosed claim + in_progress issues ✓
- Allows when claim cleared (bd close fires beads-claim-sync auto-clear) ✓

**Beads memory gate:**
- Fires at Stop when claimed issue was closed this session ✓
- marker file `.beads/.memory-gate-done` mechanism working ✓

**Pass/Fail:** PASS — no regressions

---

### 3) Hook/Event Firing Log

| Event | Hook | Observed |
|---|---|---|
| PreToolUse:Bash (ls) | main-guard.mjs | ✅ BLOCKED — 'Bash restricted on main' |
| PreToolUse:Bash (bd update) | main-guard.mjs | ✅ ALLOWED (bd in SAFE_BASH_PREFIXES) |
| PreToolUse:Write | main-guard.mjs | ✅ BLOCKED (WRITE_TOOLS) |
| PostToolUse:Bash (bd update --claim) | beads-claim-sync.mjs | ⚠️ Fired but kv/state not confirmed set |
| Stop | beads-stop-gate.mjs | Not triggered (session still active) |
| Stop | beads-memory-gate.mjs | Not triggered (claim not closed) |
| PreCompact | beads-compact-save.mjs | Not triggered this session |
| SessionStart | beads-compact-restore.mjs | Not triggered (.last_active absent) |

---

### 4) Defects Found

**D1 — Claim-sync state not applied for p2aq (MEDIUM)**
- Summary: After `bd update p2aq --claim`, .xtrm-session-state.json was not updated to p2aq and no worktree was created.
- Repro: Run `bd update <id> --claim` when issue is already bd-claimed by previous session. Observe session state file still shows prior issue.
- Expected: beads-claim-sync writes session state and creates worktree regardless of bd update exit code.
- Actual: State file shows hehp (prior session), no p2aq worktree.
- Possible cause: session_id absent from hook input, OR kv set failed for this session's ID.
- Severity: Medium — workflow incomplete, stop gate warning-only instead of blocking for p2aq
- Suggested fix: Add debug logging to beads-claim-sync stderr when session_id is missing; verify hook receives session_id.

**D2 — KV injection value stored (LOW)**
- Summary: `claimed:93dd5da0-ec62-43a0-a002-9821f5a13278 = $(bd` — a shell subexpression was stored as a kv value.
- Not exploitable (value read as string, never eval'd), but indicates no input validation on kv values.
- Severity: Low

**D3 — Stale placeholder in kv (INFO)**
- Summary: `claimed:b716dc0b-5734-470b-a6e8-766b23029cd5 = <id>` — literal template placeholder stored.
- Indicates agent followed an instruction template literally rather than substituting the actual issue ID.
- Severity: Info

---

### 5) Final Verdict

**Overall: PASS WITH RISKS**

- Main guard: fully operational, all expected blocks/allows confirmed ✓
- Stop gate: phase logic correct, messages appropriate ✓
- Compact save/restore: logic sound, sessionState preserved ✓
- Memory gate: logic correct ✓
- Edit/commit gates: logic correct ✓

**Residual risks:**
1. Claim-sync state write failure (D1): if beads-claim-sync silently fails to set kv or write session state, the stop gate degrades from BLOCK to WARN for the active session. The p2aq claim in this test session is unconfirmed in kv — stop gate may not block.
2. Session state file is single-file at repo root — concurrent worktree sessions (hehp + p2aq) will overwrite each other's state.

**Recommended next action:**
- Investigate D1: add session_id presence check logging to beads-claim-sync, reproduce in isolation to confirm if session_id is being passed to PostToolUse hooks.
- Consider session-scoped state files (e.g. `.beads/session-state-<sessionId>.json`) to avoid cross-session clobbering.

comments.author: you@example.com
comments.text:
**ADDENDUM — Stop gate runtime confirmation**

The Stop hook fired and blocked this session with the expected message:
- Showed both in-progress issues (hehp + p2aq)
- Triggered `stopBlockMessage` format (kv-level block, not session-state warning)

This corrects the D1 residual risk assessment: the stop gate did NOT degrade to warning-only. It blocked correctly, meaning either (a) `bd kv set claimed:<sessionId> p2aq` succeeded (kv claim was set), or (b) the no-session_id global fallback triggered showing all in_progress issues.

**Updated Scenario B verdict: PASS ✅ (confirmed at runtime, not just code analysis)**

D1 stands only for the session-state-file / worktree aspect: .xtrm-session-state.json was not updated to p2aq and no worktree was created. The kv claim path is unaffected.

**Overall verdict unchanged: PASS WITH RISKS** — residual risk is the single session-state file being shared between concurrent worktree sessions (hehp + p2aq), not the stop gate behavior.

title: Design CLI architecture and TypeScript module structure
description:
Design and implement the CLI architecture for documentation commands following xtrm-cli patterns.

**Requirements:**
- Create `cli/commands/docs/` directory structure
- Design command hierarchy: `xtrm docs {list,verify,show,cross-check}`
- Implement shared utilities module (`cli/lib/docs-utils.ts`) for:
  - Frontmatter parsing (gray-matter integration)
  - File scanning and filtering
  - Drift detection algorithms
  - Cross-reference validation
  - Report generation
  - GitHub API integration
  - Caching layer

**Architecture Decisions:**
- TypeScript strict mode
- Follow existing xtrm-cli patterns (Commander.js or similar)
- Reuse existing spinner, color, table utilities
- Error handling with proper exit codes
- Configuration support in .xtrm/config.json

**Shared Types:**


**Implementation:**
- Base command class with common functionality
- Individual command classes extending base
- Unit tests for utilities
- Integration tests for commands
- Help text and examples for each command

title: Implement xtrm docs cross-check command - Validate docs against PRs and issues
description:
Create `xtrm docs cross-check` command to validate documentation against latest PR activity and closed bd issues.

**Requirements:**
- Fetch latest PR merge/closed dates from GitHub API (gh CLI)
- Fetch recently closed bd issues (last 30 days)
- Compare documentation `updated_at` timestamps against PR/issue activity
- Identify documentation that may be outdated based on code changes
- Cross-reference issue/PR mentions in documentation content
- Generate report showing:
  - Docs that haven't been updated since recent PRs merged
  - Docs with stale `updated_at` vs file last modified
  - Issues/PRs referenced in docs that are still open
  - Documentation coverage gaps (new features without docs)

**Cross-Check Logic:**
- For each doc file, get last modified time from filesystem
- Compare against merged PRs since that date
- Flag docs that may need review
- Check if closed bd issues (feature work) have corresponding docs
- Validate `discovered-from` links in issues exist

**GitHub Integration:**
- Use `gh pr list` with date filters
- Use `gh issue list` with date filters
- Cache results for performance
- Handle rate limiting gracefully

**Output Format:**
- Summary table (docs checked, issues found, stale count)
- Detailed issues grouped by severity
- Suggested actions for each finding
- Support JSON output

**Integration:**
- Add as subcommand to `xtrm docs cross-check`
- Check for gh CLI availability
- Fall back gracefully if GitHub API unavailable
- Follow xtrm-cli error handling

**Test Cases:**
- Cross-check with recent PR activity
- Identify stale documentation
- Validate issue references
- GitHub API rate limiting
- Offline mode (no GitHub access)
close_reason: Completed via epic xtrm-8jr5. All implementation tasks (8jr5.1-8jr5.5) closed. Command working: xtrm docs cross-check [--days N] [--json]

title: Implement xtrm docs verify command - Detect frontmatter drift
description:
Create `xtrm docs verify` command to verify frontmatter for drift and description consistency across documentation.

**Requirements:**
- Parse frontmatter from all documentation files (YAML frontmatter between --- markers)
- Verify frontmatter schema compliance (required fields: title, description, updated_at)
- Detect drift by comparing expected vs actual frontmatter fields
- Check for orphaned references (files referenced in docs that don't exist)
- Validate cross-references between documentation files
- Display drift report with severity levels (ERROR, WARNING, INFO)
- Support auto-fix for simple issues (add missing timestamp, normalize field names)
- Generate detailed report with file paths, issues, and suggested fixes

**Drift Detection Logic:**
- Compare last modified date of file vs `updated_at` frontmatter field
- Detect outdated `version` or `status` fields based on PR/issue activity
- Check for inconsistent metadata patterns across similar doc types
- Verify `type` field values against expected vocabulary

**Integration:**
- Use gray-matter or similar library for frontmatter parsing
- Integrate with Serena memory for SSOT documentation patterns
- Follow xtrm-cli error handling patterns

**Test Cases:**
- Detect missing required fields
- Detect outdated `updated_at` timestamps
- Detect broken internal links/references
- Auto-fix simple normalization issues
- Report generation and formatting

title: guard-rules.mjs: canonical tool classification module — steal all levels from overstory
description:
Currently tool classifications are duplicated: WRITE_TOOLS inline in main-guard.mjs, matcher strings hardcoded in policies/*.json. No shared source of truth.

Steal overstory/src/agents/guard-rules.ts entirely, adapted to .mjs. All levels:

hooks/guard-rules.mjs (pure data, no logic, named exports only):

WRITE_TOOLS — Edit, Write, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit + all Serena write tools
  (mcp__serena__rename_symbol, replace_symbol_body, insert_after_symbol, insert_before_symbol)

DANGEROUS_BASH_PATTERNS — regex fragments (same as overstory + xtrm additions):
  sed -i, echo >, printf >, cat >, tee, vim/nano/vi
  mv, cp, rm, mkdir, touch, chmod, chown, >>
  git add, git commit, git merge, git push, git reset, git checkout, git rebase, git stash
  npm install, bun install, bun add
  node -e, node --eval, bun -e, bun --eval, python -c, perl -e, ruby -e (eval bypasses)

SAFE_BASH_PREFIXES — allowlist (overstory base + xtrm additions):
  git status, git log, git diff, git show, git blame, git branch, git fetch, git remote, git config
  git pull, git stash (read), git worktree
  git checkout -b, git switch -c (branch creation only)
  gh, bd, touch .beads/, npx gitnexus

NATIVE_TEAM_TOOLS — Task, TeamCreate, TeamDelete, SendMessage, TaskCreate, TaskUpdate,
  TaskList, TaskGet, TaskOutput, TaskStop

INTERACTIVE_TOOLS — AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode, EnterWorktree

Wire into:
1. main-guard.mjs: replace inline WRITE_TOOLS Set and SAFE_BASH_PATTERNS array with imports
2. compile-policies.mjs: use WRITE_TOOLS to auto-generate policy matcher strings
   (eliminates hardcoded 'Write|Edit|MultiEdit|...' in policies/*.json)
3. policy-parity.test.ts: assert policy matchers match WRITE_TOOLS export

Do NOT implement hooks-deployer equivalent yet — that belongs to specialists (filed separately).
