title: Test: xtrm finish + worktree creation — shell integration tests
description:
Layer: Shell — orchestration, git subprocess calls, gh CLI calls.
Strategy: integration tests with real temp git repos. Mock gh CLI only (external API with real costs/latency).

Covers: 1xa2.2 (worktree auto-creation), 1xa2.3 (xtrm finish phases)

Test file: cli/src/tests/xtrm-finish.test.ts

Setup: createTempGitRepo() helper (real git, two branches for merge testing)

Assertions — worktree creation:
- bd claim detected → worktree created at .overstory/worktrees/<issueId>
- state file written with phase=claimed, correct branch + worktreePath
- Already in worktree → skip (no nested worktrees)
- git worktree add fails → warning printed, claim not blocked, no state file written

Assertions — xtrm finish phase 1:
- Reads state file (never infers branch from git)
- Dirty working tree → commits before push
- Clean working tree → skips commit step
- Captures prNumber from gh pr create output → writes to state file
- phase transitions: claimed→phase1-done→waiting-merge

Assertions — xtrm finish poll loop (gh mocked):
- Mock returns state=MERGED → phase2 runs, worktree removed, phase=cleanup-done
- Mock returns mergeable=CONFLICTING → attempts rebase, if clean continues polling
- Mock returns timeout sequence → writes phase=pending-cleanup, exits with PR number in message
- Re-entrant: state=waiting-merge with prNumber → skips phase 1, resumes poll with stored prNumber

Assertions — xtrm finish phase 2:
- git worktree remove called with correct path from state file
- git fetch --prune called
- phase=cleanup-done written

Gates: 1xa2.7 (skill update) — do not update docs until integration tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Test: session state + stop gate — core unit tests
description:
Layer: Core — pure data logic, no I/O except filesystem.
Strategy: unit tests with real temp directories (no mocks).

Covers: 1xa2.1 (state file module), 1xa2.4 (stop gate phase logic)

Test file: cli/src/tests/session-state.test.ts

Assertions:
- readSessionState(): returns null when file absent, parses all phases correctly
- writeSessionState(): creates file, sets startedAt, validates schema
- updateSessionPhase(): transitions allowed (claimed→phase1-done→waiting-merge→merged→cleanup-done)
- updateSessionPhase(): invalid transitions throw
- findSessionStateFile(): walks up from nested CWD, returns null if not found
- Phase=waiting-merge with prNumber: stop gate returns BLOCK with PR number in message
- Phase=conflicting with conflictFiles: stop gate returns BLOCK with file list
- Phase=cleanup-done: stop gate returns PASS
- Phase=pending-cleanup: stop gate returns BLOCK with PR URL
- Phase=claimed: stop gate returns WARN (not block)

Property tests:
- Any write followed by read returns identical data
- findSessionStateFile always returns the nearest ancestor file

This issue gates: 1xa2.3 (xtrm finish) and 1xa2.6 (policy) — do not ship without these passing.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Policy: session-flow.json (runtime:both) + Pi extension session-flow.ts
description:
Following the runtime:both policy pattern established in main-guard.json and beads.json.

policies/session-flow.json:
{
  "id": "session-flow",
  "description": "Worktree auto-creation on claim, session closure enforcement via xtrm finish",
  "version": "1",
  "runtime": "both",
  "order": 25,
  "claude": {
    "hooks": [
      { "event": "PostToolUse", "matcher": "Bash", "command": "node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs" },
      { "event": "Stop", "command": "node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/beads-stop-gate.mjs" }
    ]
  },
  "pi": {
    "extension": "config/pi/extensions/session-flow.ts",
    "events": ["tool_result", "agent_end"]
  }
}

config/pi/extensions/session-flow.ts:
- tool_result: detect bd claim output → trigger worktree creation (calls same logic as beads-claim-sync.mjs)
- agent_end: read session state file → if phase=waiting-merge/conflicting/pending-cleanup → block with same messages as stop gate

Pi parity requirement:
- Same phase-based blocking logic as Claude stop gate
- Same worktree creation on claim detection
- CWD-aware state file lookup (findSessionStateFile walks up from CWD)

After adding policy: run npm run compile-policies to update hooks/hooks.json. Run policy-parity tests.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Compact save/restore: preserve session state across /compact
description:
Session state must survive /compact so the agent does not lose track of its PR number or worktree path mid-closure.

beads-compact-save.mjs additions:
- Read .xtrm-session-state.json if present
- Include in compact bundle under 'sessionState' key
- If phase=waiting-merge: include reminder in compact context:
  'PENDING: xtrm finish waiting for PR #<prNumber> to merge. Re-run xtrm finish to resume.'

beads-compact-restore.mjs additions:
- Restore sessionState from bundle back to .xtrm-session-state.json
- If phase=waiting-merge or pending-cleanup: inject into restored context:
  'RESUME: Run xtrm finish — PR #<prNumber> (<prUrl>) waiting for merge. Worktree: <worktreePath>'

This ensures that after a compact, the agent immediately knows it has a pending closure and exactly what to do.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Stop gate: block session end on unfinished closure (waiting-merge / conflicting / pending-cleanup)
description:
Extend beads-stop-gate.mjs to check .xtrm-session-state.json before allowing session end.

Logic (runs after existing beads claim check):
1. findSessionStateFile() — walk up from CWD
2. If not found → pass (no session in progress)
3. Read phase:
   - cleanup-done → pass
   - waiting-merge → BLOCK
       '🚫 PR #<prNumber> not yet merged. Run: xtrm finish'
       Show: prUrl
   - pending-cleanup → BLOCK (same message)
   - conflicting → BLOCK
       '🚫 Merge conflicts in: <conflictFiles>. Resolve, push, then: xtrm finish'
   - claimed / phase1-done → WARN (not block)
       '⚠ Session has an active worktree at <worktreePath>. Consider running: xtrm finish'

All messages include the PR number (when available) so the agent has exact context — no ambiguity about which PR or which worktree.
close_reason: Implemented

title: xtrm finish CLI command: phase1 + poll loop + phase2 with conflict auto-rebase
description:
Single blocking command that replaces the entire manual closure flow. Returns only when worktree is cleaned up or an unrecoverable state is reached.

Phase 1 (fast, <10s):
1. Read .xtrm-session-state.json → get issueId, branch, worktreePath
2. git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(<issueId>): <branch>' (skip if working tree clean)
3. git push -u origin <branch>
4. gh pr create --fill → capture prNumber + prUrl from output
5. gh pr merge --squash --auto
6. Write prNumber, prUrl to state file, phase: waiting-merge

Poll loop (blocks, 5s interval, 10min timeout):
- gh pr view <prNumber> --json state,mergeStateStatus,mergeable
- state=MERGED            → phase 2
- mergeStateStatus=BEHIND → git fetch + git push (rebase not needed, just push)
- mergeable=CONFLICTING   → conflict resolution:
    a. git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main
    b. if clean: git push --force-with-lease → continue polling
    c. if conflicts remain: write phase=conflicting + conflictFiles → exit with message
       'Conflicts in: <files>. Resolve, push, then re-run xtrm finish.'
- timeout (10min):        → write phase=pending-cleanup → exit with:
       'PR #<n> not yet merged. Run xtrm finish when ready.'

Phase 2 (on MERGED):
- git worktree remove <worktreePath> --force
- git fetch --prune
- write phase=cleanup-done
- print: 'Done. PR #<n> merged. Worktree removed.'

CLI: xtrm finish (no args — reads all context from state file)
Re-entrant: if state=waiting-merge/pending-cleanup, skips phase 1 and resumes poll loop with stored prNumber.
Re-entrant: if state=conflicting, expects conflicts resolved, skips to push+poll.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Worktree auto-creation on bd claim via beads-claim-sync.mjs
description:
Extend hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs to auto-create a git worktree when a claim is detected. Agent does bd quickstart / bd update --claim as normal — worktree appears automatically with no extra commands.

Logic:
1. Detect new claim (existing claim-sync logic already does this)
2. Derive branch: feature/<issueId> (deterministic, no ambiguity)
3. Derive worktree path: <repoRoot>/.overstory/worktrees/<issueId>
   - Fallback path if .overstory/ absent: <repoRoot>/.worktrees/<issueId>
4. git worktree add <path> -b <branch>
5. Write .xtrm-session-state.json (phase: claimed) — calls session-state module
6. Print to stdout: 'Worktree created: <path>  Branch: <branch>'

Guards / graceful no-ops:
- Already in a worktree → skip (do not nest worktrees)
- Branch already exists → git worktree add <path> <branch> (no -b)
- worktree add fails (e.g. locked) → log warning, continue without worktree (never block the claim)
- No git repo → skip silently

The printed path appears in Claude's context immediately after claim — agent knows where it is.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Session state file: .xtrm-session-state.json spec + read/write module
description:
The single source of truth for session closure. Prevents agent drift — every automation step reads from this file, never infers from git state or context.

Schema (written at repo root, gitignored):
{
  "issueId": "xtrm-4xr6",
  "branch": "feature/xtrm-4xr6",
  "worktreePath": "/path/to/repo/.overstory/worktrees/4xr6",
  "prNumber": null,            // filled by xtrm finish phase 1
  "prUrl": null,               // filled by xtrm finish phase 1
  "phase": "claimed",         // claimed | phase1-done | waiting-merge | conflicting | pending-cleanup | cleanup-done
  "conflictFiles": [],         // filled if phase=conflicting
  "startedAt": "ISO8601",
  "lastChecked": "ISO8601"
}

Deliverables:
- cli/src/core/session-state.ts: readSessionState(), writeSessionState(), updateSessionPhase(), findSessionStateFile() (walks up from CWD)
- Phase transition validation (e.g. cannot go from cleanup-done back to claimed)
- File is written at worktree root (or repo root if no worktree)
- Add .xtrm-session-state.json to .gitignore

This module is imported by: beads-claim-sync.mjs, xtrm finish command, stop gate hook, compact save/restore hooks.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Streamlined session closure: xtrm finish + worktree-first workflow
description:
Eliminates the mechanical multi-step session closure flow (commit→push→pr→merge→cleanup) and the multi-terminal file conflict problem. Agents drift because the flow has too many manual steps with no enforcement between them.

Core design:
- Worktree auto-created on bd claim (beads-claim-sync.mjs extended)
- .xtrm-session-state.json is the single source of truth: issueId, branch, worktreePath, prNumber, prUrl, phase
- xtrm finish is a single blocking command: phase1 (commit+push+PR+auto-merge) + poll loop + phase2 (worktree cleanup) — returns only when done
- Stop gate blocks if phase is waiting-merge, conflicting, or pending-cleanup
- Compact save/restore preserves state across /compact
- Claude first, Pi parity via policy runtime:both pattern

NOT overstory. No tmux, no coordinator, no multi-agent machinery. The specialist system (~/projects/specialists) handles multi-agent. This is single-agent closure automation that composes cleanly with specialists later.

Phase lifecycle:
  claimed → phase1-done → waiting-merge → (conflicting →) merged → cleanup-done
close_reason: Closed

description:

Core principle: hooks and skills are mechanisms (global). Project-specific DATA (service-registry.json, gitnexus index, beads DB) stays in the project. xtrm init provisions the data; the global layer already knows how to use it.

Scripts must gracefully exit 0 when no project context found (no config, no registry, no linters).

title: Adapt ralphi autonomous loop pattern for xtrm workflow
description:
## Overview

Adapt the [@manojlds/ralphi](https://github.com/manojlds/ralphi) autonomous loop pattern to integrate with the xtrm-tools workflow, replacing its native PRD/stories system with beads issue tracking and integrating all xtrm enforcement mechanisms.

## What ralphi Provides

ralphi is a Pi-native implementation of the Ralph autonomous loop pattern:

- **`/ralphi-init`** — initialize project config and guardrails
- **`/ralphi-prd`** — generate PRD markdown from feature idea
- **`/ralphi-convert`** — convert PRD markdown to \`.ralphi/prd.json\`
- **`/ralphi-loop-start`** — run iterative implementation against PRD

Key features:
- Fresh-context loop iterations (child sessions)
- PRD-driven story selection with dependency handling
- Quality gates via config.yaml commands
- Trajectory guard (drift detection)
- Reflection checkpoints

## Adaptation Goals

### 1. Replace PRD/stories with Beads

| ralphi | xtrm-adapted |
|--------|--------------|
| \`.ralphi/prd.json\` | \`.beads/issues.jsonl\` |
| `userStories[].status` | bd issue status (open/in_progress/closed) |
| `userStories[].priority` | bd priority (0-4) |
| `userStories[].dependsOn` | bd dependencies (`--blocked-by`) |

### 2. Integrate xtrm Components

- **main-guard**: PR-only workflow enforcement
- **beads gates**: Edit/commit/stop gates with claim tracking
- **quality-gates**: Lint/typecheck via Pi extension
- **test-planning**: Create companion test issues automatically
- **gitnexus**: Impact analysis before changes
- **using-xtrm**: Session operating manual

### 3. New Command Structure

```
/xtrm-init           # Initialize project with xtrm guardrails
/xtrm-prd <desc>     # Generate PRD, create bd epic + child issues
/xtrm-loop-start     # Start autonomous loop against bd issues
/xtrm-loop-status    # Show loop progress
/xtrm-loop-stop      # Halt loop
```

### 4. Loop Behavior

1. Controller reads `bd ready` to find unblocked issues
2. Claims issue: `bd update <id> --claim`
3. Fresh child session implements the issue
4. Quality gates run automatically (via Pi extension)
5. On completion: `bd close <id> --reason`
6. Next iteration picks next unblocked issue
7. Loop ends when no more ready issues

### 5. Test Planning Integration

When PRD generates issues:
- Classify each by layer (core/boundary/shell)
- Create companion test issues per test-planning skill
- Link via dependencies

## Key Differences from ralphi

| Aspect | ralphi | xtrm-adapted |
|--------|--------|--------------|
| Issue store | \`.ralphi/prd.json\` | beads (Dolt-backed) |
| Claim model | None | Session-scoped claims |
| Quality gates | config.yaml commands | Pi extension + hooks |
| Branch protection | Manual config | main-guard enforcement |
| Test strategy | None | test-planning skill |
| Code intelligence | None | gitnexus integration |

## Deliverables

1. Pi extension: `xtrm-loop.ts`
2. Skills: `xtrm-init`, `xtrm-prd`, `xtrm-loop-start`, `xtrm-loop-status`
3. Config schema: \`.xtrm/config.yaml\` structure
4. Integration tests with beads workflow
5. Documentation update to XTRM-GUIDE.md

## References

- ralphi source: https://github.com/manojlds/ralphi
- ralphi npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@manojlds/ralphi
- beads docs: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads
close_reason: Closed during board cleanup: wdnd=ralph-loop already exists; cnsd=dolt push not configured; 25v8=test-planning invoked on demand, hook automation not needed

title: Hook integration for test-planning lifecycle (UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse/SessionEnd)
description:
Purpose
Implement hook-level lifecycle integration for the test-planning skill so testing strategy is consistently introduced at planning time, validated at closure time, and reviewed at session end.

Dependency Requirement
This epic depends on completion of:
- xtrm-thn4 (Test-planning v1.1)
The hook automation in this epic must consume and enforce the improved skill guidance from that foundation epic.

Problem Statement
Today, test-planning quality relies heavily on manual invocation and agent discipline. Even with good skill docs, drift happens during high-throughput issue planning and closure. We need deterministic hook touchpoints that provide high-signal nudges (and optional strict gating) without noisy false positives.

Goals
1) Integrate test-planning at precise lifecycle points with minimal noise
2) Detect planning and closure intents automatically from tool events
3) Run a closure safety net to prevent implementation issues from being closed without planned test coverage
4) Preserve configurability through policy modes (advisory vs strict)
5) Maintain parity between Claude hook behavior and Pi extension behavior

Non-Goals
- Do not rewrite the core test-planning strategy content in this epic (owned by dependency epic)
- Do not enforce universal strict blocking by default
- Do not introduce heavyweight background indexing/polling

Scope of Work
A. UserPromptSubmit Intent Detection
- Add prompt-level detection for planning/closure phrases:
  - “create issues”, “break into tasks”, “close issue”, “bd create”, “bd close”, “bd children”
- On detection, emit targeted suggestion to apply test-planning skill
- Ensure suppression for clearly unrelated prompts to avoid noise

B. PostToolUse Deterministic Triggering (Bash + bd commands)
- For tool executions containing bd commands:
  1. On `bd create`:
     - classify issue as implementation vs non-implementation
     - check for matching/batched companion test issue
     - if absent: emit recommendation (advisory mode) or enforce next-step gate (strict mode)
  2. On `bd close`:
     - run closure gate check (existing test issue? still accurate to built scope?)
     - if missing/outdated: emit actionable update/create guidance
- Include parsing resilience for flag order variations and quoted values

C. Stop / SessionEnd Safety Net
- At session end, summarize implementation issues touched without test-plan linkage
- Default behavior: non-blocking warning + suggested bd commands
- Strict mode option: require acknowledgement or block stop until recorded exception path

D. Policy Modes + Configuration
- Add explicit config modes:
  - `advisory` (default): suggest, never block
  - `strict-close`: block/deny close path when no test plan linkage exists
  - `strict-session`: enforce end-session acknowledgement
- Document env/config toggles and precedence order

E. Claude/Pi Parity Layer
- Ensure equivalent behavior mapping in:
  - Claude hooks configuration/path
  - Pi extension equivalents
- Add parity matrix documenting event, trigger, action, and strictness behavior per runtime

F. Messaging UX
- Standardize concise, actionable messages:
  - what failed/missing
  - why it matters
  - exact `bd` command snippets to remediate
- Avoid verbose diagnostics in normal path; detailed logs only in debug mode

G. Observability + Debugging
- Add debug mode to log per-event decisions:
  - invoked / skipped / matched-intent / remediation-generated
- Include skip reasons for false-positive tuning

Deliverables
1) Hook/extension integration implementation for 3 lifecycle points:
- UserPromptSubmit
- PostToolUse (bd create/bd close)
- Stop/SessionEnd

2) Runtime configuration model for advisory/strict policies

3) Parity documentation (Claude vs Pi behavior table)

4) Test coverage for trigger detection and gating behavior

Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: Planning-intent prompts trigger test-planning suggestion with low false positives
- AC2: `bd create` path detects implementation issues and checks companion test-plan presence
- AC3: `bd close` path runs closure gate checks and emits deterministic remediation
- AC4: Session end summarizes unresolved test-plan gaps; strict mode behavior works as configured
- AC5: Advisory and strict modes are configurable, documented, and covered by tests
- AC6: Claude and Pi implementations show behavior parity for equivalent events
- AC7: Messaging includes copy/paste-safe bd remediation commands

Risk Analysis
- Risk: Over-triggering could create friction
  - Mitigation: conservative intent matching + skip reasons + advisory default
- Risk: Command parsing edge cases
  - Mitigation: robust parser tests for quoting/flag order/compound commands
- Risk: Runtime drift between Claude and Pi
  - Mitigation: parity test suite + parity matrix as release gate

Suggested Work Breakdown
1. Implement intent detector + unit tests
2. Implement PostToolUse bd parser + create/close handlers
3. Implement SessionEnd safety net
4. Add mode config and wire toggles
5. Build parity tests (Claude vs Pi)
6. Add docs and operational examples

Dependencies
- Blocked by: xtrm-thn4

Definition of Complete
This epic is complete when test-planning is automatically and predictably surfaced across planning, closure, and session-end lifecycle events—with configurable strictness and verified Claude/Pi parity—built on top of the upgraded v1.1 skill content.
close_reason: Closed during board cleanup: wdnd=ralph-loop already exists; cnsd=dolt push not configured; 25v8=test-planning invoked on demand, hook automation not needed

title: Test-planning v1.1: deterministic strategy, anti-pattern controls, and DoD templates
description:
Purpose
Elevate the existing skills/test-planning skill from a strong baseline to a production-grade planning standard for test strategy in bd-driven workflows.

Background
The current test-planning skill already provides excellent foundations:
- layer-based classification (core/boundary/shell)
- strategy routing (TDD/spec-first/property/contract/characterization/example-based)
- planning-phase + closure-gate behavior
- batching guidance and bd-native issue structures

This epic upgrades that baseline with sharper quality controls, deterministic prioritization, and explicit Definition-of-Done standards so generated/updated test issues are consistently actionable.

Problem Statement
Current skill guidance can still produce variability across agents/sessions:
- anti-patterns are described indirectly but not enforced as a standard checklist
- test issue priorities are not consistently normalized against implementation risk
- layer guidance exists, but completion criteria are not codified as strict DoD templates
- teams may over-focus on broad coverage percentages over critical-path confidence
- advisory vs enforcement boundaries are implied but not explicit

Goals
1) Add explicit anti-pattern prevention checklist for planning + closure review
2) Add risk-based priority model for generated test issues
3) Add per-layer Definition of Done templates with concrete pass criteria
4) Replace blanket coverage framing with critical-path and risk-path expectations
5) Clarify policy boundary: what skill recommends vs what hooks enforce

Non-Goals
- Do not implement hook automation in this epic (tracked separately)
- Do not add new runtime dependencies
- Do not rewrite quality-gates behavior itself beyond documentation alignment

Scope of Work
A. Anti-Pattern Checklist Integration
- Add a standardized checklist section used at both triggers (planning and closure)
- Required checks:
  1. assertion-free tests
  2. tautological assertions (self-fulfilling logic)
  3. context leakage/shared mutable state
  4. over-mocking internal collaborators
  5. test cases that cannot fail under realistic regressions
- Add “how to detect” and “how to fix” bullets per anti-pattern

B. Priority Heuristics for Test Issues
- Introduce deterministic priority mapping:
  - P0/P1: boundary/security/auth/protocol compatibility/regression-critical paths
  - P1/P2: core domain logic with high business impact
  - P2/P3: low-risk internals, non-critical adapters
  - P3/P4: polish-level test debt
- Add inheritance rule from implementation issue priority with bounded adjustment
- Add examples for when tests should be equal priority vs +1 priority

C. Layer-Specific Definition of Done Templates
- Core DoD template:
  - positive + negative path assertions
  - edge cases explicitly enumerated
  - invariants/properties included where applicable
  - deterministic fixtures and no shared mutable state
- Boundary DoD template:
  - schema/contract assertions
  - error code and retry/fallback behavior where relevant
  - drift-safe assertions (field presence/types, not brittle internals)
  - live-first policy and fallback hierarchy documented
- Shell DoD template:
  - end-to-end observable outcomes
  - failure-mode UX (exit codes/messages) assertions
  - cross-component wiring checks

D. Coverage Guidance Modernization
- Remove/avoid blanket numeric coverage framing as primary success metric
- Require “critical path map” in issue description:
  - paths covered
  - risk rationale
  - known deferred paths (with follow-up issue references)

E. Enforcement Boundary Clarification
- Add explicit section in SKILL.md:
  - advisory from skill (planning quality, strategy selection)
  - enforced by hooks/extensions (quality-gates, branch/claim rules)
- Include examples to avoid policy confusion

Deliverables
1) Updated skill file:
- skills/test-planning/SKILL.md with all new sections/templates/checklists

2) Example patterns in-document:
- at least 2 end-to-end issue description examples using new templates

3) Consistency alignment:
- terminology alignment with using-quality-gates and broader xtrm testing philosophy

Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: SKILL.md includes a mandatory anti-pattern checklist used in both trigger flows
- AC2: Priority heuristic table exists with deterministic mapping + examples
- AC3: DoD templates exist for core, boundary, shell with concrete pass/fail criteria
- AC4: Guidance uses critical-path coverage framing, not blanket percentage mandates
- AC5: Advisory vs enforcement boundaries are explicitly documented with examples
- AC6: At least two realistic issue examples show the new format end-to-end

Risks
- Over-prescriptive templates could increase issue creation overhead
- Priority heuristics may need calibration after first few real-world uses

Mitigations
- Keep templates concise and structured
- Collect first-iteration feedback and tune thresholds in follow-up task(s)

Dependencies
- None (foundation epic)

Out of Scope Follow-ups
- Hook-based automation and gating integration (separate epic)

Definition of Complete
This epic is complete when the test-planning skill documentation is upgraded to a deterministic, reusable standard that reliably produces high-quality, risk-aware test planning issues without requiring runtime hook automation.
close_reason: All 5 sections added to skills/test-planning/SKILL.md: anti-pattern checklist, priority heuristics, per-layer DoD templates, critical-path coverage guidance, advisory-vs-enforcement boundary. Two v1.1 format examples included.

title: Test: policy compiler — integration + schema validation tests
description:
## What this covers

Covers: xtrm-ei6 (policy schema), xtrm-o52 (compiler), xtrm-cuk (main-guard + beads policies)

## Layer classification

- `policies/schema.json` → Core (pure data, JSON Schema definition)
- `scripts/compile-policies.mjs` → Shell (CLI tool: reads files, writes hooks.json)

## Strategy: Integration tests (shell layer, deterministic I/O)

The compiler is a pure function over the filesystem: given N policy files → produces hooks.json. No external I/O beyond file reads/writes. Integration tests with fixture directories are ideal.

## Test file structure

`tests/compile-policies/` (or `scripts/tests/` if colocated with the script):
- `fixtures/policies/` — sample policy JSON files
- `fixtures/expected-hooks.json` — expected compiler output
- `compile-policies.test.mjs` — test suite

## What to assert

### Compiler correctness
- [ ] Given the 5 current policy files → output matches `hooks/hooks.json` exactly (golden file test)
- [ ] Same-matcher hooks from multiple policies merge into one group (order preserved)
- [ ] `order` field controls policy execution sequence within the same event
- [ ] Policies with `runtime: pi` are excluded from hooks.json output
- [ ] No matcher entry is emitted as `null` or empty string — omitted entirely

### CLI flags
- [ ] `--dry-run`: prints JSON to stdout, does NOT write hooks.json
- [ ] `--check`: exits 0 when hooks.json matches; exits 1 when stale (with useful error message)
- [ ] Write mode: overwrites hooks.json, prints per-policy hook counts

### Schema validation (if validation is added to compiler)
- [ ] Policy missing required `id` → validation error
- [ ] Policy with unknown field → validation error (additionalProperties: false)
- [ ] Policy with invalid `runtime` value → validation error

### Edge cases
- [ ] Empty policies/ directory → empty hooks object `{}`
- [ ] Policy with no `claude.hooks` → contributes nothing to output
- [ ] Timeout omitted → no timeout field in generated entry

## Acceptance criteria

All assertions pass. `npm run check-policies` passes in CI (add to .github/workflows/ci.yml).
close_reason: 12 integration tests written in cli/test/compile-policies.test.ts — covers golden file, --dry-run, --check, write mode, macro expansion, pi exclusion, no null matchers, timeout field, SessionStart merging. All 306 tests pass.

title: Add Pi extension equivalent of beads-memory-gate
description:
## Description

Port the beads-memory-gate stop hook to a Pi extension. The Claude-side hook fires at session end and prompts the agent to persist insights via `bd remember` when the session's claimed issue was closed.

The Pi equivalent should:
- Hook into Pi's session-end / stop event
- Check `getSessionClaim` (or equivalent bd kv lookup) for the session claim
- If the claim is now closed, prompt the agent to evaluate insights worth persisting
- Accept a marker signal (bd kv or equivalent) to clear the gate once evaluation is done

## Reference

Claude implementation: `hooks/beads-memory-gate.mjs` (recently fixed in #98 to scope to session claim instead of all-time closed issues).
close_reason: Closed

title: memory-gate fires every session + marker file unwritable on main
description:
## Problem

beads-memory-gate stop hook fires every session because it checks `bd list --status=closed` (all ever-closed issues) rather than issues closed *this* session. Once triggered, it requires `touch .beads/.memory-gate-done` — but the absolute path was being used, which doesn't match the main-guard allowlist pattern `/^touch\s+\.beads\//`. Using relative path `touch .beads/.memory-gate-done` from project CWD works fine.

## Root Cause

**beads-memory-gate.mjs**: checked all-time closed issues instead of scoping to the current session's claimed issue.

## Fix

Only fire the gate if the session had a claimed issue that is now closed. Use `getSessionClaim(sessionId, cwd)` to retrieve the claim, then check if it appears in `bd list --status=closed`.
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate xtrm-tools to Claude Code plugin structure
description:
Restructure xtrm-tools as a first-class Claude Code plugin.

Current state: xtrm CLI installs hooks to ~/.claude/hooks/, wires settings.json, copies skills to ~/.agents/skills/. This causes permanent drift via PROTECTED_KEYS — requires install/sync/clean/prune machinery.

Plugin approach eliminates all of that:
- hooks/hooks.json with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} portable paths — no wiring, no drift
- skills/ auto-discovered by Claude Code — no copying needed
- .mcp.json for MCP server registration
- Pi extensions remain separate (config/pi/extensions/ → Pi's own install)
- xtrm CLI shrinks to: project init, gitnexus setup, bd init

This is the structural prerequisite for the policy unification epic (k9bg).
The plugin hooks/hooks.json becomes the target output for the policy compiler (o52).

Deliverables:
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest
- hooks/hooks.json using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}
- skills/ mirrored from current skills/
- .mcp.json for core MCP servers
- Retire safeMergeConfig / PROTECTED_KEYS / install-sync hook machinery
- Update xtrm CLI: install plugin, not manage hooks directly
close_reason: Scaffolding phase complete: .claude-plugin/plugin.json, hooks/hooks.json (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT paths), .mcp.json (gitnexus added). CLI retire deferred to follow-on.

title: Policy unification + precise main-guard command-intent enforcement
description:
Epic: unified enforcement model where policies are defined once and compiled to both Claude Code plugin hooks and Pi extensions.

Prerequisite: xtrm-8r52 (plugin migration — must complete first)

Child issues (execute in order):
1. xtrm-ei6 — define policy schema
2. xtrm-o52 — build policy compiler (input: schema → output: hooks.json + Pi .ts)
3. xtrm-cuk — migrate main-guard + beads policies
4. xtrm-76v — migrate service-skills + quality-gates policies
5. xtrm-79m — cross-runtime parity test suite

End state: no hand-written hooks or Pi extension duplication. All enforcement derives from policies/*.yaml. The plugin structure (8r52) is the delivery mechanism for Claude-side compiled output.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add cross-runtime parity test suite for policy enforcement
description:
Test suite that verifies a given policy produces equivalent enforcement behavior across both Claude (plugin hooks) and Pi (extensions).

Depends on: xtrm-o52 (policy compiler — need compiled artifacts to test)
Part of epic: xtrm-k9bg

For each compiled policy:
- Claude side: simulate PreToolUse/PostToolUse events, assert block/allow/augment behavior
- Pi side: simulate Pi event handlers, assert same outcome
- Diff report: flag any behavioral divergence between runtimes

Test harness should run in CI (no live agent required). Mock event payloads based on policy schema from ei6.
close_reason: Closed

title: Build policy compiler to generate Claude hooks and Pi mappings
description:
Build a compiler that takes canonical policy definitions (ei6 schema) and generates both Claude and Pi enforcement artifacts.

Depends on: xtrm-8r52 (plugin migration), xtrm-ei6 (policy schema)
Part of epic: xtrm-k9bg

Input: policies/*.yaml (canonical policy definitions)
Output 1: plugin/hooks/hooks.json — Claude Code plugin hook wiring (${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} paths)
Output 2: config/pi/extensions/<policy>.ts — Pi extension TypeScript stubs

The plugin structure (8r52) defines where Output 1 lives. Without it, there is no clean delivery target.
The policy schema (ei6) defines what the input looks like.

Compiler should:
- Validate policies against schema
- Emit hooks.json entries with correct matchers, timeouts, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} references
- Emit Pi extension event handlers with matching logic
- Report gaps where a policy cannot be expressed in one or both runtimes
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify hook/extension policy model across Claude and Pi
description:
Define a single canonical policy schema that drives enforcement on both Claude Code (hooks) and Pi (extensions).

Context: xtrm-tools is migrating to a Claude Code plugin structure (xtrm-8r52). The plugin's hooks/hooks.json is the Claude-side delivery target. Pi extensions remain in config/pi/extensions/. A unified policy model means one definition generates both.

Depends on: xtrm-8r52 (plugin migration — establishes the target structure)
Part of epic: xtrm-k9bg

Policy schema should capture:
- event (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, etc.)
- matcher (tool pattern)
- action (block | notify | augment)
- condition (optional predicate)
- runtime targets (claude | pi | both)

Output consumed by o52 (policy compiler) to generate hooks/hooks.json and Pi extension TypeScript.
close_reason: Closed

title: Eliminate tdd-guard completely — remove from Claude hooks, Pi extension, project-skills, and skills catalog
description:
Per TDD research brief (2026-03-16) and global-first architecture (4xr6): tdd-guard is eliminated entirely.

Research findings:
- TDD gates provide 9-30% improvement ONLY when tests are provided as EXTERNAL specification
- Same agent writing both tests + impl = self-consistency theater
- LLM tests are fragile (40% coverage, 34-62% invalid syntax)
- PostToolUse quality gates are the correct universal default

Elimination scope:
1. hooks/tdd-guard.mjs (or equivalent) — delete
2. policies/tdd-guard.json — delete
3. project-skills/tdd-guard/ — delete entire directory
4. skills/using-TDD/ — keep skill (TDD as optional opt-in guidance), remove any 'guard' references
5. Pi extension config/pi/extensions/tdd-guard.ts (if exists) — delete
6. hooks.json — recompile after policy deletion
7. XTRM-GUIDE.md — remove from project skills table
8. using-xtrm skill — remove references to tdd-guard

Part of epic 4xr6 (global-first architecture). 4xr6.1 scope updated to exclude tdd-guard.
close_reason: tdd-guard fully eliminated: project-skills/tdd-guard/ deleted, .claude/hooks/tdd-guard-pretool-bridge.cjs deleted, all references removed from XTRM-GUIDE.md and using-xtrm SKILL.md

title: Consolidate duplicated hook pruning logic
description:
Two different implementations of hook pruning exist:

- atomic-config.ts: pruneStaleWrappers()
- install-project.ts: pruneStaleHooks()

Both have slightly different logic for detecting stale hooks.

**Impact**: Inconsistent behavior, maintenance burden, potential bugs from divergence.

**Recommendation**: Consolidate into a single implementation in atomic-config.ts.
close_reason: Stale: CLI Bugfixes Audit from 2026-03-17 no longer relevant

title: Use manifest for drift detection
description:
The .jaggers-sync-manifest.json tracks:

```json
{
  "lastSync": "timestamp",
  "repoRoot": "...",
  "items": 42
}
```

But it doesn't track individual file hashes. The diff.ts recalculates hashes every time instead of using the manifest for comparison.

**Impact**: Unnecessary file I/O on every status check, inaccurate drift detection.

**Recommendation**: Store file hashes in manifest for accurate drift detection.
close_reason: Done as part of 9bl fix — manifest stores per-file hashes, compareItem uses them

title: Implement cross-target transaction rollback
description:
Currently, if sync fails on the 3rd target after successfully syncing 2 targets:

- No automatic rollback across targets
- Each target has its own backup system but they're not coordinated
- User ends up with partially synced environment

**Impact**: Users can be left in an inconsistent state after partial sync failures.

**Recommendation**: Implement cross-target transaction with coordinated rollback.

title: E2E audit: Pi extensions — verify all 2.2.0 Pi changes
description:
End-to-end test run for all Pi extension changes shipped in 2.2.0.

## Scope
Install xtrm 2.2.0 and run pi on a beads project, verify:

### beads.ts
- [ ] Edit with no claim + open issues → blocked with `bd update <id> --claim` reason
- [ ] Edit with no claim + no issues → allowed (fail-open)
- [ ] `bd update <id> --claim` → kv set + "Session claimed" notice injected into tool result
- [ ] `git commit` with active claim + in_progress issues → blocked
- [ ] `git commit` with active claim + NO in_progress issues → allowed

### custom-footer.ts

- [ ] Bead chip shows `bd:<shortId>◐` when in_progress claim active
- [ ] Bead chip shows `bd:<N>○` when no claim but open issues exist
- [ ] Bead chip absent when no issues

### xtrm-loader.ts
- [ ] Session start injects CLAUDE.md content without blocking (async reads)
- [ ] Rules files loaded correctly

### service-skills.ts

### core/lib (EventAdapter)

End-to-end test run for all Claude Code hook changes shipped in 2.2.0.

## Scope

### main-guard.mjs
- [ ] Write on main → block message includes `git checkout -b feature/` AND `bd update --claim`

title: Debug: Beads claim workflow not working reliably
description:
## Problem

The beads claim workflow should auto-set `bd kv claimed:<session_id> <issue_id>` when running `bd update <id> --claim`, but it's not working reliably.

## Expected Behavior

1. Run `bd update <issue-id> --claim`
2. Hook or extension auto-sets `bd kv set "claimed:<session_id>" "<issue-id>"`
3. Statusline shows claimed issue (e.g., `bd:r34◐`)
4. Edits are unblocked

## Actual Behavior

- The claim is set on the issue (status becomes `in_progress`)
- But the kv entry is NOT set
- Statusline shows open count (`bd:2○`) instead of claimed issue
- Manual `bd kv set` is required

---

## Architecture

### Components Involved

| Component | Location | Role |
|-----------|----------|------|
| Pi Extension | `cli/extensions/beads.ts` | Should catch `bd update --claim` via `tool_result` event and set kv |
| Pi Extension | `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer.ts` | Reads kv to display beads chip in statusline |
| PostToolUse Hook | `hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs` | Should catch `bd update --claim` and set kv |
| PreToolUse Hook | `hooks/beads-edit-gate.mjs` | Checks kv to allow/block edits |

### Session ID Strategy (Attempted)

| Attempt | Session Key Source | Problem |
|---------|-------------------|---------|
| 1. PID | `process.pid.toString()` | Hook runs in subprocess, different PID than Pi |
| 2. Project path | `input.cwd` | Works but doesn't match Pi's PID-based key |
| 3. session_id from hook input | `input.session_id` | Should be UUID from Pi's `sessionManager.getSessionId()` |
| 4. ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId() | Pi extension API | Should return UUID matching hook's `input.session_id` |

---

## Everything Tried

### Attempt 1: Pi Extension tool_result Handler

**File:** `cli/extensions/beads.ts`

```typescript
pi.on("tool_result", async (event, ctx) => {
  if (isBashToolResult(event)) {
    const command = event.input.command;
    if (command && /\bbd\s+update\b/.test(command) && /--claim\b/.test(command)) {
      const issueMatch = command.match(/\bbd\s+update\s+(\S+)/);
      if (issueMatch) {
        const issueId = issueMatch[1];
        const cwd = getCwd(ctx);
        const sessionId = process.pid.toString(); // Problem: different from hook
        await SubprocessRunner.run("bd", ["kv", "set", `claimed:${sessionId}`, issueId], { cwd });
        // ...
      }
    }
  }
});
```

**Result:** Extension did not appear to fire on `bd update --claim`. No `✅ **Beads**` message appeared.

**Suspected Issue:** Extension events may require `/reload` or session restart to take effect.

---

### Attempt 2: PostToolUse Hook

**File:** `hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs`

```javascript
import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';
import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs';

function main() {
  const input = JSON.parse(readFileSync(0, 'utf-8'));
  if (input.tool_name !== 'Bash') process.exit(0);
  
  const command = input.tool_input?.command || '';
  if (!/\bbd\s+update\b/.test(command) || !/--claim\b/.test(command)) {
    process.exit(0);
  }

  const match = command.match(/\bbd\s+update\s+(\S+)/);
  const issueId = match[1];
  const sessionId = input.session_id; // Should be UUID

  spawnSync('bd', ['kv', 'set', `claimed:${sessionId}`, issueId], { ... });
}
```

**Result:** Hook never fired. Debug log file was never created.

**Suspected Issue:** 
- Hooks may only be loaded at session start, not via `/reload`
- The settings.json had a malformed entry that was fixed, but session needs restart

---

### Attempt 3: Manual Session Key Coordination

Tried multiple session key strategies:

1. **PID** - Hook PID `1225881` != Pi PID `1107931`
2. **Project path** - Works but inconsistent with statusline
3. **session_id from hook input** - Should work but hook not firing
4. **ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId()** - Should return UUID

**Files updated to use UUID:**
- `cli/extensions/beads.ts` → `ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionId?.()`
- `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer.ts` → `ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionId?.()`
- `hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs` → `input.session_id`

---

### Attempt 4: Reinstall Hooks

```bash
mv ~/.claude/settings.json ~/.claude/settings.json.broken
# Created clean settings.json
node cli/dist/index.cjs install all -y
```

**Result:** Hooks reinstalled correctly, but still not firing.

---

### Attempt 5: Add Debug Logging to Hook

```javascript
appendFileSync('/tmp/beads-hook.log', `${new Date().toISOString()} invoked\n`);
```

**Result:** Log file never created. Hook not invoked at all.

---

### Attempt 6: Test Hook Manually

```bash
echo '{"hook_event_name":"PostToolUse","tool_name":"Bash",...}' | node ~/.claude/hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs
```

**Result:** Hook works correctly when invoked directly. Outputs expected JSON.

---

### Attempt 7: Check settings.json Configuration

```json
"PostToolUse": [
  {
    "hooks": [
      {
        "type": "command",
        "command": "node \"/home/dawid/.claude/hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs\"",
        "timeout": 5000
      }
    ],
    "matcher": "Bash"
  },
  // ... other hooks
]
```

**Result:** Configuration looks correct.

---

## Current State

| Component | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| `beads.ts` extension | Updated | Uses `ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId()` |
| `custom-footer.ts` extension | Updated | Uses `ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId()` |
| `beads-claim-sync.mjs` hook | Updated | Uses `input.session_id` |
| `~/.claude/settings.json` | Regenerated | Clean, hooks wired correctly |
| Hook invocation | NOT FIRING | Log never created, hook never called |
| Extension tool_result | UNCLEAR | May or may not be firing |

---

## Questions to Answer

1. **Why is the PostToolUse hook not firing?**
   - Is there a session cache that needs clearing?
   - Does `/reload` reload hooks or only extensions/skills?
   - Is there a hook loading log we can check?

2. **Is the Pi extension tool_result event firing?**
   - How to verify?
   - Does `/reload` reload extensions?

3. **What is the correct session ID source?**
   - Hook input provides `session_id` field
   - Pi provides `ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId()`
   - Are these the same UUID?

---

## Files to Review

- `cli/extensions/beads.ts` - Pi extension for claim handling
- `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer.ts` - Statusline display
- `hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs` - PostToolUse hook
- `hooks/beads-edit-gate.mjs` - PreToolUse edit blocker
- `config/hooks.json` - Canonical hook configuration
- `~/.claude/settings.json` - Runtime hook wiring

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start a fresh Pi session
2. Run `bd update <issue-id> --claim`
3. Check `bd kv list | grep claimed`
4. Expected: New entry with session UUID
5. Actual: No new entry

---

## Workaround

Manually set the claim:
```bash
# Get your session ID from statusline or hook error message
bd kv set "claimed:<session-id>" "<issue-id>"
```

---

## Related Issues

- xtrm-xoi (session key coordination - may be obsolete after this investigation)
- PR #76 (beads-claim-sync hook - merged but not working)

title: Bug: git reset --hard origin/main blocked on protected branch — breaks post-merge sync step
description: After gh pr merge --squash, the agent must run git checkout main && git reset --hard origin/main to sync local main with remote. The reset --hard step is not in main-guard.mjs SAFE_BASH_PATTERNS, so once xtrm-3k7 is fixed (Bash added to matcher), this required workflow step will be blocked by the default-deny. Fix: add a pattern for git reset --hard origin/<protected-branch> to SAFE_BASH_PATTERNS in main-guard.mjs, scoped dynamically to the protected branches list. Also standardize sync guidance: post-push hook says git pull --ff-only but main-guard workflow message says git reset --hard origin/master — pick one (reset --hard is correct after squash merge). deps: xtrm-3k7
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: main-guard.mjs Bash handler never fires — Bash missing from hooks.json matcher
description: main-guard.mjs contains full Bash handling: blocks git commit on protected branches, blocks direct pushes to protected branches, default-deny for all other Bash on protected branches. But in config/hooks.json the hook is only registered with matcher Write|Edit|MultiEdit|mcp__serena__*. Bash is absent from the matcher, so the hook never receives Bash tool events. The entire Bash-protection code path is dead. Fix: add Bash to the matcher for main-guard.mjs in config/hooks.json (alongside the existing Write|Edit|... matcher, or as a separate entry). Verification: on main branch, git commit should be blocked with the feature-branch workflow message.

title: service-skills-set: git hooks not executed when core.hooksPath is .beads/hooks
description:
xtrm install project service-skills-set installs pre-commit and pre-push into .githooks/ and reports '✓ activated in .git/hooks/'. However, when git core.hooksPath is already set to .beads/hooks (set by beads), git never executes .githooks/. The .beads/hooks/pre-commit and pre-push only run 'bd hooks run pre-commit/pre-push' and do not chain to .githooks/.

Repro: after install, run 'git config core.hooksPath' → returns '.beads/hooks'. Run 'git commit' → .githooks/pre-commit never fires.

Fix options:
  A) Detect core.hooksPath at install time and install into that directory instead
  B) Append a chain call to .githooks scripts into the existing .beads/hooks/pre-commit and pre-push
  C) Register the service-skills git hook scripts with beads via 'bd hooks add'
close_reason: Fixed in 9af7948: service installer now chains .githooks into active core.hooksPath (including .beads/hooks)

title: py/ts quality-gate hooks silently skip Serena edits (relative_path not extracted)
description:
Both quality-check.py and quality-check.cjs extract the edited file path from tool_input.file_path or tool_input.path. Serena tools (replace_symbol_body, insert_after_symbol, insert_before_symbol, rename_symbol) pass relative_path instead. Result: the hooks fire (matcher is correct) but immediately exit 0 with 'No file path found' — no quality check runs at all.

Repro:
  echo '{"tool_name":"mcp__serena__replace_symbol_body","tool_input":{"relative_path":"src/core/store.ts","name_path":"foo","body":"x"}}' | python3 .claude/hooks/quality-check.py
  # exits 0 with 'No file to check'

Fix needed in:
  - quality-check.py :: extract_file_path() — add: tool_input.get('relative_path')
  - quality-check.cjs :: equivalent file path extraction logic

When relative_path is present, resolve it against CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR to get the absolute path before running checks.
close_reason: Fixed in 9af7948: py/ts quality hooks now resolve Serena relative_path

title: CLI: restructure install into all / basic / project subcommands
description:
## Context
The current xtrm install flow is monolithic and interactive. Users need granular control: some want everything, some only skills, some only project hooks. The new structure gives three clear install profiles while preserving all existing functionality.

## New install command structure

### xtrm install all
Global install of everything:
- All skills (skills/)
- All global hooks: general hooks (Python + main-guard.mjs) + beads gate hooks (beads-*.mjs)
- MCP server registration (~/.claude/mcp.json or settings.json)
- Runs beads+dolt dependency check BEFORE installing beads hooks (see separate issue)
- Prompts: 'Install beads+dolt? Required for workflow enforcement hooks [y/N]'
- On yes or already installed: installs beads hooks
- On no: installs everything except beads hooks, prints note

### xtrm install basic
Global install without beads hooks:
- All skills (skills/)
- General global hooks only (Python hooks + main-guard.mjs)
- MCP server registration
- No beads dependency check — beads hooks are intentionally excluded
- Safe to run anywhere with zero external deps

### xtrm install project
Project-scoped install (must be run from a project directory with a .git root):
- Installs project-skills/ (py-quality-gate, ts-quality-gate, tdd-guard, service-skills-set)
- Merges settings.json hooks into project's .claude/settings.json (deep merge, no clobber)
- Fails clearly if not in a git project directory

## Flags (all subcommands)
- --dry-run: show what would be installed, no writes
- --yes / -y: non-interactive, accept all prompts
- --no-mcp: skip MCP registration
- --force: overwrite existing files

## Acceptance criteria
- xtrm install all installs all components, runs beads check
- xtrm install basic installs skills + general hooks + mcp, skips beads
- xtrm install project installs project-skills into cwd
- --dry-run prints install manifest without writing
- --yes suppresses all prompts (for CI/automation)
- Banner displayed in all subcommand views
- No regression: existing install behavior preserved or improved

## Open idea review
Should there be even finer granularity? e.g., xtrm install hooks, xtrm install skills, xtrm install mcp as atomic subcommands composable by power users? The three profiles (all/basic/project) cover 90% of use cases but flags like --no-mcp within each profile may be cleaner than extra subcommands. What is the right granularity boundary?
close_reason: Implemented xtrm install all/basic subcommands on feat/install-subcommands

Should there be a lightweight project-skill wrapper that configures the global main-guard per-project (e.g. custom protected branches list)? Or is the MAIN_GUARD_PROTECTED_BRANCHES env var sufficient? Consider whether project-level override is a future need.

title: Migrate specialists-main-guard.mjs into hooks/ as canonical main-guard.mjs

## Context
The installed ~/.claude/hooks/specialists-main-guard.mjs is the superior version of main-guard: it covers Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit/Bash and blocks git commit + git push on protected branches. The project-skills/main-guard source is an older CJS version with narrower coverage. Since main-guard is a global/user-scope hook, it belongs in xtrm-tools hooks/ — not in project-skills.

## What to do
1. Copy ~/.claude/hooks/specialists-main-guard.mjs → hooks/main-guard.mjs
2. Update header: 'Installed by: xtrm install' (not specialists install)
3. Rename consistently — hooks/main-guard.mjs becomes the canonical file name

## The installed version capabilities (preserve all of these)
- Protects main/master branches
- Blocks: Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit (structured tools)
- Blocks: git commit, git push via Bash
- Falls through cleanly for all other Bash commands
- Falls back gracefully when not in a git repo
- Uses MAIN_GUARD_PROTECTED_BRANCHES env var for test override

## Acceptance criteria
- hooks/main-guard.mjs exists with full content of current specialists-main-guard.mjs
- Header updated, no functional changes

## Note
Issue xyi (main-guard Bash write bypass) and the reviewer's default-deny Bash recommendation are tracked separately — this is a pure migration issue, not a fix pass. Fix comes after migration.

title: drift_detector: optional --enrich-with-gitnexus for semantic + impact-ranked drift
description:
PROBLEM: skills/updating-service-skills/scripts/drift_detector.py currently flags any territory file with mtime > last_sync. Most flagged entries are cosmetic (formatting, imports, comments) and don't warrant SKILL.md updates. On a real drift sweep this produces high noise — operators get a 50-entry list when only 5 are semantically meaningful. Has no notion of impact: every entry is flat, no ranking, no cross-territory signal.

SUCCESS:
- New optional flag '--enrich-with-gitnexus' on scan and check subcommands. Default off; tool functions identically to today when flag is absent or GitNexus is unreachable.
- When enabled and GitNexus index is fresh:
  - Per drifted file, call gitnexus_detect_changes (scoped to territory) to distinguish real symbol changes from cosmetic edits.
  - Per touched symbol, call gitnexus_impact(direction=upstream) to compute blast radius (callers, affected processes).
  - Output groups entries into 'high impact' / 'medium' / 'cosmetic' tiers with cited symbol names and affected process flows.
  - Cross-territory edge note: when a symbol from territory A is imported into territory B's territory file, surface as 'cross-territory drift signal' so the librarian considers updating both SKILLs.
- When GitNexus is unreachable or index is stale (gitnexus_detect_changes warns), tool emits a stderr line ('gitnexus enrichment skipped: <reason>') and falls through to mtime-only output. Never blocks.

SCOPE:
- skills/updating-service-skills/scripts/drift_detector.py — scan/check subcommands, output formatter.
- skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py — small helper for GitNexus availability probe (importable; safe import; no top-level I/O).
- Tests: fresh project with fixture commits exercising cosmetic vs semantic edits; assert tier ranking; assert graceful fallback when GitNexus unavailable.

NON_GOALS:
- No replacement of mtime-based trigger. mtime stays primary; GitNexus is enrichment only.
- No automatic SKILL.md mutation; this remains the librarian's call.
- No GitNexus install or index management; tool only consumes if already available.
- No new env vars beyond optional GITNEXUS_DISABLE escape hatch.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Pure stdlib for the core path; GitNexus calls behind try/except so missing tools never break scan.
- Pre-flight check: gitnexus_detect_changes with timeout 2s; if it doesn't return cleanly, skip enrichment.
- Output remains parseable line-by-line so existing pipes/grep usage by operators keeps working; add structured fields after the existing 'service: file (last sync: ...)' line.

VALIDATION:
- pytest fixtures: cosmetic-only edit (returns mtime drift but enriched as 'cosmetic' tier); signature change (high tier, cites symbol + processes); cross-territory import (both services flagged, edge annotated).
- Live: run scan --enrich-with-gitnexus on darth-feedor immediately after a real session; expected: 50 mtime drift entries reduced to ~5-8 high-impact entries with the rest tagged cosmetic.

REFERENCES:
- Sibling bead xtrm-upm1 (registry-resolver fix). This bead depends on xtrm-upm1 because enrichment is moot if scan can't even find the registry.
- darth-feedor session 2026-05-07 motivated this — first real-world large-scale drift scan that produced too-noisy output to act on directly.
- gitnexus tool availability is broadly assumed across xtrm projects per CLAUDE.md conventions but not strictly required.

title: xt init/install: registry.json model — per-repo copy vs. user-scope + marker
description:
## Problem

`xt init` (and `xt install`) read `packageRoot/.xtrm/registry.json` and call `installFromRegistry` to copy hooks/skills into `userXtrmDir`. They never write `registry.json` itself into the user repo. As a result:

1. `xt update --root <dir>` can't discover newly initialized repos. `repo-discovery.ts:5-7` walks for `.xtrm/registry.json` as the managed-repo marker. Without it, fresh-init repos are invisible to bulk update.
2. Operators have to manually `cp $NPM_PKG/.xtrm/registry.json <repo>/.xtrm/registry.json` after init to make the repo discoverable. Reproduced this session across 12 mercury subprojects.

Memory: `xt-init-does-not-copy-xtrm-registry-json`.

## Proposals

### Option A — copy registry.json on init/install (minimal change)

At the end of `installFromRegistry` in `cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts:127`, write `packageRoot/.xtrm/registry.json` → `userXtrmDir/registry.json`.

- Pros: tiny diff, keeps existing discovery logic, matches what `~/dev/xtrm-tools` and `~/dev/specialists` already look like.
- Cons: per-repo registry becomes a version-stamped artifact that drifts after `npm i -g xtrm-tools@latest` until `xt update --apply` runs. Same model as today for repos that already have one.

### Option B — user-scope registry + per-repo marker

Stop copying the manifest into each repo. Read it directly from the installed npm package (`node_modules/xtrm-tools/.xtrm/registry.json`) for both install and doctor. `xt init` drops a tiny `.xtrm/.managed` (e.g., `{"managedBy":"xtrm-tools"}`) that `repo-discovery.ts` walks for instead.

- Pros: no per-repo manifest drift class. `xt doctor` always reads canonical hashes from package, so refresh-after-`npm install` is automatic. Cleaner mental model.
- Cons: loses ability to pin a repo to an older xtrm-tools via checked-in registry.json (notional — no one is doing this today; all consumer registries observed are verbatim copies of the package). Migration path: leave existing registry.json alongside the new marker; `repo-discovery.ts` accepts either; remove old form in a later release.

## Files

- `cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts:47` — `resolvePackageRoot`
- `cli/src/core/registry-scaffold.ts:127` — `installFromRegistry`
- `cli/src/core/repo-discovery.ts:5` — `REGISTRY_MARKER`
- `cli/src/commands/init.ts:760-780` — init flow
- `cli/src/commands/install.ts:140-180` — install flow
- `cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:174,283` — doctor reads `<cwd>/.xtrm/registry.json`
- `cli/src/commands/update.ts:24,29` — update reads `<repo>/.xtrm/registry.json`

## Recommendation

Option A first (1-line fix, unblocks bulk init), then Option B as a follow-up that removes per-repo registry.json from the model entirely. Option B is the cleaner long-term answer.

## Acceptance

- After `xt init` in a fresh repo, `xt update --root <parent>` discovers it without manual cp.
- `xt doctor` works in newly initialized repos.
- Existing repos with `registry.json` continue to work unchanged.

title: Migration policy + Windows stance for Cat B distribution
description:
PROBLEM: Two latent risks unaddressed by .5-.8:
1. Existing user repos may have hand-managed .claude/skills/ content that ensureAgentsSkillsSymlink() in cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts removes/replaces with a symlink. No documented migration path → operator runs xt init or xt update on an existing repo and silently loses local content.
2. Windows: cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts always calls fs.symlink. Tests skip symlink behavior on win32. Without an explicit copy-fallback or clear "Windows unsupported" stance, behavior is undefined.

GOAL: One short doc page + one preflight check that protects existing content and declares Windows posture.

SUCCESS:
- docs/cat-b-distribution.md (or extend existing relevant doc) covers:
  - Ownership model (releasing → xtrm-tools, 7 specialists-canonical vendored at publish).
  - Migration: what xt init/update do to existing .claude/skills/ — explicit. Recommended pre-migration: backup .claude/skills/. Post-migration: hand-curated content moves to .xtrm/skills/user/.
  - Windows: ONE of (a) explicit "Windows unsupported pending #X" stance with reproduction info; OR (b) implemented copy-fallback path in skills-scaffold.ts with docs.
- cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts: before replacing existing .claude/skills with a symlink, detect if it is a real dir with content NOT matching a previous xtrm-managed view (e.g. files not present in current .xtrm/skills/active/). If so:
  - default: refuse and exit non-zero with message pointing to migration doc.
  - --force flag (already exists or new): proceed, after backing up to .claude/skills.bak-<timestamp>/.
- Tests cover: existing real .claude/skills/ dir with foreign file → migration check fires; .claude/skills/ already a correct symlink → no-op.

SCOPE:
- docs/ (new or extended)
- cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts (preflight check)
- cli/test/skills-scaffold.test.ts (new cases)
- README or top-level docs index entry to point at the migration page

NON_GOALS:
- No actual Windows copy-fallback implementation (just declare stance — implementation can be follow-up bead if (a) chosen).
- No multi-repo migration tooling.
- No automatic content classification (foreign file = anything not in xtrm-managed registry view).

CONSTRAINTS:
- Documentation in markdown, English.
- Preflight check must not be skippable silently — explicit --force or env var only.
- Default behavior on a clean install (no existing .claude/skills/) unchanged.
- Backup dir name predictable (.claude/skills.bak-<ISO-timestamp>/) so users can find it.
- No data loss without explicit user opt-in.

VALIDATION:
- npm test passes.
- Manual: tmp project with hand-written .claude/skills/foo/SKILL.md, run xt init → fails with migration message.
- Manual: same tmp project, run xt init --force → existing content backed up, symlink installed, backup contains foo/SKILL.md.
- Manual: tmp project with .claude/skills already as correct symlink → xt init no-op, no backup created.
- Doc page renders; links from README work.

OUTPUT: Diff summary, doc link, sample preflight error message, validation results.

DEPENDS ON: xtrm-sjbc.5 (flat layout assumed in docs). Soft-depends on .6/.7/.8 (full picture for documentation).

REFERENCES:
- explorer findings: xtrm-kdhp risk list (ensureSkillsSymlink removes real dirs)
- overthinker findings: xtrm-9tl5 hidden risks #2 (Windows) and #5 (migration blast radius)

  - default: refuse and exit non-zero with message pointing to migration doc.
  - --force flag (already exists or new): proceed, after backing up to .claude/skills.bak-<timestamp>/.
- Tests cover: existing real .claude/skills/ dir with foreign file → migration check fires; .claude/skills/ already a correct symlink → no-op.

SCOPE:
- docs/ (new or extended)
- cli/src/core/skills-scaffold.ts (preflight check)
- cli/test/skills-scaffold.test.ts (new cases)
- README or top-level docs index entry to point at the migration page

NON_GOALS:
- No actual Windows copy-fallback implementation (just declare stance — implementation can be follow-up bead if (a) chosen).
- No multi-repo migration tooling.
- No automatic content classification (foreign file = anything not in xtrm-managed registry view).

CONSTRAINTS:
- Documentation in markdown, English.
- Preflight check must not be skippable silently — explicit --force or env var only.
- Default behavior on a clean install (no existing .claude/skills/) unchanged.
- Backup dir name predictable (.claude/skills.bak-<ISO-timestamp>/) so users can find it.
- No data loss without explicit user opt-in.

VALIDATION:
- npm test passes.
- Manual: tmp project with hand-written .claude/skills/foo/SKILL.md, run xt init → fails with migration message.
- Manual: same tmp project, run xt init --force → existing content backed up, symlink installed, backup contains foo/SKILL.md.
- Manual: tmp project with .claude/skills already as correct symlink → xt init no-op, no backup created.
- Doc page renders; links from README work.

OUTPUT: Diff summary, doc link, sample preflight error message, validation results.

DEPENDS ON: xtrm-sjbc.5 (flat layout assumed in docs). Soft-depends on .6/.7/.8 (full picture for documentation).

REFERENCES:
- explorer findings: xtrm-kdhp risk list (ensureSkillsSymlink removes real dirs)
- overthinker findings: xtrm-9tl5 hidden risks #2 (Windows) and #5 (migration blast radius)

title: specialist:researcher
notes:
### Specialist Output — researcher (job 5df5d5) [DONE]

## Summary

**caveman-compress** is a Claude Code skill that compresses natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into "caveman-speak" to reduce input tokens by ~46% on average.

### What It Does

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Purpose** | Reduce input tokens every session by compressing context files |
| **Mechanism** | Rewrites prose to caveman format while preserving all technical content |
| **Savings** | ~46% average token reduction (benchmarks: 36-60% range) |
| **Output** | `FILE.md` (compressed) + `FILE.original.md` (human-readable backup) |

### CLI Usage

```bash
# Via Claude Code skill (recommended)
/caveman:compress CLAUDE.md
/caveman:compress docs/preferences.md
/caveman:compress todos.md

# Direct Python CLI (from repo)
cd caveman-compress && python3 -m scripts <absolute_filepath>
```

### What Gets Compressed vs Preserved

| Compressed | Preserved Exactly |
|------------|-------------------|
| Articles (a, an, the) | Code blocks (```...```) |
| Filler (just, really, basically) | Inline code (`backticks`) |
| Pleasantries ("sure", "happy to") | URLs and links |
| Hedging ("might be worth") | File paths |
| Redundant phrasing | Commands (npm, git, docker) |
| Connective fluff (however, furthermore) | Technical terms, API names |
| | Dates, versions, numbers |
| | Headings, tables, structure |

### When to Use

- **CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md** — loads every session, high ROI
- **Project preferences** — coding standards, style guides
- **Todo lists** — natural language task tracking
- **Session reports** — historical context files
- **Any `.md`/`.txt` natural language file** — not code/config

### Application to xtrm-tools Workflows

| xtrm File | Benefit | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| `.xtrm/memory.md` | High | Injected every session — 46% savings compounds |
| `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` | High | Session-start context, ~1000+ tokens typical |
| `.xtrm/reports/*.md` | Medium | Historical context for new sessions |
| `docs/*.md` | Low | Only if referenced in session context |
| `*.py`, `*.ts`, `*.json` | ❌ Skip | Code/config files auto-skipped |

### Implementation Notes

- **One-time cost**: Compression uses tokens once; savings apply every session thereafter
- **Validation**: Headings, code blocks, URLs, paths validated post-compression
- **Auto-fix**: Targeted Claude fixes on validation failures (max 2 retries)
- **Reversible**: `.original.md` backup allows editing and re-compression
- **Part of caveman**: Install via `claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman`

### Recommendation for xtrm-tools

Compress `.xtrm/memory.md` and `AGENTS.md` after any significant update. The ~46% token savings compounds across all future sessions, and the backup file preserves human readability for maintenance.

title: Agent memory: context graphs, reasoning traces, entity extraction pipeline
description:
## Source

Distilled from: qMV64p-4Deo — 'Building Context Graphs for AI Agents' by Will Lyon, Neo4j (April 2026)
Analysis file: dev/omni-search-engine/docs/qMV64p-4Deo.analysis.md

## Core Concept: The Missing Why

A context graph is a knowledge graph that contains all information necessary to make decisions throughout a system — crucially including *why* decisions were made, not just what decisions were made.

Current xtrm agent sessions lose reasoning context between turns and sessions. Specialists produce outputs but don't record their reasoning traces in a queryable form.

## Three Memory Types (apply to xtrm specialist pipeline)

### 1. Short-term memory
Conversation/session state — the current turn's working context.
Already partially solved by bd prime + bead notes.

### 2. Long-term memory
Extracted entities and facts persisted across sessions.
Currently fragmented: bd memories, MEMORY.md files, bead notes — no unified graph.

### 3. Reasoning memory (MOST IMPORTANT — least supported)
Tool call traces, decision steps, token usage, specialist outputs — recorded as auditable history.
Currently: specialist results exist in job feeds and bead notes, but are not queryable or connected.

## Key Insight: Reasoning Memory as Precedent

When an agent makes a decision (e.g. executor chose approach X over Y because of Z), recording that decision in the memory graph makes it available as precedent for future similar decisions. The memory graph becomes self-improving.

Applied to xtrm: when a specialist closes a bead with --reason, that reason + the full decision context (what was tried, what failed, what worked) should be indexed as a retrievable precedent, not just a log entry.

## Entity Extraction Pipeline (cost-efficient)

Neo4j production approach — never LLM-first:
  1. spaCy / statistical NER (fast, free, CPU)
  2. GLINER 2 — local model, runs on CPU, fine-tuned for entity+relationship extraction
  3. LLM fallback ONLY when statistical methods fail

This is the right approach for extracting entities from specialist outputs, bead notes, and session logs without burning tokens on every entry.

## Proposed xtrm Integration Points

### A. bd remember enhancement
Current: bd remember 'insight' — flat key-value store
Proposed: tag entities from the insight automatically (people, tools, concepts, decisions)
so that bd memories 'keyword' can traverse entity relationships, not just keyword-match strings.

### B. Specialist reasoning traces
After each specialist run, extract the decision trace (tools called, findings, decisions made)
and store as a reasoning memory node linked to the bead.
Queryable: 'what did we try when fixing X?' returns tool call trace from that session.

### C. Precedent lookup before dispatch
Before dispatching an executor, check reasoning memory for similar past beads:
  bd precedent 'auth token refresh bug'  →  returns past decisions on similar tasks

### D. Session handoff context
When a new session starts, bd prime could surface not just active beads but relevant
reasoning memories from past sessions that match current bead topics.

## Implementation Notes

- Storage: extend the existing bd/dolt graph — reasoning memory as a new node type
- Extraction: run entity extraction on bead close (PostToolUse hook on bd close)
- No new infrastructure: leverage existing SQLite/dolt in .beads/
- GLINER 2 local model runs on CPU in seconds per document
- Start simple: capture bead close --reason text + specialist result summaries as first pass
notes: Release triage 2026-05-04: valuable roadmap work, but not required for the next patch release. Defer until after release-critical bugfix/docs/packaging items land.

title: Fix YouTube transcript fetch for non-English videos
description:
youtube_yt.py fails to fetch transcripts for non-English YouTube videos. Two root causes:

1. _fetch_transcript_ytdlp() hardcodes --sub-lang en (line 375), so yt-dlp skips videos with only non-English auto-captions (e.g. Italian-only live streams).

2. _fetch_transcript_direct() fallback to first track works in theory but YouTube's timedtext API returns 0 bytes for bare urllib requests (rate-limiting/bot detection).

Fix: Change --sub-lang en to a broader set or use --sub-lang all in _fetch_transcript_ytdlp(). Also update _fetch_transcript_direct() to try the original language track when en is unavailable.

Test video: https://youtube.com/live/ZMawN97EetI (Italian auto-generated captions only, 19k words confirmed working when fetched with --sub-lang it)

Files: .xtrm/skills/default/last30days/scripts/lib/youtube_yt.py
Symbols: _fetch_transcript_ytdlp (line 359), _fetch_transcript_direct (line 263), fetch_transcript (line 420)
close_reason: Fixed: youtube_yt.py now supports non-English transcripts via --sub-lang all and language-aware track candidates

title: P0: Create symlinks for npm package extensions (pi-gitnexus, pi-serena-tools) during xt init/reload
description:
## Problem

Specialists spawns pi with `--no-extensions` then selectively re-enables extensions via `-e <path>`. The code already has logic to load `gitnexus` and `serena` from `~/.pi/agent/extensions/`, but these are installed as npm packages, not directory extensions.

## Root Cause

- `pi-gitnexus` is installed at `~/.nvm/versions/node/v25.2.1/lib/node_modules/pi-gitnexus`
- `pi-serena-tools` is installed at `~/.nvm/versions/node/v25.2.1/lib/node_modules/pi-serena-tools`
- Specialists looks for them at `~/.pi/agent/extensions/gitnexus` and `~/.pi/agent/extensions/serena`
- Without symlinks, specialists subprocesses don't get gitnexus/serena tools

## Solution

Add symlink creation to `xt init` or `xt reload`:

```bash
# GitNexus
ln -sf ~/.nvm/versions/node/v25.2.1/lib/node_modules/pi-gitnexus \
       ~/.pi/agent/extensions/gitnexus

# Serena
ln -sf ~/.nvm/versions/node/v25.2.1/lib/node_modules/pi-serena-tools \
       ~/.pi/agent/extensions/serena
```

## Impact

Without this fix, specialists (debugger, executor, etc.) don't have access to:
- `gitnexus_query`, `gitnexus_context`, `gitnexus_impact`, `gitnexus_detect_changes`
- Serena tools for code navigation and LSP features

## Verification

After fix, specialists should show gitnexus tools in their available tool list and be able to call `/gitnexus status` successfully.
close_reason: Implemented ensureNpmPackageExtensionSymlinks() in pi-runtime.ts. Creates symlinks for pi-gitnexus and pi-serena-tools to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/gitnexus and ~/.pi/agent/extensions/serena during xt pi reload. Verified working: symlinks created and pointing to correct npm global locations.

title: Make Pi npm packages global at ~/.pi/npm/
description:
## Problem
Pi npm packages (pi-gitnexus, pi-serena-tools, etc.) are installed per-project in .pi/npm/node_modules/
This causes:
- Duplicate installs across projects
- Worktree reinstalls (even with symlink fix)
- Wasted disk space

## Solution
Install all Pi npm packages globally at ~/.pi/npm/node_modules/

## Changes Needed

### 1. cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts
Change executePiSync to default isGlobal: true for packages

### 2. cli/src/utils/worktree-session.ts
Remove the .pi/npm symlink logic (no longer needed - packages are global)

### 3. xt pi reload
Ensure it installs to global location

## Command to Migrate Existing
```bash
# Install all packages globally once
pi install npm:pi-gitnexus
pi install npm:pi-serena-tools
pi install npm:lsp-pi
pi install npm:@zenobius/pi-worktrees
pi install npm:@robhowley/pi-structured-return

# Remove per-project .pi/npm/ directories
find . -type d -name '.pi' -exec rm -rf {}/npm \; 2>/dev/null
```
close_reason: Pi npm packages are per-project by design. Worktrees now share main repo's .pi/npm via symlink (fixed in 3718f5b5). True cross-project global packages would require Pi core changes.
